flat_vs_steep_associational_hierarchies_.name: Flat vs. steep associational hierarchies in creativity
flat_vs_steep_associational_hierarchies_.description: Following Mednick (1962), creative individuals are hypothesized to have 'flat' associational hierarchies (broader, more flexible associations) whereas less creative individuals have 'steep' hierarchies (few, common associations).
flat_vs_steep_associational_hierarchies_.paper: Investigating the structure of semantic networks in low and high creative persons

association_cloud_method_for_semantic_ne.name: Association-cloud method for semantic network construction
association_cloud_method_for_semantic_ne.description: A novel computational approach constructs semantic networks by linking concepts through their association correlations—the overlap of similar associative responses ('association clouds')—derived from free association data.
association_cloud_method_for_semantic_ne.paper: Investigating the structure of semantic networks in low and high creative persons

low_creative_semantic_networks_are_more_.name: Low-creative semantic networks are more rigid and fragmented
low_creative_semantic_networks_are_more_.description: The semantic memory network of persons with low creative ability is more rigid than that of high-creative persons, manifesting as a more spread-out network that breaks apart into more sub-communities or components.
low_creative_semantic_networks_are_more_.paper: Investigating the structure of semantic networks in low and high creative persons

high_creative_semantic_networks_are_more.name: High-creative semantic networks are more cohesive and flexible
high_creative_semantic_networks_are_more.description: The semantic network of high-creative individuals is characterized by greater cohesion and flexibility relative to low-creative individuals, consistent with the theoretical expectation of richer associative structure.
high_creative_semantic_networks_are_more.paper: Investigating the structure of semantic networks in low and high creative persons

decision_tree_classification_of_creativi.name: Decision-tree classification of creativity groups
decision_tree_classification_of_creativi.description: A decision tree approach applied to multiple creativity measurements is used to divide a large participant sample into low- and high-creative groups for subsequent network comparison.
decision_tree_classification_of_creativi.paper: Investigating the structure of semantic networks in low and high creative persons

feasibility_of_network_science_for_study.name: Feasibility of network science for studying high-level cognition
feasibility_of_network_science_for_study.description: The authors argue that network science paradigms are a feasible and informative approach for investigating high-level cognitive constructs such as creativity and semantic memory.
feasibility_of_network_science_for_study.paper: Investigating the structure of semantic networks in low and high creative persons

free_association_paradigm_as_a_window_in.name: Free association paradigm as a window into semantic memory structure
free_association_paradigm_as_a_window_in.description: The study employs a free association generation paradigm at scale to empirically construct and compare semantic memory networks across creativity groups.
free_association_paradigm_as_a_window_in.paper: Investigating the structure of semantic networks in low and high creative persons

correspondence_of_network_findings_with_.name: Correspondence of network findings with Mednick's theory
correspondence_of_network_findings_with_.description: The authors claim that the observed network differences between low- and high-creative persons are in accord with and extend Mednick's (1962) theory of associative hierarchies in creativity.
correspondence_of_network_findings_with_.paper: Investigating the structure of semantic networks in low and high creative persons

network_science_as_a_quantitative_framew.name: Network science as a quantitative framework for cognitive systems
network_science_as_a_quantitative_framew.description: The review argues that network science provides a powerful quantitative approach to represent cognitive systems, enabling study of structure, process, and dynamics in cognition.
network_science_as_a_quantitative_framew.paper: Cognitive Network Science: A Review of Research on Cognition through the Lens of Network Representations, Processes, and Dynamics

structure_process_interaction_in_cogniti.name: Structure-process interaction in cognitive network models
structure_process_interaction_in_cogniti.description: The review claims that network science uniquely captures how underlying network structure and processes operating on that structure interact to produce behavioral phenomena in cognition.
structure_process_interaction_in_cogniti.paper: Cognitive Network Science: A Review of Research on Cognition through the Lens of Network Representations, Processes, and Dynamics

network_science_framework_for_cognitive_.name: Network science framework for cognitive dynamics
network_science_framework_for_cognitive_.description: The review argues that network science provides a quantitative framework to model the dynamics of cognitive systems, operationalized as structural changes on different timescales and resolutions.
network_science_framework_for_cognitive_.paper: Cognitive Network Science: A Review of Research on Cognition through the Lens of Network Representations, Processes, and Dynamics

scope_limitations_of_cognitive_network_s.name: Scope limitations of cognitive network science to date
scope_limitations_of_cognitive_network_s.description: The review notes that application of network science methodologies to quantitatively study cognition has so far been limited in scope, motivating the need for the review and future milestones.
scope_limitations_of_cognitive_network_s.paper: Cognitive Network Science: A Review of Research on Cognition through the Lens of Network Representations, Processes, and Dynamics

focus_on_semantic_and_lexical_networks_a.name: Focus on semantic and lexical networks as core case studies
focus_on_semantic_and_lexical_networks_a.description: The review focuses specifically on semantic and lexical networks as the primary empirical domain for illustrating cognitive network science arguments.
focus_on_semantic_and_lexical_networks_a.paper: Cognitive Network Science: A Review of Research on Cognition through the Lens of Network Representations, Processes, and Dynamics

key_milestones_for_maturing_cognitive_ne.name: Key milestones for maturing cognitive network science
key_milestones_for_maturing_cognitive_ne.description: The review highlights key milestones the field needs to achieve as it matures to provide continued insights into cognitive structures and processes.
key_milestones_for_maturing_cognitive_ne.paper: Cognitive Network Science: A Review of Research on Cognition through the Lens of Network Representations, Processes, and Dynamics

network_representations_of_cognition_bey.name: Network representations of cognition beyond metaphor
network_representations_of_cognition_bey.description: A key contribution of the review is demonstrating that cognitive network science moves beyond treating networks as mere metaphors, applying formal quantitative network science methods to cognitive data.
network_representations_of_cognition_bey.paper: Cognitive Network Science: A Review of Research on Cognition through the Lens of Network Representations, Processes, and Dynamics

semantic_memory_structure_as_a_driver_of.name: Semantic memory structure as a driver of creativity
semantic_memory_structure_as_a_driver_of.description: The paper asserts that the structure of semantic memory plays a meaningful role in creativity, moving beyond a theoretical assumption toward quantitative investigation using network science tools.
semantic_memory_structure_as_a_driver_of.paper: A Semantic Network Cartography of the Creative Mind.

application_of_computational_network_sci.name: Application of computational network science to semantic memory and creativity
application_of_computational_network_sci.description: The paper reviews how computational network science tools have been applied specifically to investigate the role of semantic memory structure in creativity, providing quantitative measures including connectivity, distance, and structure.
application_of_computational_network_sci.paper: A Semantic Network Cartography of the Creative Mind.

quantitative_insufficiency_of_prior_theo.name: Quantitative insufficiency of prior theoretical accounts of creativity and semantic memory
quantitative_insufficiency_of_prior_theo.description: The paper states that while the role of semantic memory in creativity is theoretically assumed, it is far from understood, implying a gap between theoretical claims and empirical, quantitative evidence.
quantitative_insufficiency_of_prior_theo.paper: A Semantic Network Cartography of the Creative Mind.

network_measures_of_connectivity_distanc.name: Network measures of connectivity, distance, and structure as creativity indices
network_measures_of_connectivity_distanc.description: The paper employs network science measures—connectivity, distance, and structural properties—as quantitative indices for characterizing semantic memory in relation to creativity.
network_measures_of_connectivity_distanc.paper: A Semantic Network Cartography of the Creative Mind.

associative_thinking_as_a_core_mechanism.name: Associative thinking as a core mechanism of creativity
associative_thinking_as_a_core_mechanism.description: The review argues that creativity fundamentally involves associative processes in memory—connecting concepts to form ideas, inventions, and artworks—and synthesizes cognitive, computational, and neuroscience evidence to support this claim.
associative_thinking_as_a_core_mechanism.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

semantic_space_navigation_strategies_lin.name: Semantic space navigation strategies linked to creativity
semantic_space_navigation_strategies_lin.description: Recent advances in computational models of semantic memory reveal how people navigate a semantic space when forming associations, and specific search strategies within that space are associated with creativity.
semantic_space_navigation_strategies_lin.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

distinction_between_free_and_goal_direct.name: Distinction between free and goal-directed association
distinction_between_free_and_goal_direct.description: The review draws a theoretical and empirical distinction between free association (unconstrained memory retrieval) and goal-directed association (association guided by a target or purpose), arguing these engage different cognitive processes relevant to creativity.
distinction_between_free_and_goal_direct.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

role_of_associative_thinking_in_the_arts.name: Role of associative thinking in the arts
role_of_associative_thinking_in_the_arts.description: The review illustrates that associative thinking plays a specific, identifiable role in artistic creativity, extending the associative account beyond problem-solving and invention.
role_of_associative_thinking_in_the_arts.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

link_between_associative_thinking_and_ep.name: Link between associative thinking and episodic memory systems
link_between_associative_thinking_and_ep.description: The review proposes that associative thinking in creativity is supported not only by semantic memory but also by episodic memory brain systems, offering a new perspective on classic creativity theory.
link_between_associative_thinking_and_ep.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

limitations_of_prior_models_in_studying_.name: Limitations of prior models in studying associative thinking
limitations_of_prior_models_in_studying_.description: The review identifies that associative thinking has been historically difficult to study due to limitations in modeling memory structure and retrieval processes, motivating the use of new computational approaches.
limitations_of_prior_models_in_studying_.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

synthesis_of_cognitive_computational_and.name: Synthesis of cognitive, computational, and neuroscience research on creativity
synthesis_of_cognitive_computational_and.description: The review integrates findings across cognitive psychology, computational modeling, and neuroscience to provide a unified account of creativity and associative thinking.
synthesis_of_cognitive_computational_and.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

new_perspective_on_a_longstanding_creati.name: New perspective on a longstanding creativity theory
new_perspective_on_a_longstanding_creati.description: By linking associative thinking to both semantic and episodic memory brain systems, the review offers a novel perspective on an established creativity theory (likely Mednick's Remote Associates theory or similar).
new_perspective_on_a_longstanding_creati.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

openness_to_experience_predicts_default_.name: Openness to Experience predicts Default Network global efficiency
openness_to_experience_predicts_default_.description: Across two studies using resting-state fMRI and graph theoretical analysis, Openness to Experience predicted the global efficiency of functional networks comprised of default network nodes and edges, explaining 18% of variance in Study 2 even after controlling for intelligence, age, gender, and other personality variables.
openness_to_experience_predicts_default_.paper: Personality and complex brain networks: The role of openness to experience in default network efficiency

biological_basis_of_openness_to_experien.name: Biological basis of Openness to Experience via DN architecture
biological_basis_of_openness_to_experien.description: The study interprets the Openness–DN efficiency link as evidence for a biological substrate of Openness, suggesting that normally distributed personality traits shape the intrinsic architecture of large-scale brain systems.
biological_basis_of_openness_to_experien.paper: Personality and complex brain networks: The role of openness to experience in default network efficiency

default_network_activity_underlies_spont.name: Default Network activity underlies spontaneous and self-generated cognition
default_network_activity_underlies_spont.description: The paper asserts that DN activity is associated with mind-wandering, episodic memory retrieval, future thinking, mental simulation, theory of mind, and creative cognition, grounding the personality–DN link in established functional roles.
default_network_activity_underlies_spont.paper: Personality and complex brain networks: The role of openness to experience in default network efficiency

normal_personality_variation_influences_.name: Normal personality variation influences intrinsic brain network architecture
normal_personality_variation_influences_.description: The authors argue that normally distributed personality traits—not just disease or development—affect the resting-state functional architecture of large-scale brain networks, broadening explanatory accounts of individual differences in brain organization.
normal_personality_variation_influences_.paper: Personality and complex brain networks: The role of openness to experience in default network efficiency

graph_theoretical_analysis_of_resting_st.name: Graph theoretical analysis of resting-state fMRI for personality research
graph_theoretical_analysis_of_resting_st.description: The study applies structural equation modeling combined with graph theoretical network analysis to resting-state fMRI data to quantify DN global efficiency and relate it to personality trait scores.
graph_theoretical_analysis_of_resting_st.paper: Personality and complex brain networks: The role of openness to experience in default network efficiency

underexplored_role_of_personality_in_nor.name: Underexplored role of personality in normal DN variation
underexplored_role_of_personality_in_nor.description: Despite large literatures on developmental and disease-related influences on the DN, the authors note that surprisingly little is known about factors driving normal variation in DN functioning, motivating the focus on personality.
underexplored_role_of_personality_in_nor.paper: Personality and complex brain networks: The role of openness to experience in default network efficiency

openness_to_experience_as_a_cognitive_pe.name: Openness to Experience as a cognitive personality trait linked to imaginative and creative processes
openness_to_experience_as_a_cognitive_pe.description: Openness is characterized as a normally distributed trait reflecting tendencies toward imaginative, creative, and abstract cognition, and the paper links this dispositional profile to DN efficiency as a plausible neural mechanism.
openness_to_experience_as_a_cognitive_pe.paper: Personality and complex brain networks: The role of openness to experience in default network efficiency

forward_flow_as_a_new_metric_for_quantif.name: Forward flow as a new metric for quantifying free thought
forward_flow_as_a_new_metric_for_quantif.description: The paper introduces 'forward flow,' a metric using latent semantic analysis to capture how much present thoughts diverge from past thoughts during free association, resolving the quantification problem for streams of thought.
forward_flow_as_a_new_metric_for_quantif.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

forward_flow_predicts_creativity_in_coll.name: Forward flow predicts creativity in college students
forward_flow_predicts_creativity_in_coll.description: Study 1 demonstrates that forward flow scores predict creative performance in a college student sample, even after controlling for intelligence.
forward_flow_predicts_creativity_in_coll.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

forward_flow_predicts_creativity_in_a_re.name: Forward flow predicts creativity in a representative American sample
forward_flow_predicts_creativity_in_a_re.description: Study 2 shows that forward flow predicts creativity in a nationally representative sample of Americans, extending the finding beyond convenience samples.
forward_flow_predicts_creativity_in_a_re.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

forward_flow_predicts_membership_in_real.name: Forward flow predicts membership in real-world creative groups
forward_flow_predicts_membership_in_real.description: Studies 3–5 show that forward flow distinguishes performance majors, professional actors, and entrepreneurs from non-creative-group members, even controlling for divergent thinking task performance.
forward_flow_predicts_membership_in_real.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

forward_flow_in_social_media_predicts_cr.name: Forward flow in social media predicts creative achievement in celebrities
forward_flow_in_social_media_predicts_cr.description: Study 6 demonstrates that forward flow measured from celebrities' Twitter posts predicts their real-world creative achievement, extending the metric to naturalistic digital text.
forward_flow_in_social_media_predicts_cr.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

forward_flow_as_a_broad_predictor_beyond.name: Forward flow as a broad predictor beyond creativity
forward_flow_as_a_broad_predictor_beyond.description: The authors claim that forward flow may also predict mental illness, emotional experience, leadership ability, adaptability, neural dynamics, group productivity, and cultural success.
forward_flow_as_a_broad_predictor_beyond.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

open_access_tools_for_assessing_and_visu.name: Open-access tools for assessing and visualizing forward flow
open_access_tools_for_assessing_and_visu.description: The paper presents publicly available online tools for computing and visualizing forward flow, intended for both illustrative and large-scale data analytic use.
open_access_tools_for_assessing_and_visu.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

latent_semantic_analysis_as_the_computat.name: Latent semantic analysis as the computational basis for forward flow
latent_semantic_analysis_as_the_computat.description: Forward flow is operationalized using latent semantic analysis (LSA) to quantify semantic distance between successive thoughts in a free association stream.
latent_semantic_analysis_as_the_computat.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

quantification_problem_for_streams_of_fr.name: Quantification problem for streams of free thought
quantification_problem_for_streams_of_fr.description: Prior to this work, there was no established quantitative method for capturing the semantic evolution of free thought streams from free association data.
quantification_problem_for_streams_of_fr.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

predictive_validity_of_free_association_.name: Predictive validity of free association for psychological phenomena
predictive_validity_of_free_association_.description: It was an open question whether streams of thought captured by free association actually predict meaningful psychological outcomes such as creativity.
predictive_validity_of_free_association_.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

process_definition_of_creativity.name: process_definition_of_creativity
process_definition_of_creativity.description: The paper proposes that creativity should be defined as internal attention constrained by a generative goal, offering a process-based definition rather than the product-focused 'novel and useful' standard definition.

creativity_as_process_vs_creativeness_di.name: creativity_as_process_vs_creativeness_distinction
creativity_as_process_vs_creativeness_di.description: The paper distinguishes between creativity as a cognitive process and 'creative-ness' as an attribute of persons or products, arguing that conflating these contributes to operationalization challenges.

product_focused_standard_definition_is_i.name: product_focused_standard_definition_is_insufficient
product_focused_standard_definition_is_i.description: The conventional 'novel and useful' definition describes creative products rather than the processes that constitute creativity, contributing to persistent difficulties in operationalizing the construct.

internal_attention_as_first_criterion.name: internal_attention_as_first_criterion
internal_attention_as_first_criterion.description: The first criterion of the process definition holds that in creativity, attention must be directed internally toward mental representations rather than externally toward the environment.

goal_constrained_attentional_operations_.name: goal_constrained_attentional_operations_as_second_criterion
goal_constrained_attentional_operations_.description: The second criterion requires that attentional operations (such as search or manipulation of representations) be constrained by parameters of a to-be-achieved goal state, distinguishing creativity from unconstrained free thought.

generative_goal_state_as_third_criterion.name: generative_goal_state_as_third_criterion
generative_goal_state_as_third_criterion.description: The third criterion specifies that the goal state must be generative—not already precisely held in memory—thereby distinguishing creativity from pure retrieval processes.

process_definition_aligns_with_creativit.name: process_definition_aligns_with_creativity_neuroscience
process_definition_aligns_with_creativit.description: The paper illustrates that the three process-definitional criteria (internal attention, goal constraint, generative goal) align with insights from creativity neuroscience, including default network involvement.

process_definition_distinguishes_creativ.name: process_definition_distinguishes_creativity_from_mind_wandering
process_definition_distinguishes_creativ.description: Applying the process definition clarifies the ontological boundary between creativity and mind-wandering: mind-wandering lacks the goal-constrained attentional criterion required by the process definition.

process_definition_distinguishes_creativ.name: process_definition_distinguishes_creativity_from_retrieval
process_definition_distinguishes_creativ.description: The generative goal criterion demarcates creativity from retrieval: retrieval involves accessing a goal state already precisely held in memory, whereas creativity requires generating a novel goal state.

call_for_process_focused_creativity_asse.name: call_for_process_focused_creativity_assessments
call_for_process_focused_creativity_asse.description: The paper recommends that researchers specify whether they are studying creativity-as-process or creativeness, and that process-focused assessments be included in creativity research.

minimal_necessity_and_sufficiency_criter.name: minimal_necessity_and_sufficiency_criteria_for_creativity
minimal_necessity_and_sufficiency_criter.description: The process definition is designed to provide minimal necessary and sufficient conditions for classifying a process as creativity, without excluding other contributing elements (perceptual, emotional, etc.).

semantic_distance_as_a_driver_of_creativ.name: Semantic distance as a driver of creative idea generation
semantic_distance_as_a_driver_of_creativ.description: The paper claims that the farther one moves away from a conventional idea semantically, the more creative the resulting idea will likely be, positioning semantic distance as a core mechanism in the creative process.
semantic_distance_as_a_driver_of_creativ.paper: What can quantitative measures of semantic distance tell us about creativity?

indirect_examination_of_semantic_distanc.name: Indirect examination of semantic distance in creativity due to measurement challenges
indirect_examination_of_semantic_distanc.description: Despite its intuitive importance, semantic distance's role in creativity has historically been examined only indirectly because quantifying it posed significant methodological challenges.
indirect_examination_of_semantic_distanc.paper: What can quantitative measures of semantic distance tell us about creativity?

quantitative_semantic_distance_measures_.name: Quantitative semantic distance measures as complements to subjective creativity assessments
quantitative_semantic_distance_measures_.description: Recent studies applying quantitative semantic distance measures are described as complementing standard subjective creativity measures by providing objective indices of creative output.
quantitative_semantic_distance_measures_.paper: What can quantitative measures of semantic distance tell us about creativity?

quantitative_semantic_distance_as_a_dire.name: Quantitative semantic distance as a direct probe of semantic memory's role in creativity
quantitative_semantic_distance_as_a_dire.description: The paper argues that quantitative semantic distance measures allow more direct examination of the role of semantic memory and distance in creativity, going beyond what subjective measures permit.
quantitative_semantic_distance_as_a_dire.paper: What can quantitative measures of semantic distance tell us about creativity?

overview_of_quantitative_approaches_to_m.name: Overview of quantitative approaches to measuring semantic distance in creativity research
overview_of_quantitative_approaches_to_m.description: The paper provides a structured overview of the main quantitative approaches being used to measure semantic distance in creativity research and discusses their advantages.
overview_of_quantitative_approaches_to_m.paper: What can quantitative measures of semantic distance tell us about creativity?

objective_measurement_of_creative_output.name: Objective measurement of creative output via semantic distance
objective_measurement_of_creative_output.description: A stated aim of applying quantitative semantic distance measures is to provide objective, rather than solely subjective, assessments of creative output.
objective_measurement_of_creative_output.paper: What can quantitative measures of semantic distance tell us about creativity?

three_psychological_dimensions_of_aesthe.name: Three psychological dimensions of aesthetic response to architectural interiors
three_psychological_dimensions_of_aesthe.description: Using PNA and PCA on ratings from 798 participants across 200 images and 16 measures, three components explained 90% of variance: coherence, fascination, and hominess. Coherence and fascination are established dimensions; hominess emerged as novel and specific to architectural interiors.
three_psychological_dimensions_of_aesthe.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

hominess_as_a_novel_aesthetic_dimension_.name: Hominess as a novel aesthetic dimension unique to architectural interiors
hominess_as_a_novel_aesthetic_dimension_.description: Unlike coherence and fascination, which are well-established in responses to natural scenes and art, hominess—reflecting the extent to which a space feels personal—emerged specifically from the architectural interior context. It was identified in Experiment 1 and replicated in Experiment 2.
hominess_as_a_novel_aesthetic_dimension_.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

replication_of_three_component_solution_.name: Replication of three-component solution in independent sample
replication_of_three_component_solution_.description: In Experiment 2 (n=614), the PCA structure from Experiment 1 was replicated in an independent sample, supporting the robustness of the coherence, fascination, and hominess dimensions.
replication_of_three_component_solution_.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

fascination_covaries_with_right_lingual_.name: Fascination covaries with right lingual gyrus activity regardless of task
fascination_covaries_with_right_lingual_.description: In the fMRI reanalysis (Experiment 3, n=18), parametric analysis showed that image fascination scores covaried with neural activity in the right lingual gyrus during both beauty judgments and approach-avoidance decisions, indicating task-independent processing.
fascination_covaries_with_right_lingual_.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

coherence_covaries_with_left_inferior_oc.name: Coherence covaries with left inferior occipital gyrus activity during beauty judgments only
coherence_covaries_with_left_inferior_oc.description: Parametric fMRI analysis showed that coherence was associated with left inferior occipital gyrus activity exclusively when participants judged beauty, not during approach-avoidance decisions, indicating a task-contingent neural signature.
coherence_covaries_with_left_inferior_oc.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

hominess_covaries_with_left_cuneus_activ.name: Hominess covaries with left cuneus activity during approach-avoidance decisions only
hominess_covaries_with_left_cuneus_activ.description: Parametric fMRI analysis showed that hominess scores covaried with left cuneus neural activity specifically during approach-avoidance decisions but not beauty judgments, indicating a task-contingent neural signature distinct from coherence and fascination.
hominess_covaries_with_left_cuneus_activ.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

neural_sensitivity_to_psychological_dime.name: Neural sensitivity to psychological dimensions is not reducible to global image properties
neural_sensitivity_to_psychological_dime.description: The paper argues that covariation of neural activity with coherence, fascination, and hominess cannot be explained by low-level image metrics such as self-similarity and complexity, implicating higher-level psychological processing.
neural_sensitivity_to_psychological_dime.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

valuation_of_architectural_processing_in.name: Valuation of architectural processing in visual cortex varies by dimension and task
valuation_of_architectural_processing_in.description: The study concludes that the visual brain differentially processes architectural interiors according to psychological dimension (coherence vs. fascination vs. hominess) and the cognitive task being performed (beauty vs. approach-avoidance), implying flexible, context-sensitive aesthetic valuation.
valuation_of_architectural_processing_in.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

psychometric_network_analysis_as_a_tool_.name: Psychometric Network Analysis as a tool for identifying aesthetic response dimensions
psychometric_network_analysis_as_a_tool_.description: The study applied Psychometric Network Analysis (PNA) alongside PCA to 16 aesthetic response measures, using the convergence of both methods to identify and validate the three-component structure of responses to architectural interiors.
psychometric_network_analysis_as_a_tool_.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

aesthetic_responses_to_architectural_int.name: Aesthetic responses to architectural interiors reduce to a small number of key psychological dimensions
aesthetic_responses_to_architectural_int.description: The first hypothesis tested is that aesthetic responses to architectural spaces are not idiosyncratic but cluster into a few interpretable dimensions sensitive to design features, as operationalized by the three-component solution.
aesthetic_responses_to_architectural_int.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

fmri_reanalysis_linking_behavioral_aesth.name: fMRI reanalysis linking behavioral aesthetic dimensions to neural activity
fmri_reanalysis_linking_behavioral_aesth.description: Experiment 3 reanalyzed data from an existing fMRI study (n=18) by using the behaviorally derived PCA dimension scores as parametric regressors on BOLD signal during beauty and approach-avoidance tasks, linking psychological constructs to neural correlates.
fmri_reanalysis_linking_behavioral_aesth.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

small_world_network_features_of_the_hebr.name: Small-World Network features of the Hebrew mental lexicon
small_world_network_features_of_the_hebr.description: Analysis of Hebrew free association norms reveals that the Hebrew lexicon exhibits small-world network properties, specifically a high clustering coefficient and a scale-free degree distribution.
small_world_network_features_of_the_hebr.paper: Global and Local Features of Semantic Networks: Evidence from the Hebrew Mental Lexicon

association_correlations_as_a_method_for.name: Association correlations as a method for probing global lexicon structure
association_correlations_as_a_method_for.description: The study uses correlations between target words based on similarity of their association responses (association correlations) to investigate the global features of the Hebrew semantic network.
association_correlations_as_a_method_for.paper: Global and Local Features of Semantic Networks: Evidence from the Hebrew Mental Lexicon

association_dependencies_as_a_method_for.name: Association dependencies as a method for probing local lexicon structure
association_dependencies_as_a_method_for.description: The study introduces association dependencies—measures of how individual words influence activation spread to other words in the network—to investigate local features of the Hebrew lexicon.
association_dependencies_as_a_method_for.paper: Global and Local Features of Semantic Networks: Evidence from the Hebrew Mental Lexicon

semantic_free_categories_emerge_from_ass.name: Semantic free categories emerge from association correlation structure
semantic_free_categories_emerge_from_ass.description: The association correlation approach enables identification of how words cluster into semantically related 'free categories,' revealing the lexicon's internal grouping structure.
semantic_free_categories_emerge_from_ass.paper: Global and Local Features of Semantic Networks: Evidence from the Hebrew Mental Lexicon

network_properties_of_hebrew_lexicon_inf.name: Network properties of Hebrew lexicon influence semantic search processes
network_properties_of_hebrew_lexicon_inf.description: The paper argues that the small-world and scale-free properties of the Hebrew semantic network, along with local dependency structures, influence how cognitive semantic search processes operate, as illustrated by the Remote Association Test.
network_properties_of_hebrew_lexicon_inf.paper: Global and Local Features of Semantic Networks: Evidence from the Hebrew Mental Lexicon

applicability_of_network_theory_to_a_non.name: Applicability of network theory to a non-English (Hebrew) lexicon
applicability_of_network_theory_to_a_non.description: The study extends semantic network methodology—previously dominated by English-language data—to Hebrew, demonstrating cross-linguistic applicability of graph-theoretic approaches to mental lexicon research.
applicability_of_network_theory_to_a_non.paper: Global and Local Features of Semantic Networks: Evidence from the Hebrew Mental Lexicon

remote_association_test_performance_link.name: Remote Association Test performance linked to lexicon network topology
remote_association_test_performance_link.description: The paper discusses the Remote Association Test (RAT) in light of its network findings, suggesting that network topology—specifically spreading activation patterns shaped by small-world and scale-free properties—underpins RAT performance.
remote_association_test_performance_link.paper: Global and Local Features of Semantic Networks: Evidence from the Hebrew Mental Lexicon

spreading_activation_properties_shaped_b.name: Spreading activation properties shaped by Hebrew network topology
spreading_activation_properties_shaped_b.description: The study finds that scale-free and small-world properties of the Hebrew lexicon determine how activation spreads—some words facilitate while others inhibit the propagation of activation through the semantic network.
spreading_activation_properties_shaped_b.paper: Global and Local Features of Semantic Networks: Evidence from the Hebrew Mental Lexicon

nct_application_to_creativity_and_intell.name: NCT application to creativity and intelligence via DTI
nct_application_to_creativity_and_intell.description: The paper applies network control theory (NCT) to diffusion tensor imaging (DTI) structural brain data in a large sample to quantitatively examine how cognitive control contributes to individual differences in creativity and intelligence.
nct_application_to_creativity_and_intell.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

intelligence_linked_to_ripl_driving_brai.name: Intelligence linked to rIPL driving brain to easy-to-reach states
intelligence_linked_to_ripl_driving_brai.description: Intelligence is associated with the ability of the right inferior parietal lobe (rIPL) to drive the brain system into energetically easy-to-reach neural states, as measured by average controllability.
intelligence_linked_to_ripl_driving_brai.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

intelligence_linked_to_lower_integration.name: Intelligence linked to lower integration in left retrosplenial cortex
intelligence_linked_to_lower_integration.description: Lower integration abilities in the left retrosplenial cortex (lRSC) are associated with higher intelligence, suggesting a distinct structural control role for this region.
intelligence_linked_to_lower_integration.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

creativity_linked_to_rdlpfc_driving_brai.name: Creativity linked to rDLPFC driving brain to difficult-to-reach states
creativity_linked_to_rdlpfc_driving_brai.description: Creativity is associated with the ability of the right dorsolateral prefrontal cortex (inferior frontal junction) to drive the brain into energetically difficult-to-reach neural states, as measured by modal controllability.
creativity_linked_to_rdlpfc_driving_brai.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

creativity_linked_to_higher_integration_.name: Creativity linked to higher integration in sensorimotor areas
creativity_linked_to_higher_integration_.description: Higher integration abilities in sensorimotor cortical areas are positively associated with creative ability, pointing to a role of sensorimotor network integration in creative cognition.
creativity_linked_to_higher_integration_.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

distinct_but_overlapping_nct_profiles_fo.name: Distinct but overlapping NCT profiles for creativity facets
distinct_but_overlapping_nct_profiles_fo.description: Different facets of creativity—fluency, flexibility, and originality—relate to generally similar but not identical network controllability processes, suggesting partially shared and partially unique neural control mechanisms.
distinct_but_overlapping_nct_profiles_fo.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

nct_as_a_quantitative_framework_for_cogn.name: NCT as a quantitative framework for cognitive control in high-level cognition
nct_as_a_quantitative_framework_for_cogn.description: Network control theory is used as a computational approach that mathematically models inter-region activity propagation along structural brain networks, characterizing each region's role in regulating whole-brain function based on its anatomical fingerprint.
nct_as_a_quantitative_framework_for_cogn.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

dynamic_neural_network_interaction_as_ke.name: Dynamic neural network interaction as key to creativity and intelligence
dynamic_neural_network_interaction_as_ke.description: The paper claims that high-level cognitive constructs like creativity and intelligence require dynamic interaction across neural network systems, and that cognitive control processes guide this interaction.
dynamic_neural_network_interaction_as_ke.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

divergent_structural_control_mechanisms_.name: Divergent structural control mechanisms for creativity vs. intelligence
divergent_structural_control_mechanisms_.description: The study claims that creativity and intelligence are served by different network control mechanisms—creativity by difficult-to-reach state transitions and intelligence by easy-to-reach ones—suggesting neurally dissociable processes.
divergent_structural_control_mechanisms_.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

semantic_network_underdevelopment_in_chi.name: Semantic network underdevelopment in children with cochlear implants
semantic_network_underdevelopment_in_chi.description: Children with cochlear implants (CIs) showed a less-developed semantic network structure compared to age-matched normal hearing (NH) peers, as measured by verbal fluency task responses analyzed with network methodology.
semantic_network_underdevelopment_in_chi.paper: Semantic organization in children with cochlear implants: computational analysis of verbal fluency

larger_aspl_and_diameter_in_nh_versus_ci.name: Larger ASPL and diameter in NH versus CI semantic networks
larger_aspl_and_diameter_in_nh_versus_ci.description: The average shortest path length and network diameter measures were larger for the NH group compared to the CI group, indicating a more expansive and differentiated semantic network structure in NH children.
larger_aspl_and_diameter_in_nh_versus_ci.paper: Semantic organization in children with cochlear implants: computational analysis of verbal fluency

verbal_fluency_task_as_a_method_for_prob.name: Verbal fluency task as a method for probing semantic network structure in CI children
verbal_fluency_task_as_a_method_for_prob.description: A timed animal verbal fluency task was administered to 27 CI children and 27 NH children ages 7–10; responses were analyzed using correlation and network methodologies to extract and compare semantic network structures.
verbal_fluency_task_as_a_method_for_prob.paper: Semantic organization in children with cochlear implants: computational analysis of verbal fluency

sample_matched_vs_word_matched_correlati.name: Sample-matched vs word-matched correlation networks as complementary analysis approaches
sample_matched_vs_word_matched_correlati.description: The study introduces two network construction approaches—sample-matched correlation networks (SMCN) based on all animal names generated by each group, and word-matched correlation networks (WMCN) based only on common animal names—to enable fair cross-group comparison.
sample_matched_vs_word_matched_correlati.paper: Semantic organization in children with cochlear implants: computational analysis of verbal fluency

hearing_experience_shapes_semantic_netwo.name: Hearing experience shapes semantic network organization in childhood
hearing_experience_shapes_semantic_netwo.description: The study implies that reduced or altered auditory input from early hearing impairment leads to underdeveloped semantic network organization, with CI children showing structurally simpler networks than NH peers despite implantation.
hearing_experience_shapes_semantic_netwo.paper: Semantic organization in children with cochlear implants: computational analysis of verbal fluency

clinical_and_practical_implications_of_s.name: Clinical and practical implications of semantic network analysis for CI populations
clinical_and_practical_implications_of_s.description: The study aims to discuss practical and clinical implications of finding underdeveloped semantic networks in CI children, suggesting that network-based verbal fluency analysis could inform rehabilitation and intervention strategies.
clinical_and_practical_implications_of_s.paper: Semantic organization in children with cochlear implants: computational analysis of verbal fluency

semantic_memory_structure_predicts_verba.name: Semantic memory structure predicts verbal but not figural creativity
semantic_memory_structure_predicts_verba.description: The study found that more flexible semantic network structure (higher connectivity, shorter distances, lower modularity) was associated with verbal creativity, but no comparable relationship was found between semantic memory structure and figural creativity.
semantic_memory_structure_predicts_verba.paper: The relation between semantic memory structure, associative abilities, and verbal and figural creativity

associative_abilities_mediate_the_link_b.name: Associative abilities mediate the link between semantic network structure and verbal creativity
associative_abilities_mediate_the_link_b.description: The study provides evidence that associative abilities statistically mediate the relationship between semantic memory structure and verbal creativity, suggesting efficient information spread in semantic memory facilitates verbal creative thinking through associative processes.
associative_abilities_mediate_the_link_b.paper: The relation between semantic memory structure, associative abilities, and verbal and figural creativity

individual_based_semantic_network_constr.name: Individual-based semantic network construction for creativity research
individual_based_semantic_network_constr.description: The study constructed individual-level semantic networks from participants' cognitive test responses and used network science metrics (connectivity, path length, modularity) to index semantic memory structure for each person.
individual_based_semantic_network_constr.paper: The relation between semantic memory structure, associative abilities, and verbal and figural creativity

differential_role_of_semantic_memory_vs_.name: Differential role of semantic memory vs. associative ability in verbal vs. figural creativity
differential_role_of_semantic_memory_vs_.description: The authors argue that semantic memory structure and associative abilities jointly explain verbal creativity but not figural creativity, implying these two creative modalities draw on fundamentally different cognitive resources.
differential_role_of_semantic_memory_vs_.paper: The relation between semantic memory structure, associative abilities, and verbal and figural creativity

support_and_extension_of_the_associative.name: Support and extension of the associative theory of creativity via network science
support_and_extension_of_the_associative.description: By linking network-level measures of semantic memory to associative abilities and verbal creativity, the study provides quantitative support for Mednick's associative theory and extends it by specifying the mediating role of associative processes.
support_and_extension_of_the_associative.paper: The relation between semantic memory structure, associative abilities, and verbal and figural creativity

semantic_induction_increases_divergent_t.name: semantic_induction_increases_divergent_thinking_originality
semantic_induction_increases_divergent_t.description: Inducing a semantic retrieval orientation prior to a divergent thinking task was associated with higher idea originality as measured by computational semantic distance estimates, compared to an episodic induction condition.
semantic_induction_increases_divergent_t.paper: Default Network Contributions to Episodic and Semantic Processing During Divergent Creative Thinking: A Representational Similarity Analysis

semantic_induction_rsa_similarity_in_ag_.name: semantic_induction_RSA_similarity_in_AG_PCC_IPL
semantic_induction_rsa_similarity_in_ag_.description: Representational similarity analysis revealed that multivoxel activity patterns during semantic induction and subsequent idea generation were more similar to each other within the left angular gyrus, posterior cingulate cortex, and left anterior inferior parietal lobe compared to the episodic induction condition.
semantic_induction_rsa_similarity_in_ag_.paper: Default Network Contributions to Episodic and Semantic Processing During Divergent Creative Thinking: A Representational Similarity Analysis

episodic_induction_rsa_similarity_in_par.name: episodic_induction_RSA_similarity_in_parahippocampal_gyrus_and_right_IPL
episodic_induction_rsa_similarity_in_par.description: Multivoxel activity patterns during episodic induction and subsequent idea generation were more similar within the left parahippocampal gyrus and right anterior inferior parietal lobe compared to the semantic induction condition.
episodic_induction_rsa_similarity_in_par.paper: Default Network Contributions to Episodic and Semantic Processing During Divergent Creative Thinking: A Representational Similarity Analysis

dissociable_episodic_and_semantic_memory.name: dissociable_episodic_and_semantic_memory_contributions_to_divergent_thinking
dissociable_episodic_and_semantic_memory.description: The study argues that episodic and semantic memory make distinct, dissociable contributions to creative cognition, with specific sub-regions of the default network preferentially supporting each memory process during divergent thinking.
dissociable_episodic_and_semantic_memory.paper: Default Network Contributions to Episodic and Semantic Processing During Divergent Creative Thinking: A Representational Similarity Analysis

rsa_as_method_for_linking_memory_inducti.name: RSA_as_method_for_linking_memory_induction_to_idea_generation_neural_patterns
rsa_as_method_for_linking_memory_inducti.description: The study employs representational similarity analysis to compare multivoxel neural activity patterns across an induction phase (episodic or semantic) and a subsequent divergent thinking phase, enabling detection of memory-process-specific neural representations.
rsa_as_method_for_linking_memory_inducti.paper: Default Network Contributions to Episodic and Semantic Processing During Divergent Creative Thinking: A Representational Similarity Analysis

default_network_regional_specialization_.name: default_network_regional_specialization_for_memory_type_during_creativity
default_network_regional_specialization_.description: Rather than acting as a uniform system, the default network contains sub-regions that are differentially recruited depending on whether episodic or semantic memory processes are engaged during divergent creative thinking.
default_network_regional_specialization_.paper: Default Network Contributions to Episodic and Semantic Processing During Divergent Creative Thinking: A Representational Similarity Analysis

episodic_semantic_retrieval_orientation_.name: episodic_semantic_retrieval_orientation_induction_paradigm
episodic_semantic_retrieval_orientation_.description: The study uses a pre-task induction procedure to orient participants toward either episodic (autobiographical event) or semantic (conceptual meaning) retrieval modes before performing a divergent thinking task inside an fMRI scanner.
episodic_semantic_retrieval_orientation_.paper: Default Network Contributions to Episodic and Semantic Processing During Divergent Creative Thinking: A Representational Similarity Analysis

computational_semantic_distance_as_origi.name: computational_semantic_distance_as_originality_measure_in_fmri_creativity_study
computational_semantic_distance_as_origi.description: The study uses computational estimates of semantic distance between generated ideas and prompt concepts as an objective measure of divergent thinking originality within an fMRI paradigm, bridging behavioral and neural levels of analysis.
computational_semantic_distance_as_origi.paper: Default Network Contributions to Episodic and Semantic Processing During Divergent Creative Thinking: A Representational Similarity Analysis

semna_pipeline_as_a_comprehensive_tutori.name: SemNA pipeline as a comprehensive tutorial for semantic network analysis
semna_pipeline_as_a_comprehensive_tutori.description: The paper presents a start-to-finish semantic network analysis (SemNA) pipeline covering preprocessing, estimating, and analyzing semantic networks, accompanied by an R tutorial using a suite of R packages. It aims to reduce barriers for both unfamiliar and experienced researchers.
semna_pipeline_as_a_comprehensive_tutori.paper: Semantic network analysis (SemNA): A tutorial on preprocessing, estimating, and analyzing semantic networks.

semnetdictionaries_and_semnetcleaner_as_.name: SemNetDictionaries and SemNetCleaner as efficient preprocessing tools
semnetdictionaries_and_semnetcleaner_as_.description: Two R packages, SemNetDictionaries and SemNetCleaner, are introduced to provide an efficient, reproducible, and transparent approach to preprocessing linguistic (semantic fluency) data, addressing the tedious and laborious nature of manual preprocessing.
semnetdictionaries_and_semnetcleaner_as_.paper: Semantic network analysis (SemNA): A tutorial on preprocessing, estimating, and analyzing semantic networks.

semnet_as_a_graphical_user_interface_dri.name: SemNeT as a graphical-user-interface-driven network estimation and comparison tool
semnet_as_a_graphical_user_interface_dri.description: The R package SemNeT provides methods and measures for estimating and statistically comparing semantic networks via a point-and-click graphical user interface, lowering the programming barrier for researchers.
semnet_as_a_graphical_user_interface_dri.paper: Semantic network analysis (SemNA): A tutorial on preprocessing, estimating, and analyzing semantic networks.

lack_of_accessible_resources_as_a_barrie.name: Lack of accessible resources as a barrier to semantic network analysis adoption
lack_of_accessible_resources_as_a_barrie.description: The paper identifies that limited scope of semantic network applications in psychology is partly due to a lack of resources for researchers unfamiliar with the approach, hindering broader uptake of the methodology.
lack_of_accessible_resources_as_a_barrie.paper: Semantic network analysis (SemNA): A tutorial on preprocessing, estimating, and analyzing semantic networks.

tedious_preprocessing_of_semantic_data_a.name: Tedious preprocessing of semantic data as a barrier to semantic network analysis
tedious_preprocessing_of_semantic_data_a.description: Manual preprocessing of linguistic data for semantic network construction is characterized as tedious and laborious, posing a barrier even for researchers already knowledgeable about semantic network methods.
tedious_preprocessing_of_semantic_data_a.paper: Semantic network analysis (SemNA): A tutorial on preprocessing, estimating, and analyzing semantic networks.

real_world_data_demonstration_of_the_sem.name: Real-world data demonstration of the SemNA pipeline
real_world_data_demonstration_of_the_sem.description: The article uses real-world data to demonstrate the full pipeline from raw semantic fluency data to semantic network analysis results, providing a concrete worked example for readers.
real_world_data_demonstration_of_the_sem.paper: Semantic network analysis (SemNA): A tutorial on preprocessing, estimating, and analyzing semantic networks.

percolation_analysis_as_a_method_for_qua.name: percolation_analysis_as_a_method_for_quantifying_semantic_network_flexibility
percolation_analysis_as_a_method_for_qua.description: Percolation analysis examines how a semantic memory network resists 'attacks' or breaking apart, providing a quantitative measure of network flexibility. The study applies this method to semantic networks derived from verbal fluency performance in younger and older adults.
percolation_analysis_as_a_method_for_qua.paper: Quantifying Flexibility in Thought: The Resiliency of Semantic Networks Differs Across the Lifespan

older_adults_semantic_networks_are_less_.name: older_adults_semantic_networks_are_less_flexible_than_younger_adults
older_adults_semantic_networks_are_less_.description: Across three independent datasets (total N=78 younger, 78 older adults), percolation integrals of younger adults were consistently larger than those of older adults, indicating older adults' semantic networks broke down faster. This finding held across all three datasets.
older_adults_semantic_networks_are_less_.paper: Quantifying Flexibility in Thought: The Resiliency of Semantic Networks Differs Across the Lifespan

dissociation_between_semantic_knowledge_.name: dissociation_between_semantic_knowledge_stability_and_network_flexibility_across_lifespan
dissociation_between_semantic_knowledge_.description: Despite older adults having a broader vocabulary and stable semantic knowledge across the lifespan, their semantic network flexibility (as measured by percolation analysis) is diminished relative to younger adults. This dissociation suggests knowledge storage and network flexibility are separable properties.
dissociation_between_semantic_knowledge_.paper: Quantifying Flexibility in Thought: The Resiliency of Semantic Networks Differs Across the Lifespan

diminished_semantic_network_flexibility_.name: diminished_semantic_network_flexibility_as_a_contributor_to_age_related_language_production_decline
diminished_semantic_network_flexibility_.description: The study proposes that reduced flexibility in older adults' semantic networks, as quantified by percolation integrals, may be a contributing factor to observed age-related differences in language production. This links structural network properties to functional language outcomes.
diminished_semantic_network_flexibility_.paper: Quantifying Flexibility in Thought: The Resiliency of Semantic Networks Differs Across the Lifespan

verbal_fluency_derived_semantic_networks.name: verbal_fluency_derived_semantic_networks_for_aging_research
verbal_fluency_derived_semantic_networks.description: The study generates semantic networks from verbal fluency task performance for both younger and older adults across three independent datasets, applying percolation analysis to these networks. This operationalizes age-related semantic memory structure differences in a quantitative, network-theoretic framework.
verbal_fluency_derived_semantic_networks.paper: Quantifying Flexibility in Thought: The Resiliency of Semantic Networks Differs Across the Lifespan

replication_of_age_related_flexibility_d.name: replication_of_age_related_flexibility_differences_across_three_independent_datasets
replication_of_age_related_flexibility_d.description: The finding that older adults' percolation integrals are smaller than younger adults' was replicated across all three independent datasets (total N=78 younger, 78 older adults), strengthening the reliability of the result.
replication_of_age_related_flexibility_d.paper: Quantifying Flexibility in Thought: The Resiliency of Semantic Networks Differs Across the Lifespan

polysemous_word_associative_fluency_task.name: Polysemous word associative fluency task for semantic search
polysemous_word_associative_fluency_task.description: The authors designed a novel associative fluency task based on polysemous words to isolate two semantic memory search components—clustering (persistent search within a meaning) and switching (shifting between different meanings)—as a window into creative cognition.
polysemous_word_associative_fluency_task.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

clustering_correlates_with_divergent_thi.name: Clustering correlates with divergent thinking
clustering_correlates_with_divergent_thi.description: Clustering behavior in the polysemous word associative fluency task—persistent search within a single meaning—correlated with divergent thinking scores, suggesting it reflects an attentional controlled search process relevant to idea generation breadth.
clustering_correlates_with_divergent_thi.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

switching_correlates_with_remote_associa.name: Switching correlates with remote associate combination ability
switching_correlates_with_remote_associa.description: Switching between different meanings of polysemous words correlated with performance on a remote associates task, indicating that flexible transitions across semantic meanings underlie the capacity to combine distant concepts creatively.
switching_correlates_with_remote_associa.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

switching_predicted_by_default_control_s.name: Switching predicted by default-control-salience network connectivity
switching_predicted_by_default_control_s.description: Switching in semantic memory search was predicted by functional connectivity between the default, control, and salience neural networks, implicating large-scale network interactions in flexible semantic exploration.
switching_predicted_by_default_control_s.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

clustering_relies_on_control_salience_at.name: Clustering relies on control-salience-attentional network interactions
clustering_relies_on_control_salience_at.description: Clustering in the polysemous word task depended on interactions among control, salience, and attentional neural networks, suggesting that sustained, focused semantic search is supported by these networks rather than the default network.
clustering_relies_on_control_salience_at.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

switching_reflects_memory_structure_and_.name: Switching reflects memory structure and executive ability interaction
switching_reflects_memory_structure_and_.description: Switching scores correlated with both semantic memory structure and executive abilities, suggesting that flexible transitions between word meanings depend on the interplay of stored knowledge organization and top-down cognitive control.
switching_reflects_memory_structure_and_.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

alternations_between_exploratory_search_.name: Alternations between exploratory search and focused attention support creativity
alternations_between_exploratory_search_.description: The authors argue that creativity is supported by dynamic alternation between exploratory (switching) and focused (clustering) semantic search modes, integrating memory structure with attentional control processes.
alternations_between_exploratory_search_.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

distinction_between_clustering_and_switc.name: Distinction between clustering and switching as semantic search components
distinction_between_clustering_and_switc.description: The study operationalizes semantic memory search as comprising two separable components—clustering (staying within a semantic cluster or word meaning) and switching (moving between meanings)—adapted from verbal fluency methodology to the polysemous word context.
distinction_between_clustering_and_switc.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

semantic_search_components_as_neurocogni.name: Semantic search components as neurocognitive correlates of creative ability
semantic_search_components_as_neurocogni.description: By linking specific semantic search behaviors to distinct creativity facets and neural network connectivity, the study provides a neurocognitive account of how memory search mechanisms underlie different aspects of creative ability.
semantic_search_components_as_neurocogni.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

semantic_memory_network_properties_predi.name: semantic_memory_network_properties_predict_real_life_creativity
semantic_memory_network_properties_predi.description: Individual semantic memory network properties, estimated from semantic relatedness judgments, significantly predicted participants' real-life creativity. This provides direct empirical support for associative theories linking semantic memory structure to creative behavior.
semantic_memory_network_properties_predi.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

task_based_functional_connectivity_predi.name: task_based_functional_connectivity_predicts_creativity_related_semantic_network_properties
task_based_functional_connectivity_predi.description: Using connectome predictive modeling on multi-echo fMRI data from a semantic relatedness judgment task, the authors identified patterns of task-based functional connectivity that significantly predicted creativity-related semantic memory network properties.
task_based_functional_connectivity_predi.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

semantic_memory_structure_mediates_brain.name: semantic_memory_structure_mediates_brain_connectivity_to_real_life_creativity
semantic_memory_structure_mediates_brain.description: Semantic memory network properties mediated the relationship between task-based functional connectivity and real-life creativity, establishing a brain–cognition–behavior pathway. This positions semantic memory structure as a mechanistic link between neural patterns and creative achievement.
semantic_memory_structure_mediates_brain.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

connectome_predictive_modeling_applied_t.name: connectome_predictive_modeling_applied_to_creativity_and_semantic_memory
connectome_predictive_modeling_applied_t.description: The study applies connectome predictive modeling (CPM) to task-based fMRI data to predict semantic memory network properties and real-life creativity, demonstrating a data-driven brain-behavior mapping approach in the creativity domain.
connectome_predictive_modeling_applied_t.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

individual_semantic_networks_estimated_f.name: individual_semantic_networks_estimated_from_relatedness_judgment_task
individual_semantic_networks_estimated_f.description: Participants' individual semantic memory networks were estimated using ratings from a semantic relatedness judgment task acquired during fMRI, enabling person-specific network properties to be linked to both brain connectivity and creative behavior.
individual_semantic_networks_estimated_f.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

multi_echo_fmri_for_creativity_and_seman.name: multi_echo_fmri_for_creativity_and_semantic_memory_research
multi_echo_fmri_for_creativity_and_seman.description: Multi-echo fMRI acquisition was used to improve signal quality and reduce noise during the semantic relatedness judgment task, supporting more reliable connectivity estimates in creativity research.
multi_echo_fmri_for_creativity_and_seman.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

coupling_behavioral_cognitive_and_neural.name: coupling_behavioral_cognitive_and_neural_levels_via_computational_network_science
coupling_behavioral_cognitive_and_neural.description: The study demonstrates how computational network science can integrate behavioral (creativity scores), cognitive (semantic memory structure), and neural (fMRI connectivity) levels of analysis within a single framework.
coupling_behavioral_cognitive_and_neural.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

real_life_creative_behavior_relies_on_se.name: real_life_creative_behavior_relies_on_semantic_memory_neural_substrates
real_life_creative_behavior_relies_on_se.description: The study argues that real-life creative behavior depends not only on cognitive semantic memory structure but also on its underlying neural substrates as indexed by task-based functional connectivity patterns.
real_life_creative_behavior_relies_on_se.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

motor_system_involvement_in_creative_tho.name: motor_system_involvement_in_creative_thought_generation
motor_system_involvement_in_creative_tho.description: The paper argues that the motor cortex and motor system are implicated in generating creative thoughts, not just executing them, supported by a collection of neuroimaging evidence.
motor_system_involvement_in_creative_tho.paper: The role of the motor system in generating creative thoughts

embodied_grounded_cognition_framework_fo.name: embodied_grounded_cognition_framework_for_creativity
embodied_grounded_cognition_framework_fo.description: The paper proposes that the embodied or grounded cognition framework accounts for creative thinking by positing that motor simulations of tool use and actions partially implement creative ideation.
embodied_grounded_cognition_framework_fo.paper: The role of the motor system in generating creative thoughts

motor_simulations_support_higher_order_c.name: motor_simulations_support_higher_order_creative_cognition
motor_simulations_support_higher_order_c.description: The paper claims that reactivating the motor system via action simulations does not produce overt movement but instead supports higher-order cognitive goals such as generating creative uses for objects or improvising.
motor_simulations_support_higher_order_c.paper: The role of the motor system in generating creative thoughts

neglect_of_motor_system_in_mechanistic_c.name: neglect_of_motor_system_in_mechanistic_creativity_models
neglect_of_motor_system_in_mechanistic_c.description: The paper identifies a gap in neurocognitive models of creativity: they typically overlook the role of the motor cortex, leaving mechanistic accounts incomplete.
neglect_of_motor_system_in_mechanistic_c.paper: The role of the motor system in generating creative thoughts

neuroimaging_evidence_for_functionally_n.name: neuroimaging_evidence_for_functionally_necessary_motor_role_in_creativity
neuroimaging_evidence_for_functionally_n.description: The paper highlights neuroimaging research indicating that the motor system plays a functionally necessary role in generating creative output, not merely correlating with it.
neuroimaging_evidence_for_functionally_n.paper: The role of the motor system in generating creative thoughts

curiosity_as_a_driver_of_creative_and_ae.name: Curiosity as a Driver of Creative and Aesthetic Experience
curiosity_as_a_driver_of_creative_and_ae.description: The paper advances the hypothesis that creative and aesthetic experiences depend on curiosity as a driver of information-seeking and exploratory behavior, because they exemplify situations that highlight gaps in knowledge or require problem finding and solving.
curiosity_as_a_driver_of_creative_and_ae.paper: A Thirst for Knowledge: Grounding Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics in Memory and Reward Neural Systems

shared_psychological_and_neural_mechanis.name: Shared Psychological and Neural Mechanisms Across Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics
shared_psychological_and_neural_mechanis.description: Despite being studied separately, curiosity, creativity, and aesthetics are argued to share overlapping psychological and neural mechanisms, all linked to Openness to Experience and information-seeking drives.
shared_psychological_and_neural_mechanis.paper: A Thirst for Knowledge: Grounding Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics in Memory and Reward Neural Systems

openness_to_experience_enhances_curious_.name: Openness to Experience Enhances Curious, Creative, and Aesthetic Behaviors
openness_to_experience_enhances_curious_.description: The review highlights Openness to Experience as a critical personality trait that enhances curious behaviors, creative acts, and aesthetic appreciation, serving as a linking construct across the three domains.
openness_to_experience_enhances_curious_.paper: A Thirst for Knowledge: Grounding Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics in Memory and Reward Neural Systems

semantic_memory_as_a_substrate_for_infor.name: Semantic Memory as a Substrate for Information-Seeking in Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics
semantic_memory_as_a_substrate_for_infor.description: The paper highlights the role of semantic memory in information-seeking behavior underlying curiosity, creativity, and aesthetic experience, linking knowledge structure to knowledge acquisition.
semantic_memory_as_a_substrate_for_infor.paper: A Thirst for Knowledge: Grounding Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics in Memory and Reward Neural Systems

mesolimbic_dopaminergic_reward_system_un.name: Mesolimbic Dopaminergic Reward System Underpins Information-Seeking in Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics
mesolimbic_dopaminergic_reward_system_un.description: The paper examines the neurobiological underpinnings of curiosity, creativity, and aesthetics in relation to the mesolimbic dopaminergic reward system as the neural substrate for information-seeking.
mesolimbic_dopaminergic_reward_system_un.paper: A Thirst for Knowledge: Grounding Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics in Memory and Reward Neural Systems

stages_of_art_viewing_and_making_as_expr.name: Stages of Art Viewing and Making as Expressions of Curiosity
stages_of_art_viewing_and_making_as_expr.description: The review argues that stages of art viewing and art making relate to curiosity, framing aesthetic and creative activities as manifestations of information-seeking motivated by knowledge gaps.
stages_of_art_viewing_and_making_as_expr.paper: A Thirst for Knowledge: Grounding Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics in Memory and Reward Neural Systems

information_seeking_as_the_unifying_mech.name: Information-Seeking as the Unifying Mechanism Linking Curiosity to Creativity and Aesthetics
information_seeking_as_the_unifying_mech.description: The paper proposes that information-seeking—the key behavior attributed to curiosity—motivates both creative and aesthetic activities, providing a unifying mechanistic account across the three domains.
information_seeking_as_the_unifying_mech.paper: A Thirst for Knowledge: Grounding Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics in Memory and Reward Neural Systems

link_between_curiosity_and_creativity_vi.name: Link Between Curiosity and Creativity via Knowledge-Gap Problem Finding
link_between_curiosity_and_creativity_vi.description: Curiosity is linked to creativity specifically because both involve identifying gaps in knowledge (problem finding), which motivates exploratory and generative cognitive behavior.
link_between_curiosity_and_creativity_vi.paper: A Thirst for Knowledge: Grounding Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics in Memory and Reward Neural Systems

associative_theory_of_creativity_as_rese.name: associative_theory_of_creativity_as_research_framework
associative_theory_of_creativity_as_rese.description: The associative theory of creativity posits that higher creative individuals possess a richer semantic memory structure that facilitates broader associative search, enabling combination of remote concepts into novel and appropriate ideas. This theory anchors the author's research program across typical and clinical populations.
associative_theory_of_creativity_as_rese.paper: The Role of Knowledge in Creative Thinking

computational_tools_enable_quantificatio.name: computational_tools_enable_quantification_of_previously_subjective_creativity_constructs
computational_tools_enable_quantificatio.description: The author argues that computational tools from network science, NLP, and machine learning allow quantification of constructs such as idea remoteness, associative flexibility, and semantic memory richness that were previously studied only subjectively. This is presented as a key advancement enabling direct empirical tests of classic creativity theories.
computational_tools_enable_quantificatio.paper: The Role of Knowledge in Creative Thinking

network_science_applied_to_semantic_memo.name: network_science_applied_to_semantic_memory_for_creativity_research_program
network_science_applied_to_semantic_memo.description: The paper outlines a research program that couples computational network science with empirical cognitive and neural methods to study semantic memory's role in creativity and associative thinking. The integration spans typical populations, clinical populations, and multiple levels of analysis (behavioral, cognitive, neural).
network_science_applied_to_semantic_memo.paper: The Role of Knowledge in Creative Thinking

role_of_knowledge_and_semantic_memory_in.name: role_of_knowledge_and_semantic_memory_in_high_level_cognition
