role_of_knowledge_and_semantic_memory_in.description: A central goal of the reviewed research is to investigate the role of knowledge (semantic memory) in high-level cognition, with particular emphasis on creativity, associative thinking, and memory search. This spans both typical and clinical populations.
role_of_knowledge_and_semantic_memory_in.paper: The Role of Knowledge in Creative Thinking

quantification_of_idea_remoteness_via_co.name: quantification_of_idea_remoteness_via_computational_methods
quantification_of_idea_remoteness_via_co.description: The author emphasizes the importance of directly quantifying the remoteness of ideas using computational approaches, treating this as a critical advance over purely subjective assessments of creative output. Network science and NLP tools operationalize remoteness as a measurable property of semantic memory structure.
quantification_of_idea_remoteness_via_co.paper: The Role of Knowledge in Creative Thinking

flexible_and_richer_semantic_memory_stru.name: flexible_and_richer_semantic_memory_structure_as_driver_of_creative_output
flexible_and_richer_semantic_memory_stru.description: The paper claims that flexible and richer semantic memory structure is a key driver of creative output, enabling individuals to traverse and combine distant concepts. This is positioned as a core mechanism linking knowledge to creativity.
flexible_and_richer_semantic_memory_stru.paper: The Role of Knowledge in Creative Thinking

mind_as_network_metaphor_evaluation.name: mind_as_network_metaphor_evaluation
mind_as_network_metaphor_evaluation.description: The paper argues that while network science is a powerful tool for representing cognitive structures, the mind is not simply a network—it may contain networks, but the metaphor is incomplete. This reframes cognitive network science as a useful but bounded framework.
mind_as_network_metaphor_evaluation.paper: Is the Mind a Network? Maps, Vehicles, and Skyhooks in Cognitive Network Science

map_and_vehicle_framework_in_cognitive_n.name: map_and_vehicle_framework_in_cognitive_network_science
map_and_vehicle_framework_in_cognitive_n.description: Cognitive network science has predominantly relied on a map-and-vehicle metaphor, where cognitive structures are represented as networks (maps) and cognitive processes operate over them (vehicles). The paper reviews the assumptions and pitfalls of this dominant framework.
map_and_vehicle_framework_in_cognitive_n.paper: Is the Mind a Network? Maps, Vehicles, and Skyhooks in Cognitive Network Science

new_metaphors_for_cognitive_network_scie.name: new_metaphors_for_cognitive_network_science
new_metaphors_for_cognitive_network_scie.description: The paper introduces new metaphors—wormholes, skyhooks, and generators—to elaborate on and extend the map-and-vehicle framework, addressing its limitations in capturing cognitive dynamics.
new_metaphors_for_cognitive_network_scie.paper: Is the Mind a Network? Maps, Vehicles, and Skyhooks in Cognitive Network Science

one_network_one_process_assumption_as_pi.name: one_network_one_process_assumption_as_pitfall
one_network_one_process_assumption_as_pi.description: The paper identifies a prevalent pitfall in cognitive network science: the tendency to assume a single network structure and a single process operating over it, which oversimplifies the richness of cognition.
one_network_one_process_assumption_as_pi.paper: Is the Mind a Network? Maps, Vehicles, and Skyhooks in Cognitive Network Science

challenge_of_capturing_network_change.name: challenge_of_capturing_network_change
challenge_of_capturing_network_change.description: One of the open questions identified is how cognitive networks change over time, with current approaches largely treating networks as static structures rather than dynamic entities.
challenge_of_capturing_network_change.paper: Is the Mind a Network? Maps, Vehicles, and Skyhooks in Cognitive Network Science

question_of_edge_composition_in_cognitiv.name: question_of_edge_composition_in_cognitive_networks
question_of_edge_composition_in_cognitiv.description: An open question raised is what cognitive networks' edges should represent—whether associations, co-occurrences, causal links, or other relations—with the choice of edge definition having major consequences for interpretation.
question_of_edge_composition_in_cognitiv.paper: Is the Mind a Network? Maps, Vehicles, and Skyhooks in Cognitive Network Science

aggregated_vs_individual_based_networks.name: aggregated_vs_individual_based_networks
aggregated_vs_individual_based_networks.description: The paper raises the open question of whether cognitive networks should be constructed from aggregated group-level data or from individual-level data, noting that each approach has different implications for understanding individual differences in cognition.
aggregated_vs_individual_based_networks.paper: Is the Mind a Network? Maps, Vehicles, and Skyhooks in Cognitive Network Science

network_science_as_tool_for_making_assum.name: network_science_as_tool_for_making_assumptions_visible
network_science_as_tool_for_making_assum.description: A key lesson the paper draws is that adopting a network science framework makes implicit theoretical assumptions about cognitive structure and process more explicit, which itself generates new research questions and enriches the field.
network_science_as_tool_for_making_assum.paper: Is the Mind a Network? Maps, Vehicles, and Skyhooks in Cognitive Network Science

incompleteness_of_network_representation.name: incompleteness_of_network_representation_for_full_cognitive_science
incompleteness_of_network_representation.description: The paper concludes that while the mind may contain networks, it is not a network in its entirety, implying that network science must be complemented by other frameworks to achieve a complete account of cognition.
incompleteness_of_network_representation.paper: Is the Mind a Network? Maps, Vehicles, and Skyhooks in Cognitive Network Science

semantic_richness_costs_and_benefits_to_.name: semantic_richness_costs_and_benefits_to_creative_idea_generation
semantic_richness_costs_and_benefits_to_.description: Semantic richness of a concept—indexed by cue set size—has both benefits and costs to creative idea generation: high-association rich cues boost fluency but reduce originality, while low-association sparse cues boost originality at the cost of fluency.
semantic_richness_costs_and_benefits_to_.paper: Semantic Memory and Creativity: The Costs and Benefits of Semantic Memory Structure in Generating Original Ideas

cue_set_size_as_index_of_semantic_richne.name: cue_set_size_as_index_of_semantic_richness
cue_set_size_as_index_of_semantic_richne.description: Cue set size—defined as the average number of elements associated with a given concept—is used as an operationalization of semantic richness to classify AUT cue words as sparse (low-association) or rich (high-association).
cue_set_size_as_index_of_semantic_richne.paper: Semantic Memory and Creativity: The Costs and Benefits of Semantic Memory Structure in Generating Original Ideas

fluid_intelligence_moderates_sparse_cue_.name: fluid_intelligence_moderates_sparse_cue_originality_penalty
fluid_intelligence_moderates_sparse_cue_.description: An interaction between fluid intelligence and cue set size was found for low-association AUT cues, indicating that individuals higher in fluid intelligence can overcome the fluency costs imposed by sparse semantic knowledge through top-down cognitive control.
fluid_intelligence_moderates_sparse_cue_.paper: Semantic Memory and Creativity: The Costs and Benefits of Semantic Memory Structure in Generating Original Ideas

cognitive_control_facilitates_idea_produ.name: cognitive_control_facilitates_idea_production_under_sparse_knowledge
cognitive_control_facilitates_idea_produ.description: When conceptual knowledge is limited (sparse cues), cognitive control processes—indexed by fluid intelligence—can compensate and facilitate idea production, suggesting top-down intervention mitigates semantic constraint.
cognitive_control_facilitates_idea_produ.paper: Semantic Memory and Creativity: The Costs and Benefits of Semantic Memory Structure in Generating Original Ideas

alternate_uses_task_as_creativity_measur.name: alternate_uses_task_as_creativity_measure_across_cue_types
alternate_uses_task_as_creativity_measur.description: The Alternate Uses Task (AUT) is used across four studies to measure both the quantity (fluency) and quality (originality) of creative idea generation, with cue words varying in semantic richness.
alternate_uses_task_as_creativity_measur.paper: Semantic Memory and Creativity: The Costs and Benefits of Semantic Memory Structure in Generating Original Ideas

differential_impact_of_semantic_richness.name: differential_impact_of_semantic_richness_on_fluency_vs_originality
differential_impact_of_semantic_richness.description: Semantic richness differentially impacts the quality and quantity of generated ideas: richness helps quantity but hurts quality, revealing a trade-off in creative cognition driven by memory structure.
differential_impact_of_semantic_richness.paper: Semantic Memory and Creativity: The Costs and Benefits of Semantic Memory Structure in Generating Original Ideas

replication_of_cue_set_size_effects_acro.name: replication_of_cue_set_size_effects_across_four_studies
replication_of_cue_set_size_effects_acro.description: The paper reports four studies all examining the effect of cue set size on AUT performance, providing a multi-study replication of the core originality-fluency trade-off finding.
replication_of_cue_set_size_effects_acro.paper: Semantic Memory and Creativity: The Costs and Benefits of Semantic Memory Structure in Generating Original Ideas

montessori_education_produces_more_flexi.name: Montessori_education_produces_more_flexible_semantic_network_structure
montessori_education_produces_more_flexi.description: Children with Montessori education show higher connectivity, shorter paths between concepts, and less modularity in their semantic networks compared to traditionally educated peers matched on socioeconomic factors and nonverbal intelligence.
montessori_education_produces_more_flexi.paper: Education shapes the structure of semantic memory and impacts creative thinking

montessori_education_associated_with_hig.name: Montessori_education_associated_with_higher_creative_thinking_scores
montessori_education_associated_with_hig.description: Swiss schoolchildren from Montessori educational backgrounds score higher on creative thinking tests relative to traditionally educated peers matched on socioeconomic factors and nonverbal intelligence.
montessori_education_associated_with_hig.paper: Education shapes the structure of semantic memory and impacts creative thinking

educational_experience_shapes_semantic_m.name: educational_experience_shapes_semantic_memory_representation
educational_experience_shapes_semantic_m.description: The study claims that educational differences influence not only what concepts children learn but how those concepts are represented in semantic memory, going beyond content to affect structural organization.
educational_experience_shapes_semantic_m.paper: Education shapes the structure of semantic memory and impacts creative thinking

flexible_semantic_network_as_mediator_be.name: flexible_semantic_network_as_mediator_between_education_and_creativity
flexible_semantic_network_as_mediator_be.description: The paper suggests that the more flexible semantic network structure observed in Montessori children underlies or mediates their higher creative thinking scores, implying a pathway from educational experience to creativity via semantic memory organization.
flexible_semantic_network_as_mediator_be.paper: Education shapes the structure of semantic memory and impacts creative thinking

computational_network_science_for_studyi.name: computational_network_science_for_studying_child_semantic_memory_across_educational_contexts
computational_network_science_for_studyi.description: The study leverages computational network science tools to extract and compare hidden knowledge structures from schoolchildren in different educational settings, extending the semantic network analysis approach to a pediatric cross-educational comparison design.
computational_network_science_for_studyi.paper: Education shapes the structure of semantic memory and impacts creative thinking

montessori_vs_traditional_education_comp.name: Montessori_vs_traditional_education_comparison_design_with_matched_groups
montessori_vs_traditional_education_comp.description: 67 Swiss schoolchildren from Montessori and traditional educational backgrounds are compared after matching on socioeconomic factors and nonverbal intelligence, isolating the effect of educational environment on semantic memory and creativity.
montessori_vs_traditional_education_comp.paper: Education shapes the structure of semantic memory and impacts creative thinking

education_as_an_environmental_driver_of_.name: education_as_an_environmental_driver_of_higher_cognitive_function
education_as_an_environmental_driver_of_.description: The findings suggest that different educational experiences can affect higher cognitive functions beyond knowledge content, including creative thinking, positioning education as an environmental factor shaping cognitive architecture.
education_as_an_environmental_driver_of_.paper: Education shapes the structure of semantic memory and impacts creative thinking

simulated_visual_hallucinations_via_vr_e.name: Simulated visual hallucinations via VR enhance cognitive flexibility
simulated_visual_hallucinations_via_vr_e.description: Exposure to DeepDream-generated hallucinatory-like panoramic VR videos enhances cognitive flexibility in participants, as measured through behavioral tasks, suggesting that simulated altered perceptual phenomenology can modulate cognition similarly to actual psychedelic drugs.
simulated_visual_hallucinations_via_vr_e.paper: Simulated visual hallucinations in virtual reality enhance cognitive flexibility

deepdream_algorithm_as_generator_of_hall.name: DeepDream algorithm as generator of hallucinatory-like VR stimuli
deepdream_algorithm_as_generator_of_hall.description: The DeepDream algorithm is applied to panoramic VR videos to generate hallucinatory-like visual counterparts that simulate altered perceptual phenomenology associated with psychedelic drug experiences.
deepdream_algorithm_as_generator_of_hall.paper: Simulated visual hallucinations in virtual reality enhance cognitive flexibility

simulated_psychedelic_exposure_produces_.name: Simulated psychedelic exposure produces more flexible semantic network structure
simulated_psychedelic_exposure_produces_.description: Following exposure to hallucinatory-like VR videos, participants' estimated semantic networks exhibit a more flexible structure compared to those estimated after standard video exposure.
simulated_psychedelic_exposure_produces_.paper: Simulated visual hallucinations in virtual reality enhance cognitive flexibility

attenuated_automatic_processing_followin.name: Attenuated automatic processing following simulated psychedelic exposure
attenuated_automatic_processing_followin.description: After simulated psychedelic exposure via hallucinatory VR, participants show a reduced contribution of automatic cognitive processes and chaotic dynamics in their decision-making, indicating a shift away from habitual responses.
attenuated_automatic_processing_followin.paper: Simulated visual hallucinations in virtual reality enhance cognitive flexibility

reorganization_of_cognitive_dynamics_as_.name: Reorganization of cognitive dynamics as mechanism for flexibility enhancement
reorganization_of_cognitive_dynamics_as_.description: The paper proposes that simulated altered perceptual phenomenology enhances cognitive flexibility by reorganizing cognitive dynamics, facilitating exploration of uncommon decision strategies and inhibiting automated choices.
reorganization_of_cognitive_dynamics_as_.paper: Simulated visual hallucinations in virtual reality enhance cognitive flexibility

phenomenological_similarity_between_arti.name: Phenomenological similarity between artificially-induced and actual psychedelic altered perception
phenomenological_similarity_between_arti.description: Prior proof suggests phenomenological similarities between artificially-induced (e.g., VR-DeepDream) and actual psychedelic altered perception, but experimental evidence linking this to cognitive flexibility modulation was previously lacking.
phenomenological_similarity_between_arti.paper: Simulated visual hallucinations in virtual reality enhance cognitive flexibility

psychedelic_drugs_modulate_cognitive_fle.name: Psychedelic drugs modulate cognitive flexibility
psychedelic_drugs_modulate_cognitive_fle.description: Historically, psychedelic drugs are known to modulate cognitive flexibility, a central aspect of cognition permitting adaptation to changing environmental demands, providing the theoretical background for the study.
psychedelic_drugs_modulate_cognitive_fle.paper: Simulated visual hallucinations in virtual reality enhance cognitive flexibility

age_related_differences_in_individual_ba.name: Age-related differences in individual-based semantic network structure
age_related_differences_in_individual_ba.description: Older adults show lower clustering coefficient and global efficiency, and higher modularity in their individual-based semantic memory networks compared to younger adults, replicating group-level findings at the individual level.
age_related_differences_in_individual_ba.paper: Age differences in semantic network structure: Acquiring knowledge shapes semantic memory.

vocabulary_knowledge_predicts_semantic_n.name: Vocabulary knowledge predicts semantic network structure across age groups
vocabulary_knowledge_predicts_semantic_n.description: Vocabulary knowledge shows a negative relationship with clustering coefficient and global efficiency, and a positive relationship with modularity, mirroring the pattern of age effects on semantic network structure.
vocabulary_knowledge_predicts_semantic_n.paper: Age differences in semantic network structure: Acquiring knowledge shapes semantic memory.

vocabulary_knowledge_increases_with_age_.name: Vocabulary knowledge increases with age as a function of knowledge accumulation
vocabulary_knowledge_increases_with_age_.description: Vocabulary knowledge was found to significantly increase with age, suggesting that life-long accumulation of knowledge reshapes the structure of semantic memory networks over the lifespan.
vocabulary_knowledge_increases_with_age_.paper: Age differences in semantic network structure: Acquiring knowledge shapes semantic memory.

individual_differences_in_vocabulary_kno.name: Individual differences in vocabulary knowledge account for age-related semantic network disparities
individual_differences_in_vocabulary_kno.description: The study argues that individual differences, particularly vocabulary knowledge, influence age-related disparities in semantic network properties and language production, going beyond group-level comparisons.
individual_differences_in_vocabulary_kno.paper: Age differences in semantic network structure: Acquiring knowledge shapes semantic memory.

semantic_network_structure_links_to_verb.name: Semantic network structure links to verbal fluency performance
semantic_network_structure_links_to_verb.description: Individual-based semantic network measures (clustering coefficient, global efficiency, modularity) are related to language production as measured by verbal fluency tasks in both age groups.
semantic_network_structure_links_to_verb.paper: Age differences in semantic network structure: Acquiring knowledge shapes semantic memory.

individual_based_semantic_network_constr.name: Individual-based semantic network construction from relatedness judgments in aging research
individual_based_semantic_network_constr.description: Participants' semantic memory networks are estimated from their individual semantic relatedness judgments rather than group-aggregated norms, enabling examination of within-group variability across the lifespan.
individual_based_semantic_network_constr.paper: Age differences in semantic network structure: Acquiring knowledge shapes semantic memory.

knowledge_accumulation_as_a_mechanistic_.name: Knowledge accumulation as a mechanistic driver of lifespan semantic network reorganization
knowledge_accumulation_as_a_mechanistic_.description: The paper proposes that the accumulation of diverse knowledge and life experiences, reflected in higher vocabulary knowledge in older adults, is a primary driver of the observed structural changes in semantic memory networks across the lifespan.
knowledge_accumulation_as_a_mechanistic_.paper: Age differences in semantic network structure: Acquiring knowledge shapes semantic memory.

aging_reduces_facial_attractiveness.name: aging_reduces_facial_attractiveness
aging_reduces_facial_attractiveness.description: The study confirms the hypothesis that with increasing age, faces are perceived as less attractive by human perceivers. This effect is treated as a primary empirical finding of the paper.
aging_reduces_facial_attractiveness.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

perceiver_age_modulates_aging_effect_on_.name: perceiver_age_modulates_aging_effect_on_facial_attractiveness
perceiver_age_modulates_aging_effect_on_.description: The negative effect of face age on attractiveness ratings is less pronounced when judgments are made by older perceivers compared to younger and middle-aged perceivers. This suggests an in-group or own-age bias in attractiveness evaluation.
perceiver_age_modulates_aging_effect_on_.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

perceiver_sex_modulates_aging_effect_on_.name: perceiver_sex_modulates_aging_effect_on_facial_attractiveness
perceiver_sex_modulates_aging_effect_on_.description: Men show a more pronounced reduction in attractiveness ratings for older faces than women do, and this effect is especially strong for male perceivers rating female faces. This sex-of-perceiver modulation is a key moderating finding.
perceiver_sex_modulates_aging_effect_on_.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

attractive_older_faces_perceived_as_eleg.name: attractive_older_faces_perceived_as_elegant_rather_than_beautiful_or_gorgeous
attractive_older_faces_perceived_as_eleg.description: When older faces are judged as attractive, they are more likely to be described as elegant than as beautiful or gorgeous, indicating that the qualitative dimension of attractiveness varies by face age.
attractive_older_faces_perceived_as_eleg.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

older_faces_cluster_together_in_attracti.name: older_faces_cluster_together_in_attractiveness_network
older_faces_cluster_together_in_attracti.description: Network analyses reveal that older faces are more similar to each other in attractiveness and form a segregated cluster distinct from younger faces, suggesting categorical processing of older faces.
older_faces_cluster_together_in_attracti.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

categorical_processing_of_older_faces_in.name: categorical_processing_of_older_faces_in_attractiveness_judgments
categorical_processing_of_older_faces_in.description: The authors conclude that perceivers tend to process older faces categorically rather than individually when making attractiveness judgments, as evidenced by the greater similarity and network segregation of older faces.
categorical_processing_of_older_faces_in.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

attractiveness_as_multidimensional_const.name: attractiveness_as_multidimensional_construct_varying_by_age_sex_and_judgment_type
attractiveness_as_multidimensional_const.description: The paper argues that attractiveness is not a monolithic construct but varies systematically by the age and sex of the perceiver and by the specific dimension of attractiveness being judged (e.g., beautiful, gorgeous, elegant).
attractiveness_as_multidimensional_const.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

computational_network_science_applied_to.name: computational_network_science_applied_to_facial_attractiveness_judgments
computational_network_science_applied_to.description: The study employs computational network science methods to analyze the structure of attractiveness judgments across faces, revealing clustering and segregation patterns that complement empirical rating data.
computational_network_science_applied_to.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

own_age_bias_in_facial_attractiveness_ev.name: own_age_bias_in_facial_attractiveness_evaluation
own_age_bias_in_facial_attractiveness_ev.description: The reduced penalty for older faces observed in older perceivers is interpreted as an own-age bias, where perceivers are less harsh in their attractiveness assessments of faces from their own age group.
own_age_bias_in_facial_attractiveness_ev.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

flexible_semantic_network_supports_creat.name: flexible_semantic_network_supports_creative_metaphor_production
flexible_semantic_network_supports_creat.description: High creative metaphor producers have more flexible, clustered, and less rigid semantic networks than low metaphor producers, as estimated from verbal fluency responses using computational network science.
flexible_semantic_network_supports_creat.paper: Flexible Semantic Network Structure Supports the Production of Creative Metaphor

creative_metaphor_production_task_as_cre.name: creative_metaphor_production_task_as_creativity_measure
creative_metaphor_production_task_as_cre.description: Participants completed a creative metaphor production task and were divided into two equal groups based on their creative metaphor production score, providing a domain-specific verbal creativity measure.
creative_metaphor_production_task_as_cre.paper: Flexible Semantic Network Structure Supports the Production of Creative Metaphor

verbal_fluency_derived_networks_index_me.name: verbal_fluency_derived_networks_index_metaphor_production_ability
verbal_fluency_derived_networks_index_me.description: Semantic networks estimated from verbal fluency responses differentiate high and low creative metaphor producers, suggesting verbal fluency captures relevant semantic structure for figurative language.
verbal_fluency_derived_networks_index_me.paper: Flexible Semantic Network Structure Supports the Production of Creative Metaphor

extension_of_semantic_network_creativity.name: extension_of_semantic_network_creativity_link_to_metaphor_production
extension_of_semantic_network_creativity.description: The study provides the first evidence that semantic network structure relates to figurative language production, extending prior work on semantic networks and creativity to the metaphor production domain.
extension_of_semantic_network_creativity.paper: Flexible Semantic Network Structure Supports the Production of Creative Metaphor

prior_evidence_for_semantic_network_flex.name: prior_evidence_for_semantic_network_flexibility_in_metaphor_comprehension
prior_evidence_for_semantic_network_flex.description: Previous studies found that higher creative individuals better comprehend novel metaphors, potentially due to more flexible semantic memory network structure conducive to remote conceptual combination.
prior_evidence_for_semantic_network_flex.paper: Flexible Semantic Network Structure Supports the Production of Creative Metaphor

replication_of_network_differences_acros.name: replication_of_network_differences_across_semantic_categories
replication_of_network_differences_acros.description: The differences in semantic network properties between high and low metaphor producers replicated across both semantic categories used in the verbal fluency tasks.
replication_of_network_differences_acros.paper: Flexible Semantic Network Structure Supports the Production of Creative Metaphor

cognitive_basis_of_figurative_language_p.name: cognitive_basis_of_figurative_language_production_poorly_understood
cognitive_basis_of_figurative_language_p.description: Despite metaphors being a common form of creative language, the cognitive mechanisms underlying figurative language production remain poorly understood, motivating the present investigation.
cognitive_basis_of_figurative_language_p.paper: Flexible Semantic Network Structure Supports the Production of Creative Metaphor

knowledge_rich_creative_problem_solving_.name: knowledge_rich_creative_problem_solving_as_understudied_domain
knowledge_rich_creative_problem_solving_.description: Classic creativity paradigms minimize knowledge content, leaving knowledge-rich CPS in real-world novice and expert contexts largely uninvestigated. The authors argue this gap limits the ecological validity of creativity research.
knowledge_rich_creative_problem_solving_.paper: Creative problem solving in knowledge-rich contexts.

creative_analogical_transfer_as_a_key_me.name: creative_analogical_transfer_as_a_key_mechanism_in_knowledge_rich_CPS
creative_analogical_transfer_as_a_key_me.description: The paper argues that analogical transfer of knowledge—applying existing knowledge to new, problem-relevant functions—is a central mechanism of knowledge-rich creative problem solving applicable to real-world contexts.
creative_analogical_transfer_as_a_key_me.paper: Creative problem solving in knowledge-rich contexts.

knowledge_reorganization_as_the_core_cog.name: knowledge_reorganization_as_the_core_cognitive_operation_in_real_world_CPS
knowledge_reorganization_as_the_core_cog.description: The authors propose that real-world CPS fundamentally depends on reorganizing existing knowledge to serve new, problem-relevant functions rather than generating ideas from sparse knowledge.
knowledge_reorganization_as_the_core_cog.paper: Creative problem solving in knowledge-rich contexts.

expansion_of_cps_paradigms_to_knowledge_.name: expansion_of_CPS_paradigms_to_knowledge_rich_contexts
expansion_of_cps_paradigms_to_knowledge_.description: The paper calls for CPS research to broaden its paradigm base to include knowledge-rich problem contexts, in both novice and expert populations within specific domains.
expansion_of_cps_paradigms_to_knowledge_.paper: Creative problem solving in knowledge-rich contexts.

process_level_insights_from_knowledge_ri.name: process_level_insights_from_knowledge_rich_CPS_paradigms
process_level_insights_from_knowledge_ri.description: Paradigms focusing on analogical transfer have begun to reveal cognitive and neural characteristics of knowledge-rich CPS at the process level, pointing to multiple avenues for future inquiry.
process_level_insights_from_knowledge_ri.paper: Creative problem solving in knowledge-rich contexts.

role_of_crystallized_knowledge_in_creati.name: role_of_crystallized_knowledge_in_creativity
role_of_crystallized_knowledge_in_creati.description: The paper explicitly frames the role of crystallized knowledge in creativity as an open question warranting further inquiry, particularly in the context of CPS research.
role_of_crystallized_knowledge_in_creati.paper: Creative problem solving in knowledge-rich contexts.

applicability_of_cps_findings_to_real_wo.name: applicability_of_CPS_findings_to_real_world_problem_solving
applicability_of_cps_findings_to_real_wo.description: The authors argue that knowledge-rich CPS paradigms, especially those involving analogical transfer, reflect skills that are applicable to genuine real-world problem solving.
applicability_of_cps_findings_to_real_wo.paper: Creative problem solving in knowledge-rich contexts.

multiplex_lexical_network_combining_sema.name: multiplex_lexical_network_combining_semantics_and_phonology
multiplex_lexical_network_combining_sema.description: The study constructs a multiplex network representation of the mental lexicon combining 150,000 phonological and semantic associations, going beyond unimodal semantic networks to capture multiple types of conceptual similarity simultaneously.
multiplex_lexical_network_combining_sema.paper: Viability in Multiplex Lexical Networks and Machine Learning Characterizes Human Creativity

viable_cluster_as_core_structure_of_mult.name: viable_cluster_as_core_structure_of_multiplex_mental_lexicon
viable_cluster_as_core_structure_of_mult.description: The multiplex lexical network contains a 'viable cluster'—a kernel of simpler, more general, concrete words acquired early in language learning—identified through percolation or viability analysis of the combined network.
viable_cluster_as_core_structure_of_mult.paper: Viability in Multiplex Lexical Networks and Machine Learning Characterizes Human Creativity

high_creatives_access_viable_cluster_les.name: high_creatives_access_viable_cluster_less_frequently_during_fluency
high_creatives_access_viable_cluster_les.description: During a semantic fluency (animal naming) task, high creative individuals access the viable cluster less frequently than low creative individuals, instead reaching more peripheral words and covering longer multiplex network distances.
high_creatives_access_viable_cluster_les.paper: Viability in Multiplex Lexical Networks and Machine Learning Characterizes Human Creativity

lower_entropy_of_viable_cluster_access_i.name: lower_entropy_of_viable_cluster_access_in_high_creatives
lower_entropy_of_viable_cluster_access_i.description: High creative individuals exhibit lower uncertainty/entropy in their access to the viable cluster compared to low creative individuals, suggesting a more directed, deliberate navigation away from the core lexicon.
lower_entropy_of_viable_cluster_access_i.paper: Viability in Multiplex Lexical Networks and Machine Learning Characterizes Human Creativity

machine_learning_classification_of_creat.name: machine_learning_classification_of_creativity_from_multiplex_network_features
machine_learning_classification_of_creat.description: Using multiplex network navigation features (viable cluster access, entropy, network distances) as inputs, a machine learning classifier distinguishes low and high creative individuals with 65.0±0.9% accuracy and AUC of 68.0±0.8%, both above the 50% random baseline.
machine_learning_classification_of_creat.paper: Viability in Multiplex Lexical Networks and Machine Learning Characterizes Human Creativity

multiplex_network_distance_as_creativity.name: multiplex_network_distance_as_creativity_index
multiplex_network_distance_as_creativity.description: The average multiplex network distance between successively generated concepts during fluency is longer for high creative individuals, making it a useful quantitative index of creative lexical navigation.
multiplex_network_distance_as_creativity.paper: Viability in Multiplex Lexical Networks and Machine Learning Characterizes Human Creativity

semantic_fluency_task_as_window_into_mul.name: semantic_fluency_task_as_window_into_multiplex_lexical_navigation
semantic_fluency_task_as_window_into_mul.description: The study models animal-name generation in a semantic fluency task as navigation on the multiplex lexical network, allowing quantification of within-, outside-, and between-viable-cluster traversals.
semantic_fluency_task_as_window_into_mul.paper: Viability in Multiplex Lexical Networks and Machine Learning Characterizes Human Creativity

combining_psycholinguistic_measures_with.name: combining_psycholinguistic_measures_with_multiplex_networks_for_creativity_modeling
combining_psycholinguistic_measures_with.description: The study aims to demonstrate the potential relevance of integrating psycholinguistic measures with multiplex network models for modeling mental navigation and automatically classifying creativity levels.
combining_psycholinguistic_measures_with.paper: Viability in Multiplex Lexical Networks and Machine Learning Characterizes Human Creativity

individual_differences_in_creativity_ref.name: individual_differences_in_creativity_reflect_differences_in_full_mental_lexicon_not_only_semantics
individual_differences_in_creativity_ref.description: The paper argues that creativity-related individual differences in lexical structure extend beyond semantic memory to encompass the broader mental lexicon including phonological information, motivating the multiplex approach.
individual_differences_in_creativity_ref.paper: Viability in Multiplex Lexical Networks and Machine Learning Characterizes Human Creativity

higher_creatives_faster_at_comprehending.name: higher_creatives_faster_at_comprehending_both_conventional_and_novel_metaphors
higher_creatives_faster_at_comprehending.description: Higher creative individuals demonstrated significantly faster reaction times than lower creative individuals when comprehending both conventional and novel metaphoric word pairs in a semantic relatedness judgment task.
higher_creatives_faster_at_comprehending.paper: Metaphor Comprehension in Low and High Creative Individuals

higher_creatives_more_accurate_only_for_.name: higher_creatives_more_accurate_only_for_novel_metaphors
higher_creatives_more_accurate_only_for_.description: Higher creative individuals were significantly more accurate than lower creative individuals specifically for novel metaphors, but not for conventional metaphors, literal pairs, or meaningless pairs.
higher_creatives_more_accurate_only_for_.paper: Metaphor Comprehension in Low and High Creative Individuals

semantic_relatedness_judgment_task_as_me.name: semantic_relatedness_judgment_task_as_method_for_studying_metaphor_comprehension_across_creativity_levels
semantic_relatedness_judgment_task_as_me.description: Participants judged the semantic relatedness of word pairs spanning novel metaphors, conventional metaphors, literal pairs, and meaningless pairs, enabling measurement of RT and accuracy as indices of comprehension efficiency.
semantic_relatedness_judgment_task_as_me.paper: Metaphor Comprehension in Low and High Creative Individuals

flexible_semantic_memory_facilitates_nov.name: flexible_semantic_memory_facilitates_novel_metaphor_comprehension
flexible_semantic_memory_facilitates_nov.description: More creative individuals possess a more flexible semantic memory structure that allows broader activation of weakly related concepts, thereby facilitating the integration of novel metaphoric word pairs into meaningful expressions.
flexible_semantic_memory_facilitates_nov.paper: Metaphor Comprehension in Low and High Creative Individuals

differential_prediction_for_novel_vs_con.name: differential_prediction_for_novel_vs_conventional_metaphor_comprehension_by_creativity
differential_prediction_for_novel_vs_con.description: The study predicted that creativity-group differences would emerge specifically for novel metaphors due to their requirement for broader semantic associations, while conventional metaphors, literal pairs, and meaningless pairs would show equivalent performance across groups.
differential_prediction_for_novel_vs_con.paper: Metaphor Comprehension in Low and High Creative Individuals

llm_creative_outputs_are_more_homogeneou.name: LLM creative outputs are more homogeneous than human creative outputs
llm_creative_outputs_are_more_homogeneou.description: The paper claims that LLM responses to standardized creativity tests are significantly more similar to other LLM responses than human responses are to each other, even after controlling for response structure and other key variables.
llm_creative_outputs_are_more_homogeneou.paper: We're Different, We're the Same: Creative Homogeneity Across LLMs

cross_llm_creative_homogeneity_as_a_dist.name: Cross-LLM creative homogeneity as a distinct phenomenon from single-LLM narrowing
cross_llm_creative_homogeneity_as_a_dist.description: Prior work showed that interacting with a single LLM narrows creative outputs, but this paper finds that homogeneity is a property shared across LLMs, suggesting the narrowing is not model-specific but a general characteristic of LLM-generated creativity.
cross_llm_creative_homogeneity_as_a_dist.paper: We're Different, We're the Same: Creative Homogeneity Across LLMs

standardized_creativity_tests_as_evaluat.name: Standardized creativity tests as evaluation method for LLM creative diversity
standardized_creativity_tests_as_evaluat.description: The study uses standardized creativity tests to elicit creative responses from both humans and a broad set of LLMs, enabling population-level comparison of response diversity.
standardized_creativity_tests_as_evaluat.paper: We're Different, We're the Same: Creative Homogeneity Across LLMs

llm_use_as_creative_partner_may_drive_us.name: LLM use as creative partner may drive users toward a limited set of creative outputs
llm_use_as_creative_partner_may_drive_us.description: If LLMs across models produce homogeneous creative outputs, widespread adoption of any LLM as a creative assistant may funnel all users toward the same narrow range of ideas regardless of which model they use.
llm_use_as_creative_partner_may_drive_us.paper: We're Different, We're the Same: Creative Homogeneity Across LLMs

population_level_diversity_comparison_as.name: Population-level diversity comparison as method for studying LLM creativity
population_level_diversity_comparison_as.description: Rather than evaluating individual LLM outputs for quality, the paper compares population-level diversity of responses across humans and LLMs, treating response similarity as the key metric of creative range.
population_level_diversity_comparison_as.paper: We're Different, We're the Same: Creative Homogeneity Across LLMs

marketing_of_llms_as_creative_assistants.name: Marketing of LLMs as creative assistants misrepresents their creative range
marketing_of_llms_as_creative_assistants.description: The paper implicitly challenges claims by LLM providers that these models serve as helpful creative assistants, given empirical evidence that they produce homogeneous outputs across models.
marketing_of_llms_as_creative_assistants.paper: We're Different, We're the Same: Creative Homogeneity Across LLMs

dmn_ecn_switching_as_a_neural_predictor_.name: DMN-ECN switching as a neural predictor of creativity
dmn_ecn_switching_as_a_neural_predictor_.description: The study claims that the number of dynamic switches between the Default Mode Network (DMN) and Executive Control Network (ECN) during resting state reliably predicts divergent thinking ability across multiple independent samples.
dmn_ecn_switching_as_a_neural_predictor_.paper: Dynamic switching between brain networks predicts creative ability

inverted_u_relationship_between_dmn_ecn_.name: Inverted-U relationship between DMN-ECN balance and creativity
inverted_u_relationship_between_dmn_ecn_.description: Creativity is not simply maximized by more DMN-ECN switching but shows an inverted-U relationship with the degree of balance between DMN and ECN switching, suggesting an optimal midpoint of network dynamics for peak creative performance.
inverted_u_relationship_between_dmn_ecn_.paper: Dynamic switching between brain networks predicts creative ability

specificity_of_dmn_ecn_switching_to_crea.name: Specificity of DMN-ECN switching to creativity versus intelligence
specificity_of_dmn_ecn_switching_to_crea.description: While DMN-ECN switching predicts creativity (divergent thinking), it does not reliably predict general intelligence, suggesting the dynamic switching mechanism is specifically linked to creative rather than general cognitive ability.
specificity_of_dmn_ecn_switching_to_crea.paper: Dynamic switching between brain networks predicts creative ability

multi_center_meta_analytic_resting_state.name: Multi-center meta-analytic resting-state fMRI approach to creativity neuroscience
multi_center_meta_analytic_resting_state.description: The study aggregates resting-state fMRI and creative task performance data from 10 independent samples (N=2433) across five countries, constituting the largest and most ethnically diverse creativity neuroscience study to date, using a meta-analytic network neuroscience approach.
multi_center_meta_analytic_resting_state.paper: Dynamic switching between brain networks predicts creative ability

task_fmri_validation_of_resting_state_dm.name: Task-fMRI validation of resting-state DMN-ECN switching findings
task_fmri_validation_of_resting_state_dm.description: An independent task-fMRI study (N=31) demonstrates that DMN-ECN switching is elevated during creative idea generation compared to a control condition, and replicates the inverted-U relationship between switching balance and creativity observed in resting-state data.
task_fmri_validation_of_resting_state_dm.paper: Dynamic switching between brain networks predicts creative ability

balanced_spontaneous_and_controlled_cogn.name: Balanced spontaneous and controlled cognition as mechanistic requirement for creativity
balanced_spontaneous_and_controlled_cogn.description: The study proposes that optimal creative performance mechanistically requires a balance between spontaneous (DMN-driven) and controlled (ECN-driven) cognition, operationalized as balanced dynamic switching between the two networks.
balanced_spontaneous_and_controlled_cogn.paper: Dynamic switching between brain networks predicts creative ability

large_scale_ethnically_diverse_creativit.name: Large-scale ethnically diverse creativity neuroscience dataset
large_scale_ethnically_diverse_creativit.description: The study assembles the largest and most ethnically diverse dataset in creativity neuroscience research to date, including 2433 participants from Austria, Canada, China, Japan, and the United States across 10 independent samples.
large_scale_ethnically_diverse_creativit.paper: Dynamic switching between brain networks predicts creative ability

dmn_causal_role_in_divergent_thinking_vi.name: DMN causal role in divergent thinking via direct cortical stimulation
dmn_causal_role_in_divergent_thinking_vi.description: Direct cortical stimulation of DMN regions selectively decreased originality of responses in the alternate uses task without affecting fluency or mind wandering performance, providing causal evidence for DMN involvement in generating original connections among concepts.
dmn_causal_role_in_divergent_thinking_vi.paper: Default mode network electrophysiological dynamics and causal role in creative thinking

task_specific_dissociation_in_dmn_spatio.name: Task-specific dissociation in DMN spatiotemporal dynamics between mind wandering and alternate uses
task_specific_dissociation_in_dmn_spatio.description: The two tasks showed different temporal dynamics within the DMN: the alternate uses task preferentially engaged DMN during the initial stage, while mind wandering engaged DMN more during the later stage, indicating flexible, task-dependent modulation of DMN activity.
task_specific_dissociation_in_dmn_spatio.paper: Default mode network electrophysiological dynamics and causal role in creative thinking

gamma_band_power_increase_and_theta_band.name: Gamma band power increase and theta band power decrease characterize DMN relative to fronto-parietal network during creative tasks
gamma_band_power_increase_and_theta_band.description: Compared to the fronto-parietal network, DMN activity during both mind wandering and alternate uses tasks was characterized by stronger increases in gamma band power (30–70 Hz) coupled with lower theta band power (4–8 Hz), with the difference especially pronounced during mind wandering.
gamma_band_power_increase_and_theta_band.paper: Default mode network electrophysiological dynamics and causal role in creative thinking

lateral_dmn_sites_drive_gamma_power_chan.name: Lateral DMN sites drive gamma power changes while theta shows task-specific spatial patterns
lateral_dmn_sites_drive_gamma_power_chan.description: Within the DMN, gamma power changes were mainly driven by lateral sites, whereas theta power changes showed spatial specificity: alternate uses task theta did not differ across DMN subregions, while mind wandering was associated with early lateral and late dorsomedial DMN engagement.
lateral_dmn_sites_drive_gamma_power_chan.paper: Default mode network electrophysiological dynamics and causal role in creative thinking

stereo_eeg_as_a_high_resolution_method_f.name: Stereo-EEG as a high-resolution method for probing DMN dynamics during creativity tasks
stereo_eeg_as_a_high_resolution_method_f.description: The study employs stereo-EEG recordings in 13 human patients to obtain high spatial and temporal resolution neural recordings across multiple canonical DMN regions during mind wandering and alternate uses tasks, combining direct cortical stimulation for causal inference.
stereo_eeg_as_a_high_resolution_method_f.paper: Default mode network electrophysiological dynamics and causal role in creative thinking

dmn_recruitment_during_both_spontaneous_.name: DMN recruitment during both spontaneous and divergent thought
dmn_recruitment_during_both_spontaneous_.description: The stereo-EEG study found DMN recruitment during both mind wandering (spontaneous thought) and the alternate uses task (divergent thought), supporting the network's general role in internally directed cognition.
dmn_recruitment_during_both_spontaneous_.paper: Default mode network electrophysiological dynamics and causal role in creative thinking

mind_wandering_and_alternate_uses_tasks_.name: Mind wandering and alternate uses tasks as proxies for spontaneous and divergent thought in intracranial research
mind_wandering_and_alternate_uses_tasks_.description: The study operationalizes spontaneous thought via a mind wandering task and divergent thinking via an alternate uses task, enabling direct neural comparison of two creativity-associated cognitive processes within the same intracranial recording session.
mind_wandering_and_alternate_uses_tasks_.paper: Default mode network electrophysiological dynamics and causal role in creative thinking

stimulation_induced_originality_decrease.name: Stimulation-induced originality decrease without fluency change as evidence for dissociable creativity components
stimulation_induced_originality_decrease.description: The finding that cortical stimulation of DMN selectively reduced originality but not fluency suggests that these two components of divergent thinking are at least partially dissociable at the neural level, with DMN specifically supporting the generation of original connections.
stimulation_induced_originality_decrease.paper: Default mode network electrophysiological dynamics and causal role in creative thinking

multilayer_network_model_of_phonological.name: multilayer_network_model_of_phonological_and_semantic_lexical_layers
multilayer_network_model_of_phonological.description: The authors construct a large-scale linguistic multilayer network with separate phonological and semantic layers to model the mental lexicon and study how the two components interact during lexical access.
multilayer_network_model_of_phonological.paper: Unveiling the nature of interaction between semantics and phonology in lexical access based on multilayer networks

high_similarity_between_phonological_and.name: high_similarity_between_phonological_and_semantic_network_links
high_similarity_between_phonological_and.description: The study finds that the edges in the phonological and semantic layers of the multilayer lexical network are highly similar to each other, suggesting structural overlap between the two representational systems.
high_similarity_between_phonological_and.paper: Unveiling the nature of interaction between semantics and phonology in lexical access based on multilayer networks

cross_layer_information_increases_lexica.name: cross_layer_information_increases_lexical_network_efficiency
cross_layer_information_increases_lexica.description: Adding information from one layer (phonological or semantic) to the other decreases overall network distances, thereby increasing efficiency of lexical access in the combined network.
cross_layer_information_increases_lexica.paper: Unveiling the nature of interaction between semantics and phonology in lexical access based on multilayer networks

cross_layer_efficiency_gain_concentrated.name: cross_layer_efficiency_gain_concentrated_in_shorter_distances
cross_layer_efficiency_gain_concentrated.description: The efficiency benefit of combining phonological and semantic layers specifically affects shorter distances in the network rather than being uniformly distributed across all path lengths.
cross_layer_efficiency_gain_concentrated.paper: Unveiling the nature of interaction between semantics and phonology in lexical access based on multilayer networks

multilayer_architecture_demonstrates_hig.name: multilayer_architecture_demonstrates_highest_lexical_network_efficiency
multilayer_architecture_demonstrates_hig.description: The full multilayer network architecture achieves higher overall efficiency than either single-layer network alone, supporting its utility as a model of integrated lexical access.
multilayer_architecture_demonstrates_hig.paper: Unveiling the nature of interaction between semantics and phonology in lexical access based on multilayer networks

multilayer_efficiency_linked_to_weak_sem.name: multilayer_efficiency_linked_to_weak_semantic_relations_in_cue_words
multilayer_efficiency_linked_to_weak_sem.description: The efficiency gains provided by the multilayer architecture are specifically related to weak semantic relations between cue words in the network, suggesting that cross-layer links are especially beneficial for semantically distant word pairs.
multilayer_efficiency_linked_to_weak_sem.paper: Unveiling the nature of interaction between semantics and phonology in lexical access based on multilayer networks

multilayer_network_science_as_tool_for_q.name: multilayer_network_science_as_tool_for_quantifying_lexical_access_models
multilayer_network_science_as_tool_for_q.description: The paper demonstrates that multilayer network science tools can quantify theoretical cognitive models of lexical access by operationalizing the interaction between phonological and semantic components.
multilayer_network_science_as_tool_for_q.paper: Unveiling the nature of interaction between semantics and phonology in lexical access based on multilayer networks

debate_over_phonological_semantic_intera.name: debate_over_phonological_semantic_interaction_in_lexical_access
debate_over_phonological_semantic_intera.description: It remains widely debated whether and how phonological and semantic components of lexical access relate to each other, motivating the need for quantitative frameworks to resolve the question.
debate_over_phonological_semantic_intera.paper: Unveiling the nature of interaction between semantics and phonology in lexical access based on multilayer networks

cognitive_psychology_evaluation_of_llms_.name: cognitive_psychology_evaluation_of_llms_must_extend_beyond_human_bias_frameworks
cognitive_psychology_evaluation_of_llms_.description: The paper argues that evaluating LLMs using cognitive psychology tasks should not be limited to detecting or mapping human-like biases; a broader set of psychological frameworks is needed to understand LLM behavior.
cognitive_psychology_evaluation_of_llms_.paper: Using cognitive psychology to understand GPT-like models needs to extend beyond human biases

cognitive_task_based_evaluation_as_drive.name: cognitive_task_based_evaluation_as_driver_of_psychological_frameworks_for_llms
cognitive_task_based_evaluation_as_drive.description: Evaluation of LLMs via cognitive tasks has propelled the adoption of psychological frameworks for studying these models, establishing cognitive psychology as a key lens for LLM research.
cognitive_task_based_evaluation_as_drive.paper: Using cognitive psychology to understand GPT-like models needs to extend beyond human biases

multilingual_semantic_distance_validatio.name: Multilingual semantic distance validation on AUT across 12 languages
multilingual_semantic_distance_validatio.description: A multilab study (N=6,522 participants, N=107,672 responses) validated automatic semantic distance scoring of the Alternate Uses Task in 12 languages (Arabic, Chinese, Dutch, English, Farsi, French, German, Hebrew, Italian, Polish, Russian, Spanish) using deep learning models compared against human creativity ratings and criterion measures.
multilingual_semantic_distance_validatio.paper: Multilingual semantic distance: Automatic verbal creativity assessment in many languages.

mbert_and_xlm_roberta_as_multilingual_se.name: mBERT and XLM-RoBERTa as multilingual semantic distance models
mbert_and_xlm_roberta_as_multilingual_se.description: The study compared two deep learning-based distributional semantic models—multilingual BERT (mBERT) and cross-lingual language model RoBERTa (XLM-RoBERTa)—to compute semantic distance for automatic creativity scoring across multiple languages.
mbert_and_xlm_roberta_as_multilingual_se.paper: Multilingual semantic distance: Automatic verbal creativity assessment in many languages.

semantic_distance_correlates_medium_to_l.name: Semantic distance correlates medium-to-large with human creativity ratings across languages
semantic_distance_correlates_medium_to_l.description: The top-performing deep learning model for each language yielded positive correlations with human creativity ratings, with correlation magnitudes ranging from medium to large across the 12 languages tested.
semantic_distance_correlates_medium_to_l.paper: Multilingual semantic distance: Automatic verbal creativity assessment in many languages.

semantic_distance_criterion_validity_com.name: Semantic distance criterion validity comparable to human ratings across languages
semantic_distance_criterion_validity_com.description: Semantic distance scores showed small-to-moderate effect sizes for criterion measures including openness to experience, creative behavior/achievement, and creative self-concept, comparable in magnitude to those obtained with human creativity ratings.
semantic_distance_criterion_validity_com.paper: Multilingual semantic distance: Automatic verbal creativity assessment in many languages.

labor_intensiveness_and_subjectivity_of_.name: Labor-intensiveness and subjectivity of manual creativity scoring as limitation
labor_intensiveness_and_subjectivity_of_.description: The paper identifies that traditional human-rater scoring of divergent thinking tasks like the AUT is labor-intensive and subjective, which can adversely affect reliability and validity of creativity assessments.
labor_intensiveness_and_subjectivity_of_.paper: Multilingual semantic distance: Automatic verbal creativity assessment in many languages.

english_language_bias_in_semantic_distan.name: English-language bias in semantic distance creativity research
english_language_bias_in_semantic_distan.description: Prior to this study, semantic distance as an automatic creativity scoring approach had been studied almost exclusively in English-speaking samples, leaving most of the world's languages unaddressed.
english_language_bias_in_semantic_distan.paper: Multilingual semantic distance: Automatic verbal creativity assessment in many languages.

open_access_multilingual_aut_dataset_and.name: Open-access multilingual AUT dataset and Python scoring code
open_access_multilingual_aut_dataset_and.description: The authors provide public access to the multilingual AUT dataset (12 languages, N=6,522, N=107,672 responses) and Python code for computing semantic distance, supporting future algorithmic development.
open_access_multilingual_aut_dataset_and.paper: Multilingual semantic distance: Automatic verbal creativity assessment in many languages.

mesoscale_brain_network_features_relate_.name: mesoscale_brain_network_features_relate_to_creative_thinking
mesoscale_brain_network_features_relate_.description: Greater capacity for creative thinking was related to increased and decreased co-assignment of medial-temporal and subcortical regions to the same community, respectively, suggesting that creative capacity is reflected in inter-individual differences in mesoscale (community and hub) organization of resting-state brain networks.
mesoscale_brain_network_features_relate_.paper: Community structure of the creative brain at rest

dmn_hub_organization_positively_predicts.name: dmn_hub_organization_positively_predicts_creative_ability
dmn_hub_organization_positively_predicts.description: Increased hubness (participation coefficient) of DMN regions was positively related to creative ability, indicating that DMN nodes serving as bridges across community boundaries support individual differences in creative thinking.
dmn_hub_organization_positively_predicts.paper: Community structure of the creative brain at rest

medial_temporal_hub_organization_negativ.name: medial_temporal_hub_organization_negatively_predicts_creative_ability
medial_temporal_hub_organization_negativ.description: Increased hubness of medial-temporal regions was negatively related to creative ability, suggesting that medial-temporal nodes acting as strong between-community bridges may constrain rather than support creativity.
medial_temporal_hub_organization_negativ.paper: Community structure of the creative brain at rest

community_co_assignment_probability_as_m.name: community_co_assignment_probability_as_method_for_individual_level_mesoscale_analysis
community_co_assignment_probability_as_m.description: The study computed for every participant the co-assignment probability of brain regions to the same community using a data-driven approach applied to resting-state fMRI, enabling person-specific characterization of mesoscale network organization.
community_co_assignment_probability_as_m.paper: Community structure of the creative brain at rest

participation_coefficient_as_hub_metric_.name: participation_coefficient_as_hub_metric_for_creativity_research
participation_coefficient_as_hub_metric_.description: Participant-specific communities were used to identify network hubs quantified by participation coefficients, measuring nodes whose connections bridge across community boundaries, and these were linked to individual differences in creative ability.
participation_coefficient_as_hub_metric_.paper: Community structure of the creative brain at rest

gap_in_mesoscale_network_understanding_o.name: gap_in_mesoscale_network_understanding_of_creativity
gap_in_mesoscale_network_understanding_o.description: Prior studies of creativity and brain networks focused on local (regional) and global levels, leaving mesoscale features such as community and hub organization underexplored in relation to individual differences in creative thinking.
gap_in_mesoscale_network_understanding_o.paper: Community structure of the creative brain at rest

resting_state_fmri_large_sample_design_f.name: resting_state_fmri_large_sample_design_for_creativity_and_mesoscale_networks
resting_state_fmri_large_sample_design_f.description: The study used resting-state functional imaging data from a large sample of participants combined with individual differences measures of creative thinking to examine mesoscale community and hub structure.
resting_state_fmri_large_sample_design_f.paper: Community structure of the creative brain at rest

overlap_between_question_asking_and_prom.name: Overlap between question-asking and prompt engineering
overlap_between_question_asking_and_prom.description: The paper argues that question-asking and prompt engineering share a common foundation in creativity, critical thinking, and cognitive flexibility, forming a symbiotic relationship despite appearing distinct.
overlap_between_question_asking_and_prom.paper: The Art of Creative Inquiry—From Question Asking to Prompt Engineering

bloom_s_taxonomy_as_a_framework_for_prom.name: Bloom's taxonomy as a framework for prompt complexity
bloom_s_taxonomy_as_a_framework_for_prom.description: The paper proposes using Bloom's taxonomy levels of cognitive complexity to guide prompt design, suggesting that higher-order question types yield more engaging and efficient prompts.
bloom_s_taxonomy_as_a_framework_for_prom.paper: The Art of Creative Inquiry—From Question Asking to Prompt Engineering

creativity_as_dynamic_driver_of_both_que.name: Creativity as dynamic driver of both question-asking and prompt engineering
creativity_as_dynamic_driver_of_both_que.description: The paper contends that creativity plays a dynamic and central role in both formulating questions and crafting prompts for language models, shaping the quality of human–machine interactions.
creativity_as_dynamic_driver_of_both_que.paper: The Art of Creative Inquiry—From Question Asking to Prompt Engineering

prompt_engineering_as_a_form_of_creative.name: Prompt engineering as a form of creative inquiry
prompt_engineering_as_a_form_of_creative.description: The paper theorizes that crafting cues for language models is an art requiring the same inquiry skills as asking effective questions, positioning prompt engineering within the broader cognitive framework of creative inquiry.
prompt_engineering_as_a_form_of_creative.paper: The Art of Creative Inquiry—From Question Asking to Prompt Engineering

strategies_for_efficient_and_engaging_pr.name: Strategies for efficient and engaging prompt design
strategies_for_efficient_and_engaging_pr.description: The paper proposes concrete strategies for prompt design that go beyond efficiency to emphasize engagement, drawing on diverse question types and complexity levels.
strategies_for_efficient_and_engaging_pr.paper: The Art of Creative Inquiry—From Question Asking to Prompt Engineering

human_machine_interaction_as_a_context_f.name: Human–machine interaction as a context for question-asking and prompt engineering convergence
human_machine_interaction_as_a_context_f.description: The paper aims to advance understanding of human–machine interactions by highlighting how prompt engineering and question-asking skills jointly shape communication with language models.
human_machine_interaction_as_a_context_f.paper: The Art of Creative Inquiry—From Question Asking to Prompt Engineering

cognitive_flexibility_as_a_shared_requir.name: Cognitive flexibility as a shared requirement for question-asking and prompt engineering
cognitive_flexibility_as_a_shared_requir.description: The paper identifies cognitive flexibility as one of the essential shared skills underlying both question-asking and prompt engineering, suggesting it moderates success in both activities.
cognitive_flexibility_as_a_shared_requir.paper: The Art of Creative Inquiry—From Question Asking to Prompt Engineering

neurally_informed_ontology_of_creativity.name: neurally_informed_ontology_of_creativity_measurement
neurally_informed_ontology_of_creativity.description: The paper aims to establish a mapping between creativity constructs and measures by leveraging neuroimaging meta-analysis and representational similarity analysis to identify optimal task sets that capture creative abilities.
neurally_informed_ontology_of_creativity.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

inconsistent_measurement_as_barrier_to_c.name: inconsistent_measurement_as_barrier_to_creativity_science
inconsistent_measurement_as_barrier_to_c.description: Different researchers use different measures for the same constructs, hindering cumulative understanding of cognitive and neural components of creativity.
inconsistent_measurement_as_barrier_to_c.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

construct_measure_mapping_as_central_cha.name: construct_measure_mapping_as_central_challenge_in_creativity_research
construct_measure_mapping_as_central_cha.description: Identifying which tasks best capture which creative abilities is framed as a foundational challenge for creativity research and cognitive neuroscience more broadly.
construct_measure_mapping_as_central_cha.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

neurosynth_meta_analytic_maps_for_creati.name: neurosynth_meta_analytic_maps_for_creativity_constructs
neurosynth_meta_analytic_maps_for_creati.description: The study uses NeuroSynth meta-analytic software to generate neural activity maps associated with tasks intended to measure specific creativity-relevant constructs, providing a data-driven basis for construct similarity.
neurosynth_meta_analytic_maps_for_creati.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

researcher_survey_as_basis_for_creativit.name: researcher_survey_as_basis_for_creativity_construct_similarity_model
researcher_survey_as_basis_for_creativit.description: Creativity researchers were surveyed to build a model of similarity between creativity-relevant constructs, providing a behaviorally grounded ground-truth for the neuroimaging-based RSA.
researcher_survey_as_basis_for_creativit.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

rsa_based_approach_for_identifying_creat.name: rsa_based_approach_for_identifying_creativity_task_construct_fit
rsa_based_approach_for_identifying_creat.description: A representational similarity analysis (RSA)-based approach was applied to identify which constructs and tasks best or least fit the similarity model of creativity, pointing toward optimal task selection.
rsa_based_approach_for_identifying_creat.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

proof_of_concept_for_neurally_informed_c.name: proof_of_concept_for_neurally_informed_creativity_ontology
proof_of_concept_for_neurally_informed_c.description: The paper demonstrates initial proof-of-concept that neural meta-analytic RSA can help identify optimal subsets of tasks reflecting creativity construct similarity structure.
proof_of_concept_for_neurally_informed_c.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

long_term_potential_to_advance_creativit.name: long_term_potential_to_advance_creativity_ontology_via_neuroimaging_growth
long_term_potential_to_advance_creativit.description: The authors claim that the approach has long-term potential to meaningfully advance the ontological development of creativity research as the neuroimaging data base grows.
long_term_potential_to_advance_creativit.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

near_term_applicability_to_fields_with_e.name: near_term_applicability_to_fields_with_extensive_neuroimaging_data
near_term_applicability_to_fields_with_e.description: Because the approach relies on neuroimaging meta-analysis, it has more immediate applicability to longer-established fields with larger existing neuroimaging datasets than creativity research currently has.
near_term_applicability_to_fields_with_e.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

meta_analytic_structure_in_neural_activi.name: meta_analytic_structure_in_neural_activity_as_guide_for_task_selection
meta_analytic_structure_in_neural_activi.description: The paper contributes the idea that identifying meta-analytic similarity structure in neural activity across creativity tasks can inform which tasks best measure which creative abilities.
meta_analytic_structure_in_neural_activi.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

psychometric_network_analysis_of_creativ.name: psychometric_network_analysis_of_creative_personality_structure
psychometric_network_analysis_of_creativ.description: The study applied psychometric network analysis to characterize multidimensional personality structure in highly creative artists, scientists, and a control group across three samples (N=4,015), including college students, a representative adult sample, and eminent creative professionals from the Big-C project.
psychometric_network_analysis_of_creativ.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

artists_show_higher_openness_to_experien.name: artists_show_higher_openness_to_experience_than_scientists_and_controls
artists_show_higher_openness_to_experien.description: Replicating prior univariate work, the study found that artists reliably showed higher levels of openness to experience compared to scientists and a control group of less creative individuals across all three samples.
artists_show_higher_openness_to_experien.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

openness_more_heterogeneous_in_artists_v.name: openness_more_heterogeneous_in_artists_via_higher_network_connectivity
openness_more_heterogeneous_in_artists_v.description: Psychometric network analysis revealed that artists showed higher connectivity (co-occurrence) of openness to experience with other personality traits, indicating greater heterogeneity in how openness manifests in highly creative people in the arts.
openness_more_heterogeneous_in_artists_v.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

scientists_personality_network_more_cohe.name: scientists_personality_network_more_cohesive_than_artists_and_controls
scientists_personality_network_more_cohe.description: Across all three samples, the personality network structure of scientists was more cohesive than that of artists and the control group, indicating greater homogeneity in the personality characteristics of scientists.
scientists_personality_network_more_cohe.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

constellation_of_traits_differentiates_a.name: constellation_of_traits_differentiates_arts_and_sciences_creative_achievement
constellation_of_traits_differentiates_a.description: The study uncovered a multidimensional constellation of personality traits that give rise to creative achievement in the arts and sciences, revealing patterns not visible through univariate analyses alone.
constellation_of_traits_differentiates_a.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

limitation_of_univariate_personality_cre.name: limitation_of_univariate_personality_creativity_research
limitation_of_univariate_personality_cre.description: Prior work on the creative personality has largely focused on univariate associations between personality traits and creativity, potentially obscuring complex interactions among multiple personality factors and yielding an incomplete picture.
limitation_of_univariate_personality_cre.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

replication_of_personality_network_findi.name: replication_of_personality_network_findings_across_three_independent_samples
replication_of_personality_network_findi.description: Key findings regarding openness heterogeneity in artists and network cohesion in scientists were replicated across three independent samples: college students, a representative adult sample, and eminent creative professionals from the Big-C project.
replication_of_personality_network_findi.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

big_c_project_eminent_professionals_as_c.name: big_c_project_eminent_professionals_as_creativity_sample
big_c_project_eminent_professionals_as_c.description: The study used data from the Big-C project, which includes eminent creative professionals, as one of three samples to ensure findings generalize beyond student and representative adult populations to highly creative real-world achievers.
big_c_project_eminent_professionals_as_c.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

gips_do_not_predict_subjective_preferenc.name: GIPs do not predict subjective preference for abstract art paintings
gips_do_not_predict_subjective_preferenc.description: The study replicated prior observations that global image properties (GIPs) fail to relate to preference ratings specifically for abstract art paintings, contrasting with other categories of visual stimuli where GIPs do predict preference.
gips_do_not_predict_subjective_preferenc.paper: Abstract art paintings, global image properties, and verbal descriptions: An empirical and computational investigation.

semantic_verbal_description_network_pred.name: Semantic verbal-description network predicts preference for abstract art
semantic_verbal_description_network_pred.description: A network constructed from verbal descriptions of abstract art paintings predicted subjective preference ratings, whereas the GIP-based network did not, highlighting the importance of semantic content over low-level image properties.
semantic_verbal_description_network_pred.paper: Abstract art paintings, global image properties, and verbal descriptions: An empirical and computational investigation.

semantic_verbal_description_network_pred.name: Semantic verbal-description network predicts art style affiliation of abstract paintings
semantic_verbal_description_network_pred.description: Network measures computed from verbal descriptions, but not from GIPs, correctly classified abstract art paintings into their specific art styles, demonstrating that semantic descriptors carry style-relevant information that low-level properties do not.
semantic_verbal_description_network_pred.paper: Abstract art paintings, global image properties, and verbal descriptions: An empirical and computational investigation.

two_network_approach_verbal_description_.name: Two-network approach: verbal-description network vs. GIP network for abstract art
two_network_approach_verbal_description_.description: The study estimated two distinct network structures for the same set of abstract art paintings—one derived from participants' verbal descriptions and one from computational global image properties—and compared their predictive validity for preference and style.
two_network_approach_verbal_description_.paper: Abstract art paintings, global image properties, and verbal descriptions: An empirical and computational investigation.

abstract_art_paintings_should_not_be_tre.name: Abstract art paintings should not be treated as a single homogeneous category
abstract_art_paintings_should_not_be_tre.description: Preference ratings and GIPs differed for sub-groups of abstract art paintings, indicating that empirical aesthetics research should distinguish among sub-categories rather than treating all abstract art as equivalent.
abstract_art_paintings_should_not_be_tre.paper: Abstract art paintings, global image properties, and verbal descriptions: An empirical and computational investigation.

verbal_descriptors_are_important_for_eva.name: Verbal descriptors are important for evaluating abstract art empirically
verbal_descriptors_are_important_for_eva.description: The study argues that verbal descriptions capture meaningful variance in how people respond to abstract art, making them a necessary component of empirical aesthetics investigations of this stimulus class.
