verbal_descriptors_are_important_for_eva.paper: Abstract art paintings, global image properties, and verbal descriptions: An empirical and computational investigation.

computational_network_science_applied_to.name: Computational network science applied to empirical aesthetics of abstract art
computational_network_science_applied_to.description: The paper introduces a computational network science framework to empirical aesthetics, constructing and comparing image-property and verbal-description networks to understand subjective responses to abstract art paintings.
computational_network_science_applied_to.paper: Abstract art paintings, global image properties, and verbal descriptions: An empirical and computational investigation.

gip_based_network_does_not_predict_prefe.name: GIP-based network does not predict preference or art style for abstract art
gip_based_network_does_not_predict_prefe.description: Network measures derived from global image properties failed to predict both subjective preference ratings and art style affiliation, contrasting with the semantic network's predictive success.
gip_based_network_does_not_predict_prefe.paper: Abstract art paintings, global image properties, and verbal descriptions: An empirical and computational investigation.

semantic_dementia_impairs_divergent_thin.name: semantic_dementia_impairs_divergent_thinking_disproportionately
semantic_dementia_impairs_divergent_thin.description: SD patients generated significantly fewer divergent thinking responses than both bvFTD patients and healthy controls, with disproportionate impairment in SD relative to bvFTD, highlighting the pivotal role of semantic memory in creative cognition.
semantic_dementia_impairs_divergent_thin.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

semantic_dementia_impairs_originality_an.name: semantic_dementia_impairs_originality_and_flexibility_of_divergent_thinking
semantic_dementia_impairs_originality_an.description: Both SD and bvFTD patient groups produced less original and less flexible responses on the divergent thinking task compared with healthy controls, indicating that conceptual knowledge degradation undermines the quality, not only quantity, of creative output.
semantic_dementia_impairs_originality_an.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

fluency_in_sd_correlates_with_semantic_a.name: fluency_in_SD_correlates_with_semantic_association_ability
fluency_in_sd_correlates_with_semantic_a.description: In SD patients specifically, fluency of divergent thinking responses correlated with performance on a measure of semantic association, suggesting semantic memory integrity directly supports the quantity of ideas generated.
fluency_in_sd_correlates_with_semantic_a.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

originality_in_sd_correlates_with_semant.name: originality_in_SD_correlates_with_semantic_naming_and_comprehension
originality_in_sd_correlates_with_semant.description: In SD patients, originality of divergent thinking responses correlated with semantic naming and comprehension ability, indicating that the richness and integrity of the conceptual knowledge base shapes the quality of creative ideas.
originality_in_sd_correlates_with_semant.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

originality_in_bvftd_correlates_with_let.name: originality_in_bvFTD_correlates_with_letter_fluency_and_response_inhibition
originality_in_bvftd_correlates_with_let.description: In bvFTD patients, originality of ideas correlated with letter fluency and response inhibition, implicating prefrontal executive processes rather than semantic memory as the primary driver of creative quality in this group.
originality_in_bvftd_correlates_with_let.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

left_anterior_hippocampus_grey_matter_as.name: left_anterior_hippocampus_grey_matter_associated_with_divergent_thinking_fluency
left_anterior_hippocampus_grey_matter_as.description: VBM analysis identified a left medial temporal lobe cluster centred on the left anterior hippocampus as consistently associated with diminished fluency of divergent thinking ideas, pointing to an essential role for this region.
left_anterior_hippocampus_grey_matter_as.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

left_middle_frontal_gyrus_grey_matter_as.name: left_middle_frontal_gyrus_grey_matter_associated_with_divergent_thinking_fluency
left_middle_frontal_gyrus_grey_matter_as.description: VBM analysis identified a left middle frontal gyrus cluster as the second region consistently associated with diminished fluency of ideas, underscoring a prefrontal contribution to creative cognition alongside temporal regions.
left_middle_frontal_gyrus_grey_matter_as.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

lesion_study_approach_reveals_regions_es.name: lesion_study_approach_reveals_regions_essential_for_divergent_thinking
lesion_study_approach_reveals_regions_es.description: This study uses a lesion-based approach contrasting SD, bvFTD, and healthy controls to reveal brain regions not merely implicated in but causally necessary for divergent thinking, providing converging evidence beyond neuroimaging correlations.
lesion_study_approach_reveals_regions_es.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

distinct_temporal_and_prefrontal_contrib.name: distinct_temporal_and_prefrontal_contributions_to_divergent_thinking
distinct_temporal_and_prefrontal_contrib.description: The study reveals a dissociation between temporal lobe semantic processes (critical in SD) and prefrontal executive processes (critical in bvFTD) as distinct but both necessary contributions to divergent thinking.
distinct_temporal_and_prefrontal_contrib.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

semantic_dementia_as_model_for_studying_.name: semantic_dementia_as_model_for_studying_semantic_memory_role_in_creativity
semantic_dementia_as_model_for_studying_.description: SD, characterised by progressive degeneration of the conceptual knowledge base, is used as a neurological model to probe the necessity of semantic memory integrity for divergent thinking, complementing neuroimaging approaches.
semantic_dementia_as_model_for_studying_.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

bvftd_as_frontal_executive_control_compa.name: bvFTD_as_frontal_executive_control_comparison_group_for_divergent_thinking
bvftd_as_frontal_executive_control_compa.description: Patients with bvFTD serve as a clinical control group with frontal executive dysfunction but relatively preserved semantic memory, enabling dissociation of semantic versus executive contributions to creative thinking.
bvftd_as_frontal_executive_control_compa.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

structural_mri_vbm_as_method_for_localiz.name: structural_mri_vbm_as_method_for_localizing_creativity_relevant_grey_matter
structural_mri_vbm_as_method_for_localiz.description: All participants underwent structural MRI with voxel-based morphometry analysis to identify grey matter clusters associated with diminished creative fluency, providing anatomical grounding for behavioral findings.
structural_mri_vbm_as_method_for_localiz.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

novel_conceptual_combination_reconfigure.name: Novel conceptual combination reconfigures semantic memory networks
novel_conceptual_combination_reconfigure.description: Interpreting unfamiliar nominal compounds can dynamically change the relational structure of semantic memory networks, as measured by network science tools comparing network properties before and after compound interpretation.
novel_conceptual_combination_reconfigure.paper: Novel conceptual combination can dynamically reconfigure semantic memory networks

relational_but_not_attributive_compounds.name: Relational but not attributive compounds increase semantic network connectivity
relational_but_not_attributive_compounds.description: Relational conceptual combinations—where a relation links two concepts—increased connectivity and lowered structure in participants' semantic networks, whereas attributive combinations—where an attribute of one concept describes another—did not produce this effect.
relational_but_not_attributive_compounds.paper: Novel conceptual combination can dynamically reconfigure semantic memory networks

relational_compound_interpretation_requi.name: Relational compound interpretation requires novel context generation
relational_compound_interpretation_requi.description: The authors suggest that constructing relational interpretations requires generating novel contexts that link the two concepts, which is the mechanism driving greater semantic network restructuring compared to attributive combinations.
relational_compound_interpretation_requi.paper: Novel conceptual combination can dynamically reconfigure semantic memory networks

distinction_between_attributive_and_rela.name: Distinction between attributive and relational nominal compound interpretation
distinction_between_attributive_and_rela.description: The study contrasts two types of nominal compound processing: attributive (one concept's attribute describes the other) versus relational (a relation is identified to link two concepts), using this distinction to examine differential effects on semantic memory network structure.
distinction_between_attributive_and_rela.paper: Novel conceptual combination can dynamically reconfigure semantic memory networks

network_science_tools_characterize_dynam.name: Network science tools characterize dynamic change in semantic networks
network_science_tools_characterize_dynam.description: The study employs network science tools to measure semantic network properties such as connectivity before and after a conceptual combination task, enabling quantification of dynamic reconfiguration.
network_science_tools_characterize_dynam.paper: Novel conceptual combination can dynamically reconfigure semantic memory networks

conceptual_combination_as_a_driver_of_se.name: Conceptual combination as a driver of semantic memory restructuring
conceptual_combination_as_a_driver_of_se.description: The paper advances understanding of the dynamic, use-dependent nature of semantic memory by showing that active conceptual combination—especially relational—restructures the associative network, extending beyond static accounts of concept representation.
conceptual_combination_as_a_driver_of_se.paper: Novel conceptual combination can dynamically reconfigure semantic memory networks

novel_embodied_coding_scheme_for_diverge.name: Novel embodied coding scheme for divergent thinking responses
novel_embodied_coding_scheme_for_diverge.description: The authors devised a new coding scheme for divergent thinking task responses that captures dimensions of simulated action or body states, grounded in embodied cognition and neurocognitive research frameworks.
novel_embodied_coding_scheme_for_diverge.paper: A novel coding scheme for assessing responses in divergent thinking: An embodied approach

reliable_coding_of_embodied_dimensions_b.name: Reliable coding of embodied dimensions by naïve raters
reliable_coding_of_embodied_dimensions_b.description: The study demonstrates that the novel embodied dimensions can be coded reliably by raters without specialist training, supporting the scheme's practical utility.
reliable_coding_of_embodied_dimensions_b.paper: A novel coding scheme for assessing responses in divergent thinking: An embodied approach

individual_differences_in_creativity_mod.name: Individual differences in creativity modulate embodied strategy use over time
individual_differences_in_creativity_mod.description: The study finds that creativity level influences how individuals deploy different embodied dimensions across the temporal course of divergent thinking response generation.
individual_differences_in_creativity_mod.paper: A novel coding scheme for assessing responses in divergent thinking: An embodied approach

embodied_perspective_as_a_framework_for_.name: Embodied perspective as a framework for divergent thinking analysis
embodied_perspective_as_a_framework_for_.description: The authors argue that considerations from embodied cognition—specifically simulations of action and the body—provide a theoretically motivated basis for coding and understanding creative responses in divergent thinking tasks.
embodied_perspective_as_a_framework_for_.paper: A novel coding scheme for assessing responses in divergent thinking: An embodied approach

characterization_of_cognitive_strategies.name: Characterization of cognitive strategies in creative endeavors via embodied coding
characterization_of_cognitive_strategies.description: A stated aim of the work is for the embodied coding scheme to serve as a tool for characterizing the cognitive strategies people use during creative tasks, enabling future process-level research.
characterization_of_cognitive_strategies.paper: A novel coding scheme for assessing responses in divergent thinking: An embodied approach

exploratory_application_of_embodied_codi.name: Exploratory application of embodied coding to an existing divergent thinking dataset
exploratory_application_of_embodied_codi.description: The authors apply their coding scheme to responses from a previously published divergent thinking dataset, providing a proof-of-concept demonstration of the scheme's applicability.
exploratory_application_of_embodied_codi.paper: A novel coding scheme for assessing responses in divergent thinking: An embodied approach

new_hypotheses_about_creative_response_g.name: New hypotheses about creative response generation from embodied analysis
new_hypotheses_about_creative_response_g.description: The exploratory results are presented as generating novel, testable hypotheses about the mechanisms underlying creative response generation in divergent thinking, extending beyond existing associative and semantic accounts.
new_hypotheses_about_creative_response_g.paper: A novel coding scheme for assessing responses in divergent thinking: An embodied approach

cognitive_multilayer_networks_as_framewo.name: cognitive_multilayer_networks_as_framework_for_mental_lexicon
cognitive_multilayer_networks_as_framewo.description: The paper proposes cognitive multilayer networks as a quantitative and interpretative framework for mapping multiple types of conceptual associations (semantic, phonological, syntactic, etc.) within the mental lexicon simultaneously, capturing how different layers co-exist and influence cognitive processing.
cognitive_multilayer_networks_as_framewo.paper: Cognitive modelling of concepts in the mental lexicon with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements, and future challenges

multiplex_viability_highlights_language_.name: multiplex_viability_highlights_language_kernels_and_facilitative_effects
multiplex_viability_highlights_language_.description: Multiplex viability analysis reveals language kernels—core structures of the multilayer mental lexicon—and facilitative effects of knowledge processing that are observable only when combining multiple lexical layers, including in healthy and clinical populations.
multiplex_viability_highlights_language_.paper: Cognitive modelling of concepts in the mental lexicon with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements, and future challenges

multilayer_community_detection_enables_c.name: multilayer_community_detection_enables_contextual_meaning_reconstruction
multilayer_community_detection_enables_c.description: Community detection applied to multilayer lexical networks enables the reconstruction of contextual word meaning depending on psycholinguistic features, a capability not afforded by single-layer approaches.
multilayer_community_detection_enables_c.paper: Cognitive modelling of concepts in the mental lexicon with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements, and future challenges

layer_mediation_suppression_and_facilita.name: layer_mediation_suppression_and_facilitation_in_lexical_access
layer_mediation_suppression_and_facilita.description: Analysis of individual layers within a multilayer lexical network can reveal latent mediation, suppression, and facilitation interactions between lexical layers that are invisible in single-layer models and that influence lexical access.
layer_mediation_suppression_and_facilita.paper: Cognitive modelling of concepts in the mental lexicon with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements, and future challenges

mental_lexicon_as_complex_multilayer_cog.name: mental_lexicon_as_complex_multilayer_cognitive_system
mental_lexicon_as_complex_multilayer_cog.description: The paper frames the mental lexicon as a complex cognitive system in which conceptual associations span multiple interactive cognitive levels, necessitating a multilayer rather than single-layer network representation.
mental_lexicon_as_complex_multilayer_cog.paper: Cognitive modelling of concepts in the mental lexicon with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements, and future challenges

future_challenges_and_directions_for_mul.name: future_challenges_and_directions_for_multilayer_mental_lexicon_research
future_challenges_and_directions_for_mul.description: The review outlines key limitations of current multilayer lexical network approaches and identifies promising directions including integration into next-generation brain/mind models.
future_challenges_and_directions_for_mul.paper: Cognitive modelling of concepts in the mental lexicon with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements, and future challenges

multilayer_network_formalism_introductio.name: multilayer_network_formalism_introduction_for_cognitive_science
multilayer_network_formalism_introductio.description: The paper provides a gentle introduction to the structure and formalism of multilayer networks aimed at cognitive scientists, lowering the barrier to adopting this mathematical framework for studying the mental lexicon.
multilayer_network_formalism_introductio.paper: Cognitive modelling of concepts in the mental lexicon with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements, and future challenges

single_layer_networks_insufficient_for_c.name: single_layer_networks_insufficient_for_capturing_mental_lexicon_complexity
single_layer_networks_insufficient_for_c.description: The review argues that single-layer network models of the mental lexicon are insufficient because they cannot capture multiplex viability, contextual meaning, or cross-layer interactions that influence language cognition.
single_layer_networks_insufficient_for_c.paper: Cognitive modelling of concepts in the mental lexicon with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements, and future challenges

semantic_distance_as_metacognitive_cue_f.name: Semantic distance as metacognitive cue for originality judgments
semantic_distance_as_metacognitive_cue_f.description: The paper claims that semantic distance between a generated idea and the stimulus functions as a heuristic cue that biases originality judgments (self-assessments), contributing to the gap between actual originality scores and perceived originality.
semantic_distance_as_metacognitive_cue_f.paper: The Role of Semantic Associations as a Metacognitive Cue in Creative Idea Generation

originality_scores_and_originality_judgm.name: Originality scores and originality judgments are produced by separate processes
originality_scores_and_originality_judgm.description: The paper builds on prior evidence that objective originality scores (frequency-based) and subjective originality judgments (self-assessments) are dissociable, implying they are not simply correlated readouts of the same underlying process.
originality_scores_and_originality_judgm.paper: The Role of Semantic Associations as a Metacognitive Cue in Creative Idea Generation

semantic_distance_as_independent_predict.name: Semantic distance as independent predictor of originality scores
semantic_distance_as_independent_predict.description: In Experiment 1 re-analysis, semantic distance between generated ideas and stimuli contributed explanatory value for predicting objective originality scores above and beyond previously established cues.
semantic_distance_as_independent_predict.paper: The Role of Semantic Associations as a Metacognitive Cue in Creative Idea Generation

priming_with_examples_at_different_seman.name: Priming with examples at different semantic distances biases originality judgments
priming_with_examples_at_different_seman.description: In Experiment 2, manipulating task instructions to prime participants with examples differing in originality level and semantic distance produced differences in the extent of bias in originality judgments, replicating the cue role of semantic distance experimentally.
priming_with_examples_at_different_seman.paper: The Role of Semantic Associations as a Metacognitive Cue in Creative Idea Generation

frequency_based_operationalization_of_or.name: Frequency-based operationalization of originality score
frequency_based_operationalization_of_or.description: The study operationalizes objective originality as the frequency percentage of each idea within the sample of participants, treating less common ideas as more original.
frequency_based_operationalization_of_or.paper: The Role of Semantic Associations as a Metacognitive Cue in Creative Idea Generation

known_heuristic_cues_for_originality_jud.name: Known heuristic cues for originality judgment biases are understudied
known_heuristic_cues_for_originality_jud.description: The paper identifies that although originality judgments are prone to biases, the specific heuristic cues driving these biases are largely unknown prior to this work.
known_heuristic_cues_for_originality_jud.paper: The Role of Semantic Associations as a Metacognitive Cue in Creative Idea Generation

semantic_distance_contributes_to_the_gap.name: Semantic distance contributes to the gap between originality scores and originality judgments
semantic_distance_contributes_to_the_gap.description: Experiment 1 reanalysis shows that semantic distance specifically explains variance in the discrepancy between objective originality scores and self-assessed originality judgments, not merely in each measure independently.
semantic_distance_contributes_to_the_gap.paper: The Role of Semantic Associations as a Metacognitive Cue in Creative Idea Generation

computational_semantic_distance_applied_.name: Computational semantic distance applied to originality judgment research
computational_semantic_distance_applied_.description: The study uses computational linguistics methods to calculate semantic distance between generated ideas and task stimuli, applying these measures to predict both objective originality and subjective originality judgments.
computational_semantic_distance_applied_.paper: The Role of Semantic Associations as a Metacognitive Cue in Creative Idea Generation

originality_judgment_as_self_assessment_.name: Originality judgment as self-assessment of idea frequency
originality_judgment_as_self_assessment_.description: Originality judgment is operationalized as participants' self-assessment of how frequently their idea was generated by others, providing a subjective frequency estimate to be compared against actual frequency scores.
originality_judgment_as_self_assessment_.paper: The Role of Semantic Associations as a Metacognitive Cue in Creative Idea Generation

context_affects_neural_mechanisms_for_me.name: Context affects neural mechanisms for metaphor comprehension
context_affects_neural_mechanisms_for_me.description: The study demonstrates that the context in which novel metaphors are encountered (meaningful narrative vs. jabberwocky) changes the neural mechanisms engaged during their comprehension, confirming that context modulates creative language processing.
context_affects_neural_mechanisms_for_me.paper: Context matters: Novel metaphors in supportive and non-supportive contexts

narrative_context_eliminates_neural_dist.name: Narrative context eliminates neural distinction between novel metaphors and literal sentences
narrative_context_eliminates_neural_dist.description: When embedded in a meaningful narrative, novel metaphors and literal sentences produce indistinguishable neural activations, with both conditions activating bilateral anterior temporal poles, middle temporal gyri, superior temporal sulci, and angular gyri.
narrative_context_eliminates_neural_dist.paper: Context matters: Novel metaphors in supportive and non-supportive contexts

sensorimotor_grounding_increases_for_nar.name: Sensorimotor grounding increases for narrative-embedded metaphors relative to jabberwocky-embedded metaphors
sensorimotor_grounding_increases_for_nar.description: Novel metaphors embedded in a meaningful narrative show increased responses in sensorimotor areas corresponding to the modality of the target word's literal meaning compared to the same metaphors embedded in jabberwocky contexts, suggesting deeper semantic processing in supportive contexts.
sensorimotor_grounding_increases_for_nar.paper: Context matters: Novel metaphors in supportive and non-supportive contexts

appropriateness_of_novel_metaphors_is_co.name: Appropriateness of novel metaphors is context-dependent
appropriateness_of_novel_metaphors_is_co.description: While novelty is an inherent property of unfamiliar metaphors, appropriateness depends on the surrounding context; the study operationalizes this by embedding identical metaphors in supportive narratives versus jabberwocky non-contexts.
appropriateness_of_novel_metaphors_is_co.paper: Context matters: Novel metaphors in supportive and non-supportive contexts

bilateral_temporal_and_angular_regions_s.name: Bilateral temporal and angular regions support creative language comprehension in context
bilateral_temporal_and_angular_regions_s.description: Bilateral anterior temporal poles, middle temporal gyri, superior temporal sulci, and angular gyri are activated during comprehension of both novel metaphors and literal sentences when embedded in a meaningful narrative, implicating these regions in context-supported semantic integration rather than metaphor-specific processing.
bilateral_temporal_and_angular_regions_s.paper: Context matters: Novel metaphors in supportive and non-supportive contexts

neural_correlates_of_novel_metaphor_comp.name: Neural correlates of novel metaphor comprehension vary with contextual support
neural_correlates_of_novel_metaphor_comp.description: The study provides fMRI evidence that the same novel metaphor stimulus recruits different neural circuits depending on whether it appears in a supportive narrative or a meaningless jabberwocky context, advancing understanding of how context shapes creative language processing.
neural_correlates_of_novel_metaphor_comp.paper: Context matters: Novel metaphors in supportive and non-supportive contexts

cognitive_basis_of_figurative_language_c.name: Cognitive basis of figurative language comprehension in context as understudied domain
cognitive_basis_of_figurative_language_c.description: The study motivates its inquiry by noting that while novelty is a well-studied property of metaphors, how contextual appropriateness shapes comprehension at the neural level has been insufficiently examined, particularly for novel metaphors.
cognitive_basis_of_figurative_language_c.paper: Context matters: Novel metaphors in supportive and non-supportive contexts

anodal_lpfc_stimulation_increases_approp.name: anodal_lpfc_stimulation_increases_appropriateness_and_decreases_novelty
anodal_lpfc_stimulation_increases_approp.description: Anodal (excitatory) tDCS over left prefrontal cortex increased the appropriateness and decreased the novelty of creative sentence completions, suggesting that enhanced cognitive control biases responses toward more conventional, appropriate ideas.
anodal_lpfc_stimulation_increases_approp.paper: Noninvasive brain stimulation to lateral prefrontal cortex alters the novelty of creative idea generation

cathodal_lpfc_stimulation_does_not_incre.name: cathodal_lpfc_stimulation_does_not_increase_novelty_in_sentence_completion_task
cathodal_lpfc_stimulation_does_not_incre.description: Unlike several prior brain stimulation studies, cathodal (inhibitory) tDCS over left prefrontal cortex did not increase novelty of creative responses, which the authors attribute to task-specific demands of the sentence completion paradigm.
cathodal_lpfc_stimulation_does_not_incre.paper: Noninvasive brain stimulation to lateral prefrontal cortex alters the novelty of creative idea generation

cognitive_control_inhibits_novel_idea_ge.name: cognitive_control_inhibits_novel_idea_generation
cognitive_control_inhibits_novel_idea_ge.description: The study argues that cognitive control mechanisms can inhibit novel idea generation, with enhanced prefrontal activity constraining access to weaker, more remote associations that underlie creative novelty.
cognitive_control_inhibits_novel_idea_ge.paper: Noninvasive brain stimulation to lateral prefrontal cortex alters the novelty of creative idea generation

task_demands_moderate_effect_of_cognitiv.name: task_demands_moderate_effect_of_cognitive_control_on_creative_ideation
task_demands_moderate_effect_of_cognitiv.description: The paper proposes that whether reduced cognitive control facilitates or hinders creativity depends on task demands, explaining why cathodal stimulation did not increase novelty in a task requiring appropriate completions.
task_demands_moderate_effect_of_cognitiv.paper: Noninvasive brain stimulation to lateral prefrontal cortex alters the novelty of creative idea generation

tdcs_over_lpfc_as_method_for_probing_cog.name: tDCS_over_lpfc_as_method_for_probing_cognitive_control_in_creativity
tdcs_over_lpfc_as_method_for_probing_cog.description: Participants underwent anodal, cathodal, or sham tDCS over left prefrontal cortex while completing sentences with uncommon but appropriate endings, with responses rated by independent raters for novelty and appropriateness.
tdcs_over_lpfc_as_method_for_probing_cog.paper: Noninvasive brain stimulation to lateral prefrontal cortex alters the novelty of creative idea generation

sentence_completion_with_uncommon_ending.name: sentence_completion_with_uncommon_endings_as_creative_ideation_task
sentence_completion_with_uncommon_ending.description: Participants read sentences with the final word missing and were instructed to produce an uncommon but appropriate completion, providing a constrained creative ideation paradigm distinct from open-ended divergent thinking tasks.
sentence_completion_with_uncommon_ending.paper: Noninvasive brain stimulation to lateral prefrontal cortex alters the novelty of creative idea generation

independent_rater_scoring_of_novelty_and.name: independent_rater_scoring_of_novelty_and_appropriateness_for_creative_responses
independent_rater_scoring_of_novelty_and.description: Creative sentence completions were evaluated by an independent sample of raters on dimensions of novelty and appropriateness, providing behavioral outcome measures for the tDCS manipulation.
independent_rater_scoring_of_novelty_and.paper: Noninvasive brain stimulation to lateral prefrontal cortex alters the novelty of creative idea generation

differential_effect_of_lpfc_stimulation_.name: differential_effect_of_lpfc_stimulation_on_novelty_versus_appropriateness
differential_effect_of_lpfc_stimulation_.description: Anodal stimulation selectively affected novelty and appropriateness in opposite directions, suggesting that cognitive control differentially impacts distinct components of creative output quality.
differential_effect_of_lpfc_stimulation_.paper: Noninvasive brain stimulation to lateral prefrontal cortex alters the novelty of creative idea generation

weak_association_access_as_mechanism_lin.name: weak_association_access_as_mechanism_linking_reduced_control_to_increased_novelty
weak_association_access_as_mechanism_lin.description: The paper theorizes that reduced cognitive control allows access to weaker, less inhibited associations, thereby increasing the novelty of generated ideas, consistent with associative theories of creativity.
weak_association_access_as_mechanism_lin.paper: Noninvasive brain stimulation to lateral prefrontal cortex alters the novelty of creative idea generation

nct_applied_to_subclinical_depression_vi.name: NCT applied to subclinical depression via DTI
nct_applied_to_subclinical_depression_vi.description: The study applies computational network control theory to structural brain imaging data (DTI) in a large sample to examine how network control metrics relate to individual differences in subclinical depression symptoms.
nct_applied_to_subclinical_depression_vi.paper: A computational network control theory analysis of depression symptoms

subclinical_depression_negatively_relate.name: Subclinical depression negatively related to integration ability in right anterior insula
subclinical_depression_negatively_relate.description: Higher integration ability in the right anterior insula is negatively associated with subclinical depression scores, implicating atypical switching between the default mode and Executive Control Networks.
subclinical_depression_negatively_relate.paper: A computational network control theory analysis of depression symptoms

subclinical_depression_related_to_easy_t.name: Subclinical depression related to easy-to-reach neural state driving in bilateral lingual and lateral occipital gyri
subclinical_depression_related_to_easy_t.description: Subclinical depression is associated with the ability to drive the brain into easy-to-reach neural states in the bilateral lingual gyrus and lateral occipital gyrus, highlighting regions less studied in depression.
subclinical_depression_related_to_easy_t.paper: A computational network control theory analysis of depression symptoms

rumination_and_impaired_inhibition_as_co.name: Rumination and impaired inhibition as core neurocognitive targets for NCT in depression
rumination_and_impaired_inhibition_as_co.description: The neurocognitive mechanisms underlying rumination and impaired inhibition in depression remain unclear, motivating application of network control theory to characterize brain regions' regulatory roles.
rumination_and_impaired_inhibition_as_co.paper: A computational network control theory analysis of depression symptoms

replication_and_extension_of_atypical_dm.name: Replication and extension of atypical DMN-ECN switching findings in depression
replication_and_extension_of_atypical_dm.description: The right anterior insula finding replicates and extends previous studies implicating atypical switching between the default mode network and the Executive Control Network in depression.
replication_and_extension_of_atypical_dm.paper: A computational network control theory analysis of depression symptoms

nct_anatomical_fingerprint_model_of_regi.name: NCT anatomical fingerprint model of regional brain dynamics
nct_anatomical_fingerprint_model_of_regi.description: Network control theory mathematically models patterns of inter-region activity propagated along structural network architecture, characterizing each brain region's potential regulatory role based on its anatomical connectivity fingerprint and simplified node dynamics.
nct_anatomical_fingerprint_model_of_regi.paper: A computational network control theory analysis of depression symptoms

novel_roles_of_lingual_and_lateral_occip.name: Novel roles of lingual and lateral occipital gyri in depression
novel_roles_of_lingual_and_lateral_occip.description: The bilateral lingual gyrus and lateral occipital gyrus are identified as regions whose controllability relates to depression symptoms, highlighting their roles in driving the brain into different neural states that are less known in depression research.
novel_roles_of_lingual_and_lateral_occip.paper: A computational network control theory analysis of depression symptoms

entropy_modulation_theory_of_creative_ex.name: entropy_modulation_theory_of_creative_exploration
entropy_modulation_theory_of_creative_ex.description: A new theoretical framework integrating structure- and process-based accounts of creativity, proposing that higher creative individuals differ from lower creative individuals in their executive modulation of entropy during cognitive search and memory retrieval.
entropy_modulation_theory_of_creative_ex.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

faster_and_further_phenomenology_in_crea.name: faster_and_further_phenomenology_in_creativity
faster_and_further_phenomenology_in_crea.description: Higher creative individuals tend to produce ideas more quickly and with more novelty than lower creative individuals, a pattern termed faster-and-further phenomenology that any adequate creativity theory must explain.
faster_and_further_phenomenology_in_crea.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

limitations_of_structural_accounts_in_ex.name: limitations_of_structural_accounts_in_explaining_faster_and_further_phenomenology
limitations_of_structural_accounts_in_ex.description: Although research demonstrates semantic structure differences between higher and lower creative individuals, purely structural explanations are formally insufficient to account for faster-and-further phenomenology without a process-level mechanism.
limitations_of_structural_accounts_in_ex.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

limitations_of_executive_accounts_in_exp.name: limitations_of_executive_accounts_in_explaining_faster_and_further_phenomenology
limitations_of_executive_accounts_in_exp.description: Although executive abilities correlate with creativity, no formal process model exists that explains how these abilities contribute to the faster-and-further phenomenology observed in higher creative individuals.
limitations_of_executive_accounts_in_exp.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

entropy_as_the_mechanism_linking_executi.name: entropy_as_the_mechanism_linking_executive_control_and_semantic_structure_in_creativity
entropy_as_the_mechanism_linking_executi.description: Entropy modulation theory posits that the apparent differences in semantic structure between higher and lower creative individuals can be produced via an entropy-modulating retrieval process, which tunes cognitive entropy to mediate flexibility and the exploration-exploitation trade-off.
entropy_as_the_mechanism_linking_executi.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

activation_magnitude_and_variance_as_ind.name: activation_magnitude_and_variance_as_independent_contributors_to_retrieval_entropy
activation_magnitude_and_variance_as_ind.description: Within entropy modulation theory, both activation magnitude and activation variance independently enhance the entropy of target retrieval, which in turn increases retrieval speed and the breadth of ideas accessed during creative search.
activation_magnitude_and_variance_as_ind.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

entropy_modulation_as_integration_of_str.name: entropy_modulation_as_integration_of_structure_and_process_accounts
entropy_modulation_as_integration_of_str.description: Entropy modulation theory is positioned as an integrative framework that subsumes both associative (structure-based) and executive (process-based) theories of creativity under a single formal mechanism.
entropy_modulation_as_integration_of_str.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

racing_to_threshold_retrieval_model_unde.name: racing_to_threshold_retrieval_model_underlying_entropy_modulation
racing_to_threshold_retrieval_model_unde.description: Entropy modulation theory assumes a race-to-threshold model of memory retrieval in which multiple targets compete to reach an activation threshold, with entropy governing the distribution of activation across candidates.
racing_to_threshold_retrieval_model_unde.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

exploration_exploitation_trade_off_as_a_.name: exploration_exploitation_trade_off_as_a_core_feature_of_creative_search
exploration_exploitation_trade_off_as_a_.description: Entropy modulation theory frames creative cognitive search as governed by an exploration-exploitation trade-off, with higher entropy facilitating exploratory search and lower entropy favoring focused exploitation of well-connected memory regions.
exploration_exploitation_trade_off_as_a_.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

combination_association_task_as_a_measur.name: Combination Association Task as a measure of remote associate combining
combination_association_task_as_a_measur.description: The Combination Association Task (CAT) is used to measure participants' ability to combine close and distant remote associates, providing a graded index of associative remoteness in creative thinking. The task allows dissociation of close versus distant associative demands within the same paradigm.
combination_association_task_as_a_measur.paper: Brain Connectivity-Based Prediction of Combining Remote Semantic Associates for Creative Thinking

whole_brain_functional_connectivity_pred.name: Whole-brain functional connectivity predicts CAT performance
whole_brain_functional_connectivity_pred.description: Connectome predictive modeling of whole-brain functional connectivity identified brain networks that predict individual differences in performance on the Combination Association Task. These patterns reflect the neural basis of combining remote semantic associates.
whole_brain_functional_connectivity_pred.paper: Brain Connectivity-Based Prediction of Combining Remote Semantic Associates for Creative Thinking

default_mode_network_dominates_connectiv.name: Default Mode Network dominates connectivity predictors of general CAT performance
default_mode_network_dominates_connectiv.description: Brain functional connectivity networks predicting overall CAT performance are predominantly anchored in the Default Mode Network, consistent with the DMN's known role in associative and self-generated thought. This contribution is present across all trials regardless of associative remoteness.
default_mode_network_dominates_connectiv.paper: Brain Connectivity-Based Prediction of Combining Remote Semantic Associates for Creative Thinking

executive_control_dorsal_attention_and_s.name: Executive Control, Dorsal Attention, and Somatomotor networks linked to higher associative remoteness in CAT
executive_control_dorsal_attention_and_s.description: Functional connectivity patterns specifically associated with trials requiring combination of more distant associates implicate the Executive Control Network, Dorsal Attention Network, and Somatomotor networks, indicating that controlled processes become increasingly important as associative remoteness increases.
executive_control_dorsal_attention_and_s.paper: Brain Connectivity-Based Prediction of Combining Remote Semantic Associates for Creative Thinking

shared_functional_connectivity_between_r.name: Shared functional connectivity between remote associative combining and divergent thinking
shared_functional_connectivity_between_r.description: The functional connectivity patterns predicting higher creative demands in the CAT overlap with patterns previously found to predict divergent thinking performance, suggesting common neural mechanisms underlie both convergent and divergent forms of remote thinking.
shared_functional_connectivity_between_r.paper: Brain Connectivity-Based Prediction of Combining Remote Semantic Associates for Creative Thinking

connectome_predictive_modeling_applied_t.name: Connectome predictive modeling applied to remote associative thinking
connectome_predictive_modeling_applied_t.description: Connectome predictive modeling (CPM) is applied to whole-brain resting-state or task-based functional connectivity data to predict performance on the CAT, extending CPM's application from divergent thinking to convergent remote association tasks.
connectome_predictive_modeling_applied_t.paper: Brain Connectivity-Based Prediction of Combining Remote Semantic Associates for Creative Thinking

neural_mechanisms_of_combining_remote_as.name: Neural mechanisms of combining remote associates are poorly understood
neural_mechanisms_of_combining_remote_as.description: Despite the established importance of associative thinking in creativity, the specific neural mechanisms enabling the combination of distant conceptual associates in creative tasks remain largely uncharacterized. This gap motivates the current study.
neural_mechanisms_of_combining_remote_as.paper: Brain Connectivity-Based Prediction of Combining Remote Semantic Associates for Creative Thinking

convergent_and_divergent_remote_thinking.name: Convergent and divergent remote thinking share neural substrates
convergent_and_divergent_remote_thinking.description: The authors propose that neural mechanisms supporting remote associative combining in the CAT (a convergent task) overlap with those supporting divergent thinking, suggesting a domain-general neural architecture for remote semantic thought.
convergent_and_divergent_remote_thinking.paper: Brain Connectivity-Based Prediction of Combining Remote Semantic Associates for Creative Thinking

akinator_game_as_a_mirror_of_human_quest.name: Akinator game as a mirror of human question-asking strategies
akinator_game_as_a_mirror_of_human_quest.description: The authors propose that the Akinator online game's question-asking process reflects how humans ask questions, making it a tractable proxy for studying human question-asking behavior.
akinator_game_as_a_mirror_of_human_quest.paper: A Mirror to Human Question Asking: Analyzing the Akinator Online Question Game

exploratory_data_driven_analysis_of_akin.name: Exploratory data-driven analysis of Akinator question corpus
exploratory_data_driven_analysis_of_akin.description: The study conducts an exploratory data analysis of complete Akinator games and individual questions, examining mathematical, algorithmic, and gamification-based strategies for question selection.
exploratory_data_driven_analysis_of_akin.paper: A Mirror to Human Question Asking: Analyzing the Akinator Online Question Game

topic_modelling_of_akinator_questions_re.name: Topic modelling of Akinator questions reveals coherent inquiry clusters
topic_modelling_of_akinator_questions_re.description: Using topic-modelling techniques, the authors map Akinator questions into clusters and find coherent topics that the Akinator draws upon when generating questions.
topic_modelling_of_akinator_questions_re.paper: A Mirror to Human Question Asking: Analyzing the Akinator Online Question Game

gamification_characteristics_drive_quest.name: Gamification characteristics drive question specificity and type in Akinator
gamification_characteristics_drive_quest.description: The analysis reveals surprising aspects of question specificity and type that appear to be driven by the gamification features of the Akinator game rather than purely by information-theoretic optimality.
gamification_characteristics_drive_quest.paper: A Mirror to Human Question Asking: Analyzing the Akinator Online Question Game

commonalities_between_human_and_akinator.name: Commonalities between human and Akinator question-asking strategies
commonalities_between_human_and_akinator.description: The study identifies shared strategies between human question-askers and the Akinator, suggesting that the game encodes human-like inquiry patterns.
commonalities_between_human_and_akinator.paper: A Mirror to Human Question Asking: Analyzing the Akinator Online Question Game

question_asking_cognitive_mechanisms_as_.name: Question-asking cognitive mechanisms as understudied domain
question_asking_cognitive_mechanisms_as_.description: The authors note that little is known about why people ask questions, what makes a question better than others, or what cognitive mechanisms underlie informative question generation.
question_asking_cognitive_mechanisms_as_.paper: A Mirror to Human Question Asking: Analyzing the Akinator Online Question Game

mathematical_and_gamification_strategy_c.name: Mathematical and gamification strategy comparison for question selection
mathematical_and_gamification_strategy_c.description: The analysis compares mathematical (e.g., information-theoretic) algorithms against gamification-driven considerations as explanations for the Akinator's question selection process.
mathematical_and_gamification_strategy_c.paper: A Mirror to Human Question Asking: Analyzing the Akinator Online Question Game

network_science_application_to_cognition.name: network_science_application_to_cognition_as_underutilized_relative_to_brain
network_science_application_to_cognition.description: The editors assert that while network science has greatly advanced understanding of brain structure and function, its application to studying cognition specifically has been done to a much lesser extent, representing an underutilized opportunity.
network_science_application_to_cognition.paper: Editors' Introduction to Networks of the Mind: How Can Network Science Elucidate Our Understanding of Cognition?

breadth_of_cognitive_domains_addressable.name: breadth_of_cognitive_domains_addressable_by_network_science
breadth_of_cognitive_domains_addressable.description: The editors argue that network science methods are applicable across a wide scope of cognitive research areas, from generalist accounts of memory and encoding to individual differences, communities, and cultural and individual change.
breadth_of_cognitive_domains_addressable.paper: Editors' Introduction to Networks of the Mind: How Can Network Science Elucidate Our Understanding of Cognition?

network_science_as_a_multidisciplinary_q.name: network_science_as_a_multidisciplinary_quantitative_field_for_complex_systems
network_science_as_a_multidisciplinary_q.description: The editors introduce network science as a rapidly growing multidisciplinary quantitative field that represents complex systems as networks or graphs and studies their properties, positioning it as a unifying framework for cognitive science.
network_science_as_a_multidisciplinary_q.paper: Editors' Introduction to Networks of the Mind: How Can Network Science Elucidate Our Understanding of Cognition?

cultural_and_individual_change_as_a_targ.name: cultural_and_individual_change_as_a_target_for_cognitive_network_science
cultural_and_individual_change_as_a_targ.description: The editors identify cultural and individual change as a research area where network science can be fruitfully applied to study cognition, expanding beyond static representations to dynamic and longitudinal phenomena.
cultural_and_individual_change_as_a_targ.paper: Editors' Introduction to Networks of the Mind: How Can Network Science Elucidate Our Understanding of Cognition?

community_level_cognition_as_addressable.name: community_level_cognition_as_addressable_by_network_science
community_level_cognition_as_addressable.description: The editors highlight communities as a scope of research where network science tools can elucidate cognitive phenomena, suggesting applicability beyond the individual to group and societal levels.
community_level_cognition_as_addressable.paper: Editors' Introduction to Networks of the Mind: How Can Network Science Elucidate Our Understanding of Cognition?

semantic_memory_restructuring_supports_s.name: Semantic memory restructuring supports successful riddle solving
semantic_memory_restructuring_supports_s.description: The study provides initial evidence that changes in participants' semantic memory networks (SemNets) between pre- and post-solving phases are associated with successfully solving a riddle. Restructuring was quantified as differences in SemNet metrics before and after the problem-solving attempt.
semantic_memory_restructuring_supports_s.paper: Changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving and analogical transfer

restructuring_of_semantically_remote_con.name: Restructuring of semantically remote concepts is instrumental in active problem solving
restructuring_of_semantically_remote_con.description: Only changes involving semantically remote concepts (not solution-relevant concepts) were found to be instrumental in actively solving the riddle. Changes in solution-relevant concepts may instead reflect pre-exposure to the solution rather than active restructuring.
restructuring_of_semantically_remote_con.paper: Changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving and analogical transfer

semantic_memory_restructuring_facilitate.name: Semantic memory restructuring facilitates analogical transfer
semantic_memory_restructuring_facilitate.description: Restructuring of problem-related semantic memory networks not only supports solving the original riddle but also facilitates solving an analogous problem subsequently. The abstract problem representation formed through restructuring is posited as the mechanism enabling analogical transfer.
semantic_memory_restructuring_facilitate.paper: Changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving and analogical transfer

network_science_methodology_for_quantify.name: Network science methodology for quantifying problem representation change
network_science_methodology_for_quantify.description: The study uses semantic memory network (SemNet) estimation to measure participants' problem-related representations before and after a riddle-solving attempt. Restructuring is operationalized as the difference in SemNet metrics (e.g., distances, connectivity) between pre- and post-solving phases.
network_science_methodology_for_quantify.paper: Changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving and analogical transfer

solution_relevant_and_remote_concepts_bo.name: Solution-relevant and remote concepts both become more strongly related in solvers
solution_relevant_and_remote_concepts_bo.description: In participants who successfully solve the riddle, both solution-relevant concepts and semantically remote concepts become more strongly related in their semantic memory networks after the solving attempt compared to before.
solution_relevant_and_remote_concepts_bo.paper: Changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving and analogical transfer

abstract_problem_representation_as_mecha.name: Abstract problem representation as mechanism for analogical transfer in problem solving
abstract_problem_representation_as_mecha.description: The paper proposes that restructuring leads to an abstract problem-related representation, and this abstraction is the mechanism that facilitates solving structurally analogous problems. This extends the restructuring hypothesis to explain analogical transfer.
abstract_problem_representation_as_mecha.paper: Changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving and analogical transfer

pre_to_post_semnet_change_as_operational.name: Pre-to-post SemNet change as operational measure of representational restructuring
pre_to_post_semnet_change_as_operational.description: The study introduces quantifying representational restructuring in problem solving as the difference in semantic memory network metrics between pre- and post-solving phases, providing an objective, network-based operationalization of a core but previously hard-to-measure cognitive construct.
pre_to_post_semnet_change_as_operational.paper: Changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving and analogical transfer

semantic_network_analysis_of_beauty_and_.name: Semantic network analysis of beauty and wellness concepts
semantic_network_analysis_of_beauty_and_.description: The study applied network science methods to estimate and compare semantic networks associated with beauty and wellness using free association responses to 47 words across age cohorts and sexes.
semantic_network_analysis_of_beauty_and_.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

core_semantic_associates_of_beauty_are_e.name: Core semantic associates of beauty are elegance, feminine, gorgeous, lovely, sexy, and stylish
core_semantic_associates_of_beauty_are_e.description: Across groups, beauty was consistently associated with a specific set of terms including Elegance, Feminine, Gorgeous, Lovely, Sexy, and Stylish in the estimated semantic networks.
core_semantic_associates_of_beauty_are_e.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

core_semantic_associates_of_wellness_are.name: Core semantic associates of wellness are aerobics, fitness, health, holistic, lifestyle, medical, nutrition, and thrive
core_semantic_associates_of_wellness_are.description: Wellness was consistently linked to health-related and lifestyle terms such as Aerobics, Fitness, Health, Holistic, Lifestyle, Medical, Nutrition, and Thrive across groups.
core_semantic_associates_of_wellness_are.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

older_cohorts_have_less_connected_and_mo.name: Older cohorts have less connected and more segregated beauty and wellness semantic networks
older_cohorts_have_less_connected_and_mo.description: Older generational cohorts (Generation X and Baby Boomers) showed semantic networks that were less connected and more segregated compared to younger cohorts (Generation Z and Millennials).
older_cohorts_have_less_connected_and_mo.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

women_have_more_segregated_and_organized.name: Women have more segregated and organized beauty and wellness semantic networks than men
women_have_more_segregated_and_organized.description: Women compared to men showed more segregated and organized conceptual structures for both beauty and wellness in their group-based semantic networks.
women_have_more_segregated_and_organized.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

age_cohort_differences_in_beauty_and_wel.name: Age-cohort differences in beauty and wellness semantic network structure
age_cohort_differences_in_beauty_and_wel.description: Generational cohort membership (Gen Z, Millennials, Gen X, Baby Boomers) is associated with systematic differences in the semantic network representations of beauty and wellness.
age_cohort_differences_in_beauty_and_wel.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

conceptual_distinctiveness_of_beauty_ver.name: Conceptual distinctiveness of beauty versus wellness in semantic memory
conceptual_distinctiveness_of_beauty_ver.description: Beauty and wellness occupy separable semantic spaces despite co-occurring in contemporary health and lifestyle discourse, as evidenced by distinct core associates for each domain.
conceptual_distinctiveness_of_beauty_ver.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

group_based_semantic_network_estimation_.name: Group-based semantic network estimation from free association responses as method for probing lay concepts
group_based_semantic_network_estimation_.description: The study used free association responses to a 47-word stimulus list (beauty, wellness, and combined categories) to construct group-based semantic networks for comparing generational and sex-based differences.
group_based_semantic_network_estimation_.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

sex_differences_in_conceptual_organizati.name: Sex differences in conceptual organization of beauty and wellness
sex_differences_in_conceptual_organizati.description: The paper claims that women hold more segregated and organized concepts of beauty and wellness compared to men, reflecting societal preoccupation with beauty and healthy lifestyle.
sex_differences_in_conceptual_organizati.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

generational_shift_in_beauty_and_wellnes.name: Generational shift in beauty and wellness semantic representations
generational_shift_in_beauty_and_wellnes.description: The study claims that older generational cohorts conceptualize beauty and wellness in less connected and more segregated ways than younger cohorts, suggesting temporal cultural change in these concepts.
generational_shift_in_beauty_and_wellnes.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

application_of_network_science_to_lay_un.name: Application of network science to lay understanding of beauty and wellness as understudied domain
application_of_network_science_to_lay_un.description: The paper contributes a novel empirical mapping of how everyday people conceptualize and relate beauty and wellness, using computational network science rather than self-report scales.
application_of_network_science_to_lay_un.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

fan_effect_in_creative_thought.name: fan_effect_in_creative_thought
fan_effect_in_creative_thought.description: Increasing association size (semantic richness) of AUT cues creates interference from conceptually related information, facilitating idea quantity (fluency) but constraining idea quality (originality) in divergent thinking, analogous to the fan effect in memory research.
fan_effect_in_creative_thought.paper: A Fan Effect for Creative Thought: Semantic Richness Facilitates Idea Quantity but Constrains Idea Quality

cue_association_size_as_index_of_semanti.name: cue_association_size_as_index_of_semantic_richness_for_aut
cue_association_size_as_index_of_semanti.description: The study operationalizes semantic richness via cue association size—the average number of elements associated with a given concept—as an index for probing how the breadth of a concept's semantic neighborhood influences AUT performance.
cue_association_size_as_index_of_semanti.paper: A Fan Effect for Creative Thought: Semantic Richness Facilitates Idea Quantity but Constrains Idea Quality

sparse_semantic_knowledge_penalizes_orig.name: sparse_semantic_knowledge_penalizes_originality_when_fluid_intelligence_is_low
sparse_semantic_knowledge_penalizes_orig.description: An interaction between cue association size and fluid intelligence was observed for low-association AUT cues, such that individuals with higher fluid intelligence could overcome the originality constraints imposed by sparse semantic knowledge.
sparse_semantic_knowledge_penalizes_orig.paper: A Fan Effect for Creative Thought: Semantic Richness Facilitates Idea Quantity but Constrains Idea Quality

top_down_cognitive_control_compensates_f.name: top_down_cognitive_control_compensates_for_sparse_semantic_network_during_idea_generation
top_down_cognitive_control_compensates_f.description: When conceptual knowledge about a cue is limited (low association size), cognitive control processes indexed by fluid intelligence allow individuals to generate higher-quality ideas, suggesting executive resources substitute for richness of semantic context.
top_down_cognitive_control_compensates_f.paper: A Fan Effect for Creative Thought: Semantic Richness Facilitates Idea Quantity but Constrains Idea Quality

three_experiment_replication_of_cue_asso.name: three_experiment_replication_of_cue_association_size_effects
three_experiment_replication_of_cue_asso.description: The paper reports three experiments that confirm the hypothesized differential impact of cue association size on fluency versus originality in the AUT, providing converging evidence across study replications.
three_experiment_replication_of_cue_asso.paper: A Fan Effect for Creative Thought: Semantic Richness Facilitates Idea Quantity but Constrains Idea Quality

spreading_activation_interference_as_mec.name: spreading_activation_interference_as_mechanism_for_association_size_effects_on_creativity
spreading_activation_interference_as_mec.description: The paper proposes that the fan effect mechanism—more associates yielding more interference during spreading activation—explains why semantic richness raises fluency but lowers originality in divergent thinking.
spreading_activation_interference_as_mec.paper: A Fan Effect for Creative Thought: Semantic Richness Facilitates Idea Quantity but Constrains Idea Quality

modular_complex_network_resilience_under.name: Modular complex network resilience under targeted attacks
modular_complex_network_resilience_under.description: The paper claims that modular complex networks exhibit specific resilience properties when nodes with high betweenness (interconnected nodes between modules) are targeted, and that these properties can be studied analytically and numerically.

high_betweenness_interconnected_nodes_as.name: High-betweenness interconnected nodes as primary vulnerability sites in modular networks
high_betweenness_interconnected_nodes_as.description: Interconnected nodes bridging modules tend to have high betweenness centrality and are therefore disproportionately exposed to failure, making targeted attacks on these nodes especially damaging.

analytical_framework_for_modularity_and_.name: Analytical framework for modularity and network vulnerability
analytical_framework_for_modularity_and_.description: The paper develops a theoretical framework, leveraging advances in multilayer network theory, to systematically and analytically study the effect of modularity on network vulnerability and resilience.

feedback_between_structure_and_function_.name: Feedback between structure and function in modular real-world systems
feedback_between_structure_and_function_.description: The model provides insight into how structural damage (node removal) feeds back into functional degradation in real-world modular systems such as infrastructure, biological, and social networks.

implications_for_immunization_strategies.name: Implications for immunization strategies in modular networks
implications_for_immunization_strategies.description: The paper argues that understanding modular network resilience has direct implications for designing efficient immunization strategies by identifying which nodes to protect or vaccinate.

multilayer_network_theory_as_basis_for_m.name: Multilayer network theory as basis for modular resilience analysis
multilayer_network_theory_as_basis_for_m.description: The paper leverages recent advances in multilayer network theory to build an analytical treatment of modular networks, treating modules as layers or coupled subgraphs subject to inter-layer attack dynamics.

robustness_design_principles_for_large_s.name: Robustness design principles for large-scale infrastructure from modular resilience theory
robustness_design_principles_for_large_s.description: The authors propose that the analytical framework can guide the design of robust large-scale infrastructure by identifying structural features that improve resilience of modular systems.

modularity_effects_on_network_evolution_.name: Modularity effects on network evolution and stability
modularity_effects_on_network_evolution_.description: Prior work cited in the paper establishes that modularity has important effects on the evolution and stability of biological networks, scalability of infrastructure, and development of economic and social systems.

missing_analytical_framework_for_modular.name: Missing analytical framework for modularity and vulnerability as key research gap
missing_analytical_framework_for_modular.description: The paper identifies that despite known effects of modularity, an analytical framework for understanding modularity's effect on network vulnerability is still missing prior to this work.

associative_creativity_sparker_acs_as_a_.name: Associative Creativity Sparker (ACS) as a cognitive network science tool for breaking creative impasse
associative_creativity_sparker_acs_as_a_.description: The ACS is an online tool that uses cognitive network science to generate word recommendations aimed at breaking creative block. Recommendations are positioned either toward or away from previous ideas and close or far from the target object, based on a conceptual space derived from participant responses via online text analysis.
associative_creativity_sparker_acs_as_a_.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

location_of_word_recommendations_affects.name: Location of word recommendations affects fluency and originality in creative idea generation
location_of_word_recommendations_affects.description: Across both studies, the spatial positioning of word recommendations (toward vs. away from previous ideas; close vs. far from target object) influenced participants' fluency and originality scores in an alternative uses task, suggesting that semantic memory navigation direction matters for creative output.
location_of_word_recommendations_affects.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

novice_and_experienced_writers_different.name: Novice and experienced writers differentially benefit from word recommendations
novice_and_experienced_writers_different.description: Study 2, comparing 120 novice and 120 experienced writers, found that writing experience moderates the benefit derived from ACS word recommendations, indicating that prior expertise shapes how external semantic cues are utilized during creative idea generation.
novice_and_experienced_writers_different.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

creative_block_as_rooted_in_semantic_mem.name: Creative block as rooted in semantic memory network entrapment
creative_block_as_rooted_in_semantic_mem.description: The paper frames creative block as a state in which a person becomes stuck in a particular region of their semantic memory network, limiting the generation of novel ideas. Breaking creative impasse therefore requires navigating away from that entrenched region.
creative_block_as_rooted_in_semantic_mem.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

online_text_analysis_for_real_time_conce.name: Online text analysis for real-time conceptual space extraction as ACS method
online_text_analysis_for_real_time_conce.description: The ACS uses online text analysis applied to participant responses to extract a conceptual space in real time, which then determines the direction and distance of word recommendations relative to previous ideas and the target object.
online_text_analysis_for_real_time_conce.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

direction_and_distance_as_two_orthogonal.name: Direction and distance as two orthogonal dimensions of semantic word recommendations
direction_and_distance_as_two_orthogonal.description: The ACS operationalizes word recommendations along two dimensions: direction (toward or away from previous ideas) and distance (close or far from the target object), creating a multi-dimensional semantic navigation framework for creative facilitation.
direction_and_distance_as_two_orthogonal.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

writing_experience_as_a_moderator_of_sem.name: Writing experience as a moderator of semantic priming benefits for creativity
writing_experience_as_a_moderator_of_sem.description: The study proposes and tests that writing experience moderates how much individuals benefit from externally provided semantic cues, with novice versus experienced writers showing different patterns of response to ACS recommendations.
writing_experience_as_a_moderator_of_sem.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

acs_as_an_approach_to_studying_semantic_.name: ACS as an approach to studying semantic memory network navigation during creative impasse
acs_as_an_approach_to_studying_semantic_.description: Beyond being a practical tool, the ACS provides a paradigm for directly examining how positioning within semantic memory affects creative generation, allowing experimental manipulation of semantic search direction and distance.
acs_as_an_approach_to_studying_semantic_.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

replication_of_acs_effects_across_two_in.name: Replication of ACS effects across two independent studies
replication_of_acs_effects_across_two_in.description: The paper reports that the core finding—that word recommendation location affects fluency and originality—was observed in both Study 1 (121 participants, general population) and Study 2 (novice and experienced writers), providing a within-paper replication.
replication_of_acs_effects_across_two_in.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

semantic_memory_network_structure_as_a_d.name: Semantic memory network structure as a driver of creative block
semantic_memory_network_structure_as_a_d.description: The paper argues that creative block arises not merely from motivational or attentional factors but specifically from how a person's semantic memory network is organized, such that certain structural features lead to entrapment in unproductive regions.
semantic_memory_network_structure_as_a_d.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

neural_correlates_of_semantic_relatednes.name: Neural correlates of semantic relatedness judgment increase with semantic distance
neural_correlates_of_semantic_relatednes.description: Brain regions where activity increased with semantic relatedness judgments mainly overlapped with default, control, salience, semantic control, and multiple demand networks, with default and semantic control networks showing heightened involvement for more remote associations.
neural_correlates_of_semantic_relatednes.paper: Creative connections: the neural correlates of semantic relatedness are associated with creativity

higher_creative_individuals_show_lower_n.name: Higher creative individuals show lower neural activity during remote relatedness judgments
higher_creative_individuals_show_lower_n.description: Higher creative people, who provided higher relatedness judgments on average for remote concept pairs, exhibited lower activity in the default and semantic control network regions engaged by semantic relatedness, possibly reflecting higher neural efficiency.
higher_creative_individuals_show_lower_n.paper: Creative connections: the neural correlates of semantic relatedness are associated with creativity

judging_remote_concepts_as_related_is_a_.name: Judging remote concepts as related is a cognitive mechanism underlying creativity
judging_remote_concepts_as_related_is_a_.description: The study argues that the tendency to judge remotely related concepts as more related reflects a core cognitive mechanism of creativity, grounded in flexible semantic memory organization consistent with the associative theory.
judging_remote_concepts_as_related_is_a_.paper: Creative connections: the neural correlates of semantic relatedness are associated with creativity

hd_eeg_combined_with_connectome_modeling.name: HD-EEG combined with connectome modeling predicts individual creative behavior from resting-state functional connectivity
hd_eeg_combined_with_connectome_modeling.description: Combining high-density EEG with connectome modeling successfully predicts individual creative behavior from resting-state functional connectivity, emphasizing optimal activation interplay across networks in creative cognition.
hd_eeg_combined_with_connectome_modeling.paper: Creative connections: the neural correlates of semantic relatedness are associated with creativity

semantic_relatedness_judgment_task_as_an.name: Semantic relatedness judgment task as an fMRI paradigm for probing creativity-relevant semantic memory
semantic_relatedness_judgment_task_as_an.description: An fMRI semantic relatedness judgment task varying concept pairs by semantic distance was used in 93 participants to link neural responses to individual differences in creativity, operationalizing the associative theory of creativity neurally.
semantic_relatedness_judgment_task_as_an.paper: Creative connections: the neural correlates of semantic relatedness are associated with creativity

default_and_semantic_control_network_co_.name: Default and semantic control network co-engagement during remote association evaluation
default_and_semantic_control_network_co_.description: The default network and semantic control network both show increased involvement specifically when evaluating more remote semantic associations, suggesting their joint role in flexible conceptual processing underlying creativity.
default_and_semantic_control_network_co_.paper: Creative connections: the neural correlates of semantic relatedness are associated with creativity

non_specialized_hemisphere_recruits_for_.name: Non-specialized hemisphere recruits for unconventional stimulus processing
non_specialized_hemisphere_recruits_for_.description: The study claims that while conventional faces are better processed by the specialized right hemisphere, unconventional faces are better processed by the non-specialized left hemisphere, as demonstrated via the split visual field paradigm.
non_specialized_hemisphere_recruits_for_.paper: Processing of unconventional stimuli requires the recruitment of the non-specialized hemisphere

unconventional_face_processing_by_non_sp.name: Unconventional face processing by non-specialized LH correlates with verbal creativity
unconventional_face_processing_by_non_sp.description: Only unconventional face recognition performance attributed to the non-specialized left hemisphere was significantly related to verbal creativity measures; conventional face processing showed no such relationship.
unconventional_face_processing_by_non_sp.paper: Processing of unconventional stimuli requires the recruitment of the non-specialized hemisphere

unconventional_face_processing_by_non_sp.name: Unconventional face processing by non-specialized LH correlates with visual creativity
unconventional_face_processing_by_non_sp.description: The study reports that left hemisphere processing of unconventional faces is related not only to verbal but also to visual measures of creative ability, suggesting domain-general involvement of the non-specialized hemisphere in creativity.
unconventional_face_processing_by_non_sp.paper: Processing of unconventional stimuli requires the recruitment of the non-specialized hemisphere

mooney_face_and_silhouette_recognition_a.name: Mooney face and silhouette recognition as unconventional visual creativity measures
