mooney_face_and_silhouette_recognition_a.description: Experiment 1 used two unconventional visual recognition tasks—Mooney face recognition and objects' silhouette recognition—as measures linking visual processing to verbal creative ability.
mooney_face_and_silhouette_recognition_a.paper: Processing of unconventional stimuli requires the recruitment of the non-specialized hemisphere

split_visual_field_paradigm_for_hemisphe.name: Split visual field paradigm for hemispheric creativity research
split_visual_field_paradigm_for_hemisphe.description: Experiment 2 employed the split visual field (SVF) paradigm to lateralize presentation of conventional and unconventional faces and examine hemisphere-specific contributions to creative ability.
split_visual_field_paradigm_for_hemisphe.paper: Processing of unconventional stimuli requires the recruitment of the non-specialized hemisphere

verbal_creativity_linked_to_unconvention.name: Verbal creativity linked to unconventional visual recognition in Experiment 1
verbal_creativity_linked_to_unconvention.description: In Experiment 1, measures of verbal creativity showed a significant relationship with unconventional face (Mooney) recognition performance, connecting verbal creative ability to unconventional visual processing without hemispheric decomposition.
verbal_creativity_linked_to_unconvention.paper: Processing of unconventional stimuli requires the recruitment of the non-specialized hemisphere

specialized_rh_advantage_for_conventiona.name: Specialized RH advantage for conventional faces
specialized_rh_advantage_for_conventiona.description: The study confirms that conventional faces are better processed by the specialized right hemisphere, consistent with established literature on hemispheric lateralization of face processing.
specialized_rh_advantage_for_conventiona.paper: Processing of unconventional stimuli requires the recruitment of the non-specialized hemisphere

semantic_memory_restructuration_as_mecha.name: Semantic memory restructuration as mechanism for riddle solving
semantic_memory_restructuration_as_mecha.description: The study argues that restructuration—reinterpretation and reorganization of problem-related representations—underlies successful problem-solving, and tests this empirically using pre- and post-solving semantic network comparisons.
semantic_memory_restructuration_as_mecha.paper: Dynamic changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving

local_versus_global_restructuration_spec.name: Local versus global restructuration specificity in problem-solving
local_versus_global_restructuration_spec.description: Restructuration predicts successful problem-solving only locally—in edges and nodes judged as helpful for solving the riddle—not globally across the entire semantic network.
local_versus_global_restructuration_spec.paper: Dynamic changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving

individual_semantic_network_estimation_f.name: Individual semantic network estimation from relatedness judgments for problem-solving research
individual_semantic_network_estimation_f.description: Participants provided pairwise relatedness judgments for solution-relevant and irrelevant terms before and after a riddle-solving attempt; these judgments formed individual semantic memory networks whose structure was compared across phases.
individual_semantic_network_estimation_f.paper: Dynamic changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving

scarcity_of_empirical_evidence_for_repre.name: Scarcity of empirical evidence for representational restructuration in problem-solving
scarcity_of_empirical_evidence_for_repre.description: Although restructuration is theoretically central to problem-solving research, the paper notes that empirical evidence directly supporting it as an operative mechanism is scarce.
scarcity_of_empirical_evidence_for_repre.paper: Dynamic changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving

network_science_methodology_for_quantify.name: Network science methodology for quantifying representational restructuring
network_science_methodology_for_quantify.description: The paper introduces a method to quantify mental restructuring by computing differences in semantic network metrics between pre- and post-solving phases, enabling objective measurement of an otherwise difficult-to-observe cognitive process.
network_science_methodology_for_quantify.paper: Dynamic changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving

solution_relevant_versus_irrelevant_term.name: Solution-relevant versus irrelevant term distinction in semantic network restructuring
solution_relevant_versus_irrelevant_term.description: Nodes and edges were classified as solution-relevant or irrelevant based on participant ratings of helpfulness, allowing targeted assessment of restructuration in the semantically pertinent subgraph.
solution_relevant_versus_irrelevant_term.paper: Dynamic changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving

pre_to_post_semantic_network_change_as_o.name: Pre-to-post semantic network change as operational measure of representational restructuring
pre_to_post_semantic_network_change_as_o.description: Restructuration is operationalized as the difference in semantic network metrics (edge weights, node centrality, or clustering) between networks estimated before and after a problem-solving attempt.
pre_to_post_semantic_network_change_as_o.paper: Dynamic changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving

cyber_creativity_as_a_unifying_construct.name: Cyber-creativity as a unifying construct for human-AI collaboration in creative processes
cyber_creativity_as_a_unifying_construct.description: The paper introduces the term 'cyber-creativity' to label all forms of human–AI collaboration in creative domains, providing a shared vocabulary for an emerging interdisciplinary field.
cyber_creativity_as_a_unifying_construct.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

utopian_vs_dystopian_scenario_framing_fo.name: Utopian vs. dystopian scenario framing for AI impact on human creativity
utopian_vs_dystopian_scenario_framing_fo.description: The paper structures the field around two forward-looking scenarios: a utopian vision where AI enhances human creativity, and a dystopian one where AI pre-empts human creative agency, arguing that science should steer toward the former.
utopian_vs_dystopian_scenario_framing_fo.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

need_for_a_theoretical_framework_for_cyb.name: Need for a theoretical framework for cyber-creativity
need_for_a_theoretical_framework_for_cyb.description: The paper identifies the absence of an adequate theoretical framework for cyber-creativity as the first major research challenge, noting that existing creativity theories were not designed with AI co-creation in mind.
need_for_a_theoretical_framework_for_cyb.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

sociocultural_perspectives_on_cyber_crea.name: Sociocultural perspectives on cyber-creativity as understudied domain
sociocultural_perspectives_on_cyber_crea.description: The paper highlights that sociocultural dimensions of cyber-creativity—including how cultural context shapes human-AI creative collaboration—are underexplored and represent a key research challenge.
sociocultural_perspectives_on_cyber_crea.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

characterization_of_the_cyber_creative_p.name: Characterization of the cyber-creative process
characterization_of_the_cyber_creative_p.description: Understanding how creativity unfolds when humans and AI collaborate is identified as a core research challenge, requiring new process models distinct from those developed for solo human creativity.
characterization_of_the_cyber_creative_p.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

individual_creative_agent_differences_in.name: Individual creative agent differences in cyber-creative contexts
individual_creative_agent_differences_in.description: The paper raises the challenge of understanding how individual-level factors (skills, personality, motivation) in the human agent shape the outcomes of human-AI collaboration in creativity.
individual_creative_agent_differences_in.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

co_creative_team_dynamics_in_cyber_creat.name: Co-creative team dynamics in cyber-creativity
co_creative_team_dynamics_in_cyber_creat.description: The paper identifies the composition, roles, and dynamics of co-creative teams that include both humans and AI as an underexplored research challenge.
co_creative_team_dynamics_in_cyber_creat.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

evaluation_of_cyber_creative_products.name: Evaluation of cyber-creative products
evaluation_of_cyber_creative_products.description: Assessing the quality, originality, and value of products generated through human-AI collaboration requires new metrics and evaluation frameworks, as existing creativity assessments may not apply directly.
evaluation_of_cyber_creative_products.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

domain_specificity_of_cyber_creativity_c.name: Domain-specificity of cyber-creativity challenges
domain_specificity_of_cyber_creativity_c.description: The paper identifies that cyber-creativity poses domain-specific research challenges (e.g., visual art, music, writing, science), and that findings may not generalize uniformly across domains.
domain_specificity_of_cyber_creativity_c.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

cyber_creative_education_as_a_pedagogica.name: Cyber-creative education as a pedagogical challenge
cyber_creative_education_as_a_pedagogica.description: The paper argues that educational systems must adapt to prepare learners for a world where AI is a creative collaborator, identifying this as a distinct and urgent research challenge.
cyber_creative_education_as_a_pedagogica.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

ethical_dimensions_of_cyber_creativity.name: Ethical dimensions of cyber-creativity
ethical_dimensions_of_cyber_creativity.description: The paper flags ethical challenges unique to cyber-creativity, including authorship attribution, intellectual property, informed consent, and equitable access to AI creative tools.
ethical_dimensions_of_cyber_creativity.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

dark_side_of_cyber_creativity_including_.name: Dark side of cyber-creativity including misuse and creative displacement risks
dark_side_of_cyber_creativity_including_.description: The paper identifies a 'dark side' dimension encompassing risks such as AI-generated misinformation, deepfakes, creative job displacement, and homogenization of culture, which require dedicated scientific attention.
dark_side_of_cyber_creativity_including_.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

risk_of_ai_pre_empting_human_creative_ag.name: Risk of AI pre-empting human creative agency
risk_of_ai_pre_empting_human_creative_ag.description: The paper argues that widespread AI use in creative domains could erode human creative agency over time, representing a systemic risk that the scientific community must monitor and counteract.
risk_of_ai_pre_empting_human_creative_ag.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

decalogue_of_research_challenges_as_a_st.name: Decalogue of research challenges as a structured agenda for cyber-creativity science
decalogue_of_research_challenges_as_a_st.description: The paper organizes ten research challenge dimensions into a structured agenda intended to guide future scientific work on cyber-creativity, acknowledging the agenda is non-exhaustive but serves as a starting point.
decalogue_of_research_challenges_as_a_st.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

alignment_of_ai_creative_systems_to_huma.name: Alignment of AI creative systems to human values
alignment_of_ai_creative_systems_to_huma.description: The paper expresses the ambition that cyber-creativity research should contribute to aligning AI systems with human creative values, framing this as both a scientific and normative objective.
alignment_of_ai_creative_systems_to_huma.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

high_knowledge_students_have_more_cluste.name: High-knowledge students have more clustered semantic memory networks than low-knowledge students
high_knowledge_students_have_more_cluste.description: Study 1 (N=213) found that students scoring higher on the Intro Psych Test had semantic memory networks with higher clustering and shorter distances between concepts compared to low-knowledge students, across both domain-specific (psychology) and domain-general (animal) verbal fluency tasks.
high_knowledge_students_have_more_cluste.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

high_knowledge_students_semantic_memory_.name: High-knowledge students' semantic memory networks become more interconnected over time
high_knowledge_students_semantic_memory_.description: Study 2 (N=145) longitudinally tracked students from the start to the end of a semester, finding that high-knowledge students' semantic memory networks increased in interconnectivity over time across both domain-specific and domain-general categories.
high_knowledge_students_semantic_memory_.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

verbal_fluency_tasks_as_method_for_estim.name: Verbal fluency tasks as method for estimating semantic memory networks in students
verbal_fluency_tasks_as_method_for_estim.description: Two verbal fluency tasks—domain-specific (naming psychology concepts) and domain-general (naming animals)—were used to estimate individual semantic memory networks, enabling comparison between high- and low-knowledge student groups.
verbal_fluency_tasks_as_method_for_estim.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

intro_psych_test_as_cumulative_knowledge.name: Intro Psych Test as cumulative knowledge measure for grouping students
intro_psych_test_as_cumulative_knowledge.description: A cumulative multiple-choice test of psychology knowledge (Intro Psych Test) administered at the end of an introductory psychology course was used to categorize students into high-knowledge and low-knowledge groups.
intro_psych_test_as_cumulative_knowledge.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

domain_generality_of_knowledge_related_s.name: Domain-generality of knowledge-related semantic network differences
domain_generality_of_knowledge_related_s.description: Differences in semantic memory network structure between high- and low-knowledge students were observed not only in domain-specific (psychology) networks but also in domain-general (animal) networks, suggesting that successful learning reorganizes memory beyond the studied domain.
domain_generality_of_knowledge_related_s.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

cognitive_network_science_as_tool_for_me.name: Cognitive network science as tool for measuring student learning organization
cognitive_network_science_as_tool_for_me.description: The paper demonstrates that cognitive network science methods can quantify how knowledge is organized in students' semantic memory, providing a complement to standard assessments that measure only what is known, not how it is structured.
cognitive_network_science_as_tool_for_me.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

longitudinal_semantic_network_analysis_o.name: Longitudinal semantic network analysis of undergraduate learning
longitudinal_semantic_network_analysis_o.description: Study 2 employed a longitudinal design collecting semantic network data near the start and end of a semester, enabling within-student tracking of semantic memory reorganization as a function of learning outcomes.
longitudinal_semantic_network_analysis_o.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

standard_assessments_measure_knowledge_c.name: Standard assessments measure knowledge content but not knowledge organization
standard_assessments_measure_knowledge_c.description: Multiple-choice tests and similar standard learning assessments capture what students know but do not reveal how that knowledge is organized in semantic memory, which may be a distinct and important dimension of learning.
standard_assessments_measure_knowledge_c.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

successful_learners_have_a_distinct_sema.name: Successful learners have a distinct semantic memory organization characterized by high connectivity and short path distances
successful_learners_have_a_distinct_sema.description: The authors claim that successful learners are characterized by semantic memory networks with high connectivity and short path distances between concepts, distinguishing them from less successful learners.
successful_learners_have_a_distinct_sema.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

replication_of_knowledge_group_semantic_.name: Replication of knowledge-group semantic network differences across two independent student samples
replication_of_knowledge_group_semantic_.description: The network differences between high- and low-knowledge students found in Study 1 (N=213) were replicated in Study 2 (N=145), strengthening confidence in the reliability of the findings.
replication_of_knowledge_group_semantic_.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

alpha_oscillations_increase_during_gener.name: Alpha oscillations increase during generation of more distant semantic associations
alpha_oscillations_increase_during_gener.description: In two experiments, alpha oscillatory activity was consistently higher when participants generated semantic associations more distant from a cue concept compared to less distant associations, with the effect widespread across left and right hemispheres.
alpha_oscillations_increase_during_gener.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

goal_directed_creative_instruction_incre.name: Goal-directed creative instruction increases alpha phase synchronisation from left to right temporal areas
goal_directed_creative_instruction_incre.description: When participants were instructed to 'be creative' (goal-directed) compared to generating free associations, alpha phase synchronisation increased specifically from left to right temporal brain areas, suggesting altered information flux reflecting increased top-down control of semantic search.
goal_directed_creative_instruction_incre.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

goal_directed_remote_association_relies_.name: Goal-directed remote association relies more on top-down control than free association
goal_directed_remote_association_relies_.description: The study concludes that intentionally generating remote associations (goal-directed creativity) engages greater top-down cognitive control mechanisms compared to freely generated associations, as evidenced by the left-to-right temporal alpha synchronisation pattern.
goal_directed_remote_association_relies_.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

method_distinguishing_genuine_alpha_osci.name: Method distinguishing genuine alpha oscillations from transient events applied to semantic association research
method_distinguishing_genuine_alpha_osci.description: The study employed an analytical approach that separates genuine sustained alpha oscillatory activity from transient burst events, providing a more precise characterisation of alpha dynamics during free and goal-directed semantic association generation.
method_distinguishing_genuine_alpha_osci.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

comparison_of_free_versus_goal_directed_.name: Comparison of free versus goal-directed association as experimental manipulation for studying creative intention
comparison_of_free_versus_goal_directed_.description: Experiment 2 directly compared alpha activity during free association generation versus generation with the explicit instruction to be creative, enabling isolation of the neural correlates of creative intention independent of association distance.
comparison_of_free_versus_goal_directed_.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

role_of_alpha_oscillations_in_remote_sem.name: Role of alpha oscillations in remote semantic association generation as understudied gap
role_of_alpha_oscillations_in_remote_sem.description: Despite alpha's established role in attention, working memory, and semantic memory, its specific contribution to generating remote semantic associations—key to creative cognition—remained poorly characterised prior to this work.
role_of_alpha_oscillations_in_remote_sem.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

alpha_oscillations_link_to_semantic_memo.name: Alpha oscillations link to semantic memory and creative thinking
alpha_oscillations_link_to_semantic_memo.description: The paper asserts that alpha oscillations play a central role in semantic memory and creative thinking, situating its novel findings within a broader established literature on alpha and higher-order cognition.
alpha_oscillations_link_to_semantic_memo.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

network_science_model_of_improvised_tona.name: Network science model of improvised tonal music melodic patterns
network_science_model_of_improvised_tona.description: The authors constructed a network model from a large corpus of transcribed improvisations where 5-pitch sequences are linked by consecutive occurrences, yielding 116,403 nodes and 157,429 edges, to represent the hidden structure of melodic patterns in expert improvised music.
network_science_model_of_improvised_tona.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

melodic_distance_in_pitch_sequence_netwo.name: Melodic distance in pitch-sequence network predicts perceived melodic relatedness
melodic_distance_in_pitch_sequence_netwo.description: As melodic distance within the constructed network increased, participants judged melodic sequences as less related, paralleling findings from language network research on semantic distance and relatedness judgments.
melodic_distance_in_pitch_sequence_netwo.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

quadratic_relationship_between_melodic_n.name: Quadratic relationship between melodic network distance and reaction time
quadratic_relationship_between_melodic_n.description: Reaction time to melodic relatedness judgments slowed as network distance increased up to distance four, then accelerated at greater distances, mirroring a quadratic pattern found in language network studies.
quadratic_relationship_between_melodic_n.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

human_sensitivity_to_melodic_network_dis.name: Human sensitivity to melodic network distance property
human_sensitivity_to_melodic_network_dis.description: The study claims that humans are sensitive to the property of melodic distance in the pitch-sequence network, as evidenced by both relatedness judgments and reaction time patterns in a behavioral experiment.
human_sensitivity_to_melodic_network_dis.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

music_and_language_share_analogous_compl.name: Music and language share analogous complex network structure
music_and_language_share_analogous_compl.description: The authors argue that music and language are analogous as complex systems, supported by the parallel between melodic distance effects in music networks and distance effects in language/semantic networks.
music_and_language_share_analogous_compl.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

extension_of_network_science_methodology.name: Extension of network science methodology to melodic pattern modeling
extension_of_network_science_methodology.description: The work demonstrates that network science methods developed for social and cognitive systems can be extended to quantify melodic complexity in improvised music, broadening the scope of network science applications.
extension_of_network_science_methodology.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

behavioral_paradigm_paralleling_language.name: Behavioral paradigm paralleling language network experiments applied to music
behavioral_paradigm_paralleling_language.description: A behavioral experiment modeled after paradigms used in language network research was used to test whether melodic network distance influences participants' relatedness judgments and reaction times in real-world listening contexts.
behavioral_paradigm_paralleling_language.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

5_pitch_sequence_as_the_unit_of_network_.name: 5-pitch sequence as the unit of network node for melodic representation
5_pitch_sequence_as_the_unit_of_network_.description: The researchers chose 5-pitch sequences as nodes in the melodic network, with edges defined by consecutive occurrences, as the representational unit for capturing melodic patterns in the corpus.
5_pitch_sequence_as_the_unit_of_network_.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

large_corpus_of_transcribed_improvisatio.name: Large corpus of transcribed improvisations as empirical basis for melodic network
large_corpus_of_transcribed_improvisatio.description: A large corpus of expert improvised music transcriptions served as the empirical foundation for constructing the melodic pitch-sequence network, enabling data-driven graph modeling of musical structure.
large_corpus_of_transcribed_improvisatio.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

aesthetic_emotions_are_context_specific_.name: Aesthetic emotions are context-specific relative to non-aesthetic and consumer activities
aesthetic_emotions_are_context_specific_.description: The study argues that aesthetic emotions are not unique but are distributed differently across a multidimensional emotional space compared to non-aesthetic and consumer activities, based on psychometric network comparisons across three recalled-activity conditions.
aesthetic_emotions_are_context_specific_.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

recalled_aesthetic_activities_rated_as_l.name: Recalled aesthetic activities rated as least vivid but most intense
recalled_aesthetic_activities_rated_as_l.description: Behaviorally, participants rated recollections of aesthetic activities as lower in vividness but higher in emotional intensity compared to non-aesthetic and consumer activity recollections.
recalled_aesthetic_activities_rated_as_l.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

psychometric_network_analysis_as_method_.name: Psychometric network analysis as method for mapping aesthetic emotional space
psychometric_network_analysis_as_method_.description: The study applies a computational psychometric network approach to 75 candidate aesthetic emotion ratings to represent and compare the structure of emotional spaces across activity conditions, identifying typology, communities, and core nodes.
psychometric_network_analysis_as_method_.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

quantitative_network_differences_across_.name: Quantitative network differences across aesthetic, non-aesthetic, and consumer conditions
quantitative_network_differences_across_.description: Network-level analysis revealed multiple quantitative differences across the three conditions in typology, community structure, and core nodes of the emotional networks, suggesting the three activity types evoke structurally distinct emotional spaces.
quantitative_network_differences_across_.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

75_candidate_aesthetic_emotion_rating_in.name: 75-candidate aesthetic emotion rating instrument as empirical basis
75_candidate_aesthetic_emotion_rating_in.description: The study employs a curated set of 75 candidate aesthetic emotions rated by a large, diverse sample, providing a broad empirical basis for psychometric network construction and comparison across activity contexts.
75_candidate_aesthetic_emotion_rating_in.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

whether_aesthetic_emotions_are_qualitati.name: Whether aesthetic emotions are qualitatively unique or context-dependent variants of general emotions
whether_aesthetic_emotions_are_qualitati.description: The paper directly addresses the debate over whether aesthetic emotions are a categorically distinct class or whether they represent context-specific configurations of broader emotional responses evoked by any engaging activity.
whether_aesthetic_emotions_are_qualitati.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

aesthetic_activities_evoke_a_distinct_mu.name: Aesthetic activities evoke a distinct multidimensional emotional space
aesthetic_activities_evoke_a_distinct_mu.description: Based on the network results, the authors propose that aesthetic emotions are distributed differently in a multidimensional aesthetic space compared to non-aesthetic and consumer activities, implying a structurally distinct affective signature for aesthetic experience.
aesthetic_activities_evoke_a_distinct_mu.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

recalled_activity_paradigm_as_experiment.name: Recalled activity paradigm as experimental design for studying aesthetic emotions
recalled_activity_paradigm_as_experiment.description: Participants were asked to recall engaging aesthetic, non-aesthetic, or consumer activities and then rate 75 emotions, enabling a between-condition comparison of emotional profiles without requiring laboratory stimulus presentation.
recalled_activity_paradigm_as_experiment.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

semantic_flow_as_a_novel_discourse_level.name: Semantic flow as a novel discourse-level metric for characterizing lexical-semantic access in aphasia
semantic_flow_as_a_novel_discourse_level.description: The study introduces word-by-word semantic flow (taxonomic and thematic semantic distance across running word pairs) as a novel metric for characterizing how controlled lexical-semantic retrieval deficits compound over the course of narrative production in people with aphasia.
semantic_flow_as_a_novel_discourse_level.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

reduced_taxonomic_and_thematic_semantic_.name: Reduced taxonomic and thematic semantic distance in aphasia narratives relative to controls
reduced_taxonomic_and_thematic_semantic_.description: People with chronic post-stroke aphasia (n=259) showed significantly lower both taxonomic and thematic inter-word semantic distances across their narratives compared to age-matched controls (n=203), based on 232,490 word transitions.
reduced_taxonomic_and_thematic_semantic_.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

taxonomic_and_thematic_semantic_distance.name: Taxonomic and thematic semantic distance as predictors of semantic impairment and aphasia severity
taxonomic_and_thematic_semantic_distance.description: Both taxonomic and thematic semantic distance metrics computed from narrative word transitions were strongly predictive of offline measures of semantic impairment and overall aphasia severity in the sample.
taxonomic_and_thematic_semantic_distance.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

perseveration_as_a_confound_in_aphasia_n.name: Perseveration as a confound in aphasia narrative semantic flow
perseveration_as_a_confound_in_aphasia_n.description: People with aphasia often produce perseverative language output (repetitions), which may artificially reduce inter-word semantic distances; the study conducted additional analyses excluding repetitions to test whether group differences were driven by perseveration.
perseveration_as_a_confound_in_aphasia_n.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

cumulative_compounding_of_controlled_wor.name: Cumulative compounding of controlled word retrieval deficits over narrative production
cumulative_compounding_of_controlled_wor.description: The study argues that deficits in controlled lexical-semantic retrieval characteristic of aphasia do not operate in isolation but compound across the multiword span of narratives, reducing semantic flow at the discourse level.
cumulative_compounding_of_controlled_wor.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

taxonomic_versus_thematic_semantic_dista.name: Taxonomic versus thematic semantic distance as distinct dimensions of narrative semantic flow
taxonomic_versus_thematic_semantic_dista.description: The study computed two types of inter-word semantic distance—taxonomic (category-based) and thematic (contextual/event-based)—for every running word pair in transcribed narratives, enabling a dissociable characterization of semantic flow.
taxonomic_versus_thematic_semantic_dista.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

large_scale_aphasia_narrative_corpus_as_.name: Large-scale aphasia narrative corpus as empirical basis for semantic flow analysis
large_scale_aphasia_narrative_corpus_as_.description: The study assembled transcribed storytelling narratives from 259 individuals with chronic post-stroke aphasia and 203 age-matched controls, yielding 232,490 inter-word transitions for analysis.
large_scale_aphasia_narrative_corpus_as_.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

extension_of_aphasiological_methods_from.name: Extension of aphasiological methods from single-word to multiword discourse level
extension_of_aphasiological_methods_from.description: Whereas aphasiology has historically focused on single-word dissociations, this work extends the analysis to combinatorial semantic processes at the narrative level, characterizing discourse-level integrity of lexical-semantic access.
extension_of_aphasiological_methods_from.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

bigram_semantic_distance_as_an_index_of_.name: Bigram semantic distance as an index of continuous semantic flow
bigram_semantic_distance_as_an_index_of_.description: The paper proposes that computing semantic distance over ordered bigrams extracted from continuous language samples yields a metric of conceptual dispersion and topic flow, bridging single-word and discourse-level analyses.
bigram_semantic_distance_as_an_index_of_.paper: Bigram Semantic Distance as an Index of Continuous Semantic Flow in Natural Language: Theory, Tools, and Applications

sentence_boundary_punctuation_by_semanti.name: Sentence boundary punctuation by semantic distance jumps
sentence_boundary_punctuation_by_semanti.description: An application to Jack London's 'To Build a Fire' showed that bigrams spanning sentence boundaries exhibit higher semantic distances compared to within-sentence bigrams, suggesting sentences demarcate conceptual discontinuities.
sentence_boundary_punctuation_by_semanti.paper: Bigram Semantic Distance as an Index of Continuous Semantic Flow in Natural Language: Theory, Tools, and Applications

validation_of_bigram_semantic_distance_o.name: Validation of bigram semantic distance on simulated verbal fluency data
validation_of_bigram_semantic_distance_o.description: The authors validated the bigram distance metric by applying it to a simulated verbal fluency stream containing alternating semantic clusters (animals, musical instruments, fruit), demonstrating that the metric predicts switch markers between clusters.
validation_of_bigram_semantic_distance_o.paper: Bigram Semantic Distance as an Index of Continuous Semantic Flow in Natural Language: Theory, Tools, and Applications

semdistflow_r_package_for_bigram_semanti.name: semdistflow R package for bigram semantic distance computation
semdistflow_r_package_for_bigram_semanti.description: The paper introduces an R package ('semdistflow') that transforms any user-specified language transcript into an ordered vector of bigrams and appends two semantic distance metrics to each pair.
semdistflow_r_package_for_bigram_semanti.paper: Bigram Semantic Distance as an Index of Continuous Semantic Flow in Natural Language: Theory, Tools, and Applications

bigram_distance_norms_on_a_large_text_sa.name: Bigram distance norms on a large text sample
bigram_distance_norms_on_a_large_text_sa.description: The authors generated bigram semantic distance norms from a large corpus of text to serve as a reference baseline for future applications of the technique.
bigram_distance_norms_on_a_large_text_sa.paper: Bigram Semantic Distance as an Index of Continuous Semantic Flow in Natural Language: Theory, Tools, and Applications

application_of_bigram_semantic_distance_.name: Application of bigram semantic distance to classic short fiction
application_of_bigram_semantic_distance_.description: The technique is demonstrated on Jack London's 'To Build a Fire' (1908), illustrating how semantic distance profiles characterize narrative flow and conceptual cohesion across a complete literary text.
application_of_bigram_semantic_distance_.paper: Bigram Semantic Distance as an Index of Continuous Semantic Flow in Natural Language: Theory, Tools, and Applications

bridging_single_word_semantics_and_macro.name: Bridging single-word semantics and macroscale discourse analysis via bigram distance
bridging_single_word_semantics_and_macro.description: A core objective of the paper is to connect word-level semantic space models with broader discourse-level analyses using the bigram distance approach as an intermediate metric.
bridging_single_word_semantics_and_macro.paper: Bigram Semantic Distance as an Index of Continuous Semantic Flow in Natural Language: Theory, Tools, and Applications

two_metrics_of_semantic_distance_appende.name: Two metrics of semantic distance appended per bigram
two_metrics_of_semantic_distance_appende.description: The semdistflow package appends two distinct semantic distance metrics to each ordered bigram, allowing alternative representations of conceptual proximity between consecutive words.
two_metrics_of_semantic_distance_appende.paper: Bigram Semantic Distance as an Index of Continuous Semantic Flow in Natural Language: Theory, Tools, and Applications

potential_of_bigram_semantic_distance_fo.name: Potential of bigram semantic distance for characterizing semantic processing in clinical and real-world narratives
potential_of_bigram_semantic_distance_fo.description: The authors discuss the promise of bigram semantic distance for characterizing semantic processing in real-world and potentially clinical narratives, extending its utility beyond laboratory paradigms.
potential_of_bigram_semantic_distance_fo.paper: Bigram Semantic Distance as an Index of Continuous Semantic Flow in Natural Language: Theory, Tools, and Applications

environmental_scene_and_body_posture_int.name: Environmental scene and body posture interaction on AUT performance
environmental_scene_and_body_posture_int.description: The study demonstrates that environmental context and body posture interact to shape performance on the Alternative Uses Task, such that body posture facilitates fluent responding specifically under high-fixedness environmental contexts.
environmental_scene_and_body_posture_int.paper: The Effects of Environmental Scene and Body Posture on Embodied Strategies in Creative Thinking

away_from_body_action_descriptions_incre.name: Away-from-body action descriptions increase AUT originality
away_from_body_action_descriptions_incre.description: Instructing participants to describe actions directed away from the body (versus toward the body or general instructions) results in more original responses on the Alternative Uses Task.
away_from_body_action_descriptions_incre.paper: The Effects of Environmental Scene and Body Posture on Embodied Strategies in Creative Thinking

replication_of_creativity_level_differen.name: Replication of creativity-level differences in embodied strategy use on AUT
replication_of_creativity_level_differen.description: The study replicates prior findings that higher-creative individuals use different embodied strategies than lower-creative individuals when generating creative uses for objects on the AUT.
replication_of_creativity_level_differen.paper: The Effects of Environmental Scene and Body Posture on Embodied Strategies in Creative Thinking

abstract_action_space_navigation_as_mech.name: Abstract action space navigation as mechanism for higher-creative embodied cognition
abstract_action_space_navigation_as_mech.description: The authors propose that higher-creative individuals differentially access and navigate an abstract 'action space' necessary for simulating creative uses of objects, accounting for their distinct embodied strategy profiles.
abstract_action_space_navigation_as_mech.paper: The Effects of Environmental Scene and Body Posture on Embodied Strategies in Creative Thinking

body_posture_manipulation_as_experimenta.name: Body posture manipulation as experimental tool for studying embodied creativity
body_posture_manipulation_as_experimenta.description: The study adopts a previously validated body posture manipulation (toward vs. away from body) while participants perform the AUT, allowing direct investigation of how motor states influence creative ideation.
body_posture_manipulation_as_experimenta.paper: The Effects of Environmental Scene and Body Posture on Embodied Strategies in Creative Thinking

high_fixedness_environmental_context_as_.name: High-fixedness environmental context as boundary condition for posture effects
high_fixedness_environmental_context_as_.description: The facilitating effect of body posture on AUT fluency is specific to high-fixedness environmental scenes, indicating that contextual fixedness moderates the relationship between motor state and creative output.
high_fixedness_environmental_context_as_.paper: The Effects of Environmental Scene and Body Posture on Embodied Strategies in Creative Thinking

embodied_cognition_framework_for_aut_cre.name: Embodied cognition framework for AUT creative use generation
embodied_cognition_framework_for_aut_cre.description: The theoretical basis of the study holds that environmental scene and motor information are used in neural simulation when generating creative uses for manipulable objects, drawing on accounts of embodied cognition.
embodied_cognition_framework_for_aut_cre.paper: The Effects of Environmental Scene and Body Posture on Embodied Strategies in Creative Thinking

general_vs_action_directed_aut_instructi.name: General vs. action-directed AUT instruction as experimental manipulation
general_vs_action_directed_aut_instructi.description: Participants performed the AUT under either general instructions or instructions to specifically describe actions toward or away from the body, allowing comparison of how action-direction framing shapes creative output.
general_vs_action_directed_aut_instructi.paper: The Effects of Environmental Scene and Body Posture on Embodied Strategies in Creative Thinking

creativity_as_protective_factor_for_sema.name: creativity_as_protective_factor_for_semantic_memory_flexibility_in_aging
creativity_as_protective_factor_for_sema.description: Higher creative older adults preserve overall semantic memory network flexibility compared to lower creative older adults, suggesting creativity may buffer against age-related semantic rigidity. The study frames this as a potential protective role of creativity for active ageing.
creativity_as_protective_factor_for_sema.paper: Creativity, the fountain of youth: Association between creativity and semantic memory networks across the lifespan.

age_related_decrease_in_creative_perform.name: age_related_decrease_in_creative_performance_linked_to_semantic_network_rigidity
age_related_decrease_in_creative_perform.description: Older adults (M=77.8 years) showed decreased divergent thinking performance relative to younger adults (M=20.3 years), and this decline was associated with more rigid semantic memory network structures estimated from verbal fluency and free association tasks.
age_related_decrease_in_creative_perform.paper: Creativity, the fountain of youth: Association between creativity and semantic memory networks across the lifespan.

verbal_production_tasks_as_method_for_es.name: verbal_production_tasks_as_method_for_estimating_semantic_memory_networks_across_age_groups
verbal_production_tasks_as_method_for_es.description: Four verbal production tasks—two verbal fluency tasks and two free association tasks—were used to estimate individual semantic memory networks in both older and younger adult participants, enabling network-level comparison across age and creativity groups.
verbal_production_tasks_as_method_for_es.paper: Creativity, the fountain of youth: Association between creativity and semantic memory networks across the lifespan.

creative_older_adults_resemble_low_creat.name: creative_older_adults_resemble_low_creative_young_adults_in_semantic_network_flexibility
creative_older_adults_resemble_low_creat.description: The semantic memory network flexibility of higher creative older adults was found to be similar to that of lower creative younger adults, suggesting creativity moderates but does not eliminate age-related network changes.
creative_older_adults_resemble_low_creat.paper: Creativity, the fountain of youth: Association between creativity and semantic memory networks across the lifespan.

divergent_thinking_as_measure_of_creativ.name: divergent_thinking_as_measure_of_creative_performance_in_lifespan_aging_study
divergent_thinking_as_measure_of_creativ.description: Two Alternative Uses Task variants were administered to assess creative performance in both older (n=77) and younger (n=81) adults, providing the criterion measure for grouping participants by creativity level.
divergent_thinking_as_measure_of_creativ.paper: Creativity, the fountain of youth: Association between creativity and semantic memory networks across the lifespan.

propaedeutic_role_of_creativity_in_activ.name: propaedeutic_role_of_creativity_in_active_ageing_via_semantic_network_maintenance
propaedeutic_role_of_creativity_in_activ.description: The study proposes that creative engagement may serve a propaedeutic (preparatory/preventive) function in active ageing by maintaining flexible organization and access to semantic memory structures across the lifespan.
propaedeutic_role_of_creativity_in_activ.paper: Creativity, the fountain of youth: Association between creativity and semantic memory networks across the lifespan.

connector_hubs_facilitate_more_novel_and.name: connector_hubs_facilitate_more_novel_and_remote_ideas_than_provincial_hubs_in_aut
connector_hubs_facilitate_more_novel_and.description: Across four experiments, cue words that are connector hubs (C-hubs) in the semantic network elicited more novel and remote alternative uses than provincial hubs (P-hubs), demonstrating that inter-module connectivity of a concept facilitates creative ideation.
connector_hubs_facilitate_more_novel_and.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

c_hub_advantage_is_concentrated_in_early.name: c_hub_advantage_is_concentrated_in_early_stage_of_creative_thinking
c_hub_advantage_is_concentrated_in_early.description: The facilitation of novel idea generation by connector hubs is predominantly observed in the early stage of the creative process, as indexed by changes in brain activation and functional connectivity early in idea generation.
c_hub_advantage_is_concentrated_in_early.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

connector_vs_provincial_hub_distinction_.name: connector_vs_provincial_hub_distinction_based_on_intra_and_intermodule_connections_in_semantic_network
connector_vs_provincial_hub_distinction_.description: C-hubs and P-hubs were identified from a large-scale semantic network by analyzing their intra-module versus inter-module connection patterns, likely using participation coefficient, to classify concepts by their bridging role across semantic modules.
connector_vs_provincial_hub_distinction_.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

dmn_and_fpn_core_regions_underlie_c_hub_.name: dmn_and_fpn_core_regions_underlie_c_hub_creative_advantage
dmn_and_fpn_core_regions_underlie_c_hub_.description: Neural evidence from brain activation and functional connectivity analyses implicated core regions of the default mode network and frontoparietal network—including dorsolateral prefrontal cortex, angular gyrus, and precuneus—as the neural substrate of C-hub-driven creative superiority.
dmn_and_fpn_core_regions_underlie_c_hub_.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

c_hub_superior_performance_depends_on_in.name: c_hub_superior_performance_depends_on_interaction_of_spreading_activation_controlled_retrieval_and_attentional_regulation
c_hub_superior_performance_depends_on_in.description: The study proposes that connector hubs benefit creative thinking through stronger interactions among automatic spreading activation, controlled semantic retrieval, and attentional regulation of salient information, as supported by the neural findings.
c_hub_superior_performance_depends_on_in.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

modular_structure_of_semantic_network_bo.name: modular_structure_of_semantic_network_both_facilitates_routine_retrieval_and_impedes_creativity
modular_structure_of_semantic_network_bo.description: The paper argues that the modular organization of semantic memory—dense intra-module and sparse inter-module connections—supports efficient information storage and retrieval but constrains creative thinking that requires inter-module concept bridging.
modular_structure_of_semantic_network_bo.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

hub_type_as_cue_word_property_in_aut_as_.name: hub_type_as_cue_word_property_in_aut_as_experimental_method
hub_type_as_cue_word_property_in_aut_as_.description: Connector hubs and provincial hubs identified from a large-scale semantic network were used as cue words in the Alternative Uses Task across four experiments combining behavioral and neuroimaging measures.
hub_type_as_cue_word_property_in_aut_as_.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

large_scale_semantic_network_analysis_as.name: large_scale_semantic_network_analysis_as_empirical_basis_for_hub_identification
large_scale_semantic_network_analysis_as.description: The study analyzed a large-scale semantic network to derive hub concepts classified by their inter- versus intra-module connectivity, providing an empirical foundation for the experimental stimuli and theoretical interpretation.
large_scale_semantic_network_analysis_as.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

participation_coefficient_as_operational.name: participation_coefficient_as_operationalization_of_connector_hub_status_in_semantic_network
participation_coefficient_as_operational.description: The study operationalizes connector hub status using participation coefficient or an analogous inter-module connectivity metric derived from community detection on the semantic network, distinguishing C-hubs (high inter-module) from P-hubs (high intra-module).
participation_coefficient_as_operational.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

forward_flow_conceptual_clarification_ag.name: Forward flow conceptual clarification against Rossiter critique
forward_flow_conceptual_clarification_ag.description: The authors argue that Rossiter's (2020) conceptual and measurement concerns about forward flow rest on fundamental misunderstandings of the aims and approach behind the measure, and that these concerns are therefore specious.
forward_flow_conceptual_clarification_ag.paper: Clarifying what forward flow is (and isn't): Reply to Rossiter (2020).

forward_flow_robustness_to_rossiter_meas.name: Forward flow robustness to Rossiter measurement concerns
forward_flow_robustness_to_rossiter_meas.description: The reply asserts that the specific measurement concerns raised by Rossiter about forward flow do not undermine the validity of the measure as operationalized in Gray et al. (2019).
forward_flow_robustness_to_rossiter_meas.paper: Clarifying what forward flow is (and isn't): Reply to Rossiter (2020).

creativity_predicts_associative_learning.name: creativity_predicts_associative_learning_outcomes
creativity_predicts_associative_learning.description: Students higher in creativity learn more words on a foreign language learning test 24 hours later, suggesting creativity contributes to academic learning outcomes beyond general intelligence.
creativity_predicts_associative_learning.paper: Creativity supports learning through associative thinking

associative_thinking_mediates_creativity.name: associative_thinking_mediates_creativity_learning_link
associative_thinking_mediates_creativity.description: Associative thinking—the ability to generate novel word associations—statistically mediates the relationship between creativity and associative learning in Study 2, suggesting it is a shared cognitive mechanism.
associative_thinking_mediates_creativity.paper: Creativity supports learning through associative thinking

novel_word_associations_predict_foreign_.name: novel_word_associations_predict_foreign_language_learning
novel_word_associations_predict_foreign_.description: In Study 1, students who generated more novel word associations in an associative thinking task learned more foreign language words on a test 24 hours later.
novel_word_associations_predict_foreign_.paper: Creativity supports learning through associative thinking

naturalistic_creativity_tasks_extend_the.name: naturalistic_creativity_tasks_extend_the_creativity_learning_effect
naturalistic_creativity_tasks_extend_the.description: Study 2 replicates and extends the creativity-learning relationship using naturalistic creativity tasks—writing short stories and sketching line drawings—rather than laboratory measures alone.
naturalistic_creativity_tasks_extend_the.paper: Creativity supports learning through associative thinking

associative_thinking_as_shared_cognitive.name: associative_thinking_as_shared_cognitive_mechanism_between_creativity_and_learning
associative_thinking_as_shared_cognitive.description: The paper proposes that a shared capacity for making new connections—operationalized as associative thinking—underlies both creative performance and associative learning, explaining their observed correlation.
associative_thinking_as_shared_cognitive.paper: Creativity supports learning through associative thinking

creativity_as_predictor_of_academic_lear.name: creativity_as_predictor_of_academic_learning_outcomes
creativity_as_predictor_of_academic_lear.description: The paper frames creativity as a consistent predictor of academic learning outcomes, motivating investigation of the cognitive mechanisms involved.
creativity_as_predictor_of_academic_lear.paper: Creativity supports learning through associative thinking

two_study_replication_design_for_creativ.name: two_study_replication_design_for_creativity_learning_mechanism
two_study_replication_design_for_creativ.description: The research employs two studies: Study 1 uses associative thinking and foreign language word learning with a 24-hour delay; Study 2 replicates using naturalistic creativity tasks (stories, drawings) and tests mediation.
two_study_replication_design_for_creativ.paper: Creativity supports learning through associative thinking

question_asking_as_a_cognitive_mechanism.name: Question asking as a cognitive mechanism for creative ideation
question_asking_as_a_cognitive_mechanism.description: The paper argues that asking questions is a likely cognitive mechanism that supports creative ideation by helping define problems and facilitating creative problem solving, and that this mechanism deserves increased empirical attention.
question_asking_as_a_cognitive_mechanism.paper: Applying Text Mining to Analyze Human Question Asking in Creativity Research

text_mining_as_a_method_for_measuring_co.name: Text mining as a method for measuring cognitive potential of questions
text_mining_as_a_method_for_measuring_co.description: The paper proposes applying text mining and natural language processing methods to quantify properties of questions—including question type, question complexity, and answer content—as a way to assess their cognitive potential in creativity research.
text_mining_as_a_method_for_measuring_co.paper: Applying Text Mining to Analyze Human Question Asking in Creativity Research

question_type_as_a_measurable_dimension_.name: Question type as a measurable dimension of question cognitive potential
question_type_as_a_measurable_dimension_.description: The paper identifies question type as one of three core dimensions used in the text mining framework to assess the cognitive potential of questions in creativity contexts.
question_type_as_a_measurable_dimension_.paper: Applying Text Mining to Analyze Human Question Asking in Creativity Research

question_complexity_as_a_measurable_dime.name: Question complexity as a measurable dimension of question cognitive potential
question_complexity_as_a_measurable_dime.description: The paper uses question complexity—framed within frameworks such as Bloom's taxonomy—as one of the key text-mining dimensions for evaluating questions in creativity research.
question_complexity_as_a_measurable_dime.paper: Applying Text Mining to Analyze Human Question Asking in Creativity Research

answer_content_as_a_measurable_dimension.name: Answer content as a measurable dimension of question cognitive potential
answer_content_as_a_measurable_dimension.description: The paper incorporates the content of answers to questions as a third dimension in the text mining framework, alongside question type and complexity.
answer_content_as_a_measurable_dimension.paper: Applying Text Mining to Analyze Human Question Asking in Creativity Research

history_of_question_mining_in_creativity.name: History of question mining in creativity research
history_of_question_mining_in_creativity.description: The paper reviews and summarizes the historical development of question mining as a sub-field within creativity research, providing an organized background for current NLP approaches.
history_of_question_mining_in_creativity.paper: Applying Text Mining to Analyze Human Question Asking in Creativity Research

nlp_methods_applied_to_five_creativity_r.name: NLP methods applied to five creativity-relevant question datasets
nlp_methods_applied_to_five_creativity_r.description: The paper implements and applies the proposed NLP text mining approach to five separate datasets, obtaining results that are comprehensively analyzed to assess the role of natural language processing in creativity research.
nlp_methods_applied_to_five_creativity_r.paper: Applying Text Mining to Analyze Human Question Asking in Creativity Research

nlp_has_a_role_to_play_in_creativity_res.name: NLP has a role to play in creativity research on question asking
nlp_has_a_role_to_play_in_creativity_res.description: The paper concludes from its experimental results that natural language processing methods are useful or helpful for studying question asking in creativity research, offering new measurement possibilities beyond traditional manual approaches.
nlp_has_a_role_to_play_in_creativity_res.paper: Applying Text Mining to Analyze Human Question Asking in Creativity Research

unknown_exact_role_of_questions_in_creat.name: Unknown exact role of questions in creativity as motivation for study
unknown_exact_role_of_questions_in_creat.description: Despite growing empirical attention to question asking, the paper identifies that much remains unknown about the exact cognitive role questions play in creativity, motivating the development of new measurement approaches.
unknown_exact_role_of_questions_in_creat.paper: Applying Text Mining to Analyze Human Question Asking in Creativity Research

dissociation_between_objective_originali.name: Dissociation between objective originality and originality self-assessment in AUT
dissociation_between_objective_originali.description: Objective originality in the AUT was predicted by broadly connected semantic networks that avoided tightly knit clusters, whereas originality judgments (self-assessments) were predicted by highly integrated and efficient semantic networks, revealing a dissociation between performance and metacognition.

semantic_network_integration_predicts_au.name: Semantic network integration predicts AUT originality self-assessment
semantic_network_integration_predicts_au.description: Individuals with more integrated and efficient semantic memory networks gave higher originality ratings to their own AUT responses, linking network-level integration to metacognitive accuracy or bias in evaluating creative output.

broad_connectivity_and_low_clustering_pr.name: Broad connectivity and low clustering predict objective AUT originality
broad_connectivity_and_low_clustering_pr.description: Objective originality scores in the AUT were predicted by semantic memory networks characterized by broad connectivity and avoidance of tightly knit clusters, consistent with theories linking flexible semantic search to creative output.

aut_fluency_as_a_consistent_predictor_of.name: AUT fluency as a consistent predictor of both creative performance and originality judgments
aut_fluency_as_a_consistent_predictor_of.description: The number of ideas generated in the AUT (fluency) was a consistently strong predictor of both objective creative performance and individuals' self-assessments of originality, suggesting fluency underlies both production and metacognitive evaluation.

individual_semantic_network_construction.name: Individual semantic network construction via relatedness judgment task for creativity and metacognition research
individual_semantic_network_construction.description: The study constructed individual-level semantic memory networks for N=106 participants using the relatedness judgment task, yielding measures of network integration (efficiency), local connectivity, and community structure for relating to both AUT performance and metacognitive originality judgments.

semantic_network_community_structure_rel.name: Semantic network community structure relates to creative metacognition
semantic_network_community_structure_rel.description: Community structure of individual semantic memory networks was included as a metric alongside efficiency and local connectivity, contributing to the explanation of either objective originality or originality self-assessment in the AUT.

metacognitive_perspective_on_creative_ev.name: Metacognitive perspective on creative evaluation as understudied domain
metacognitive_perspective_on_creative_ev.description: The paper identifies that while creative idea generation has received extensive research attention, creative idea evaluation—especially the metacognitive processes by which individuals assess the originality of their own ideas—remains poorly understood.

knowledge_structures_relate_to_metacogni.name: Knowledge structures relate to metacognitive processes in creative thinking
knowledge_structures_relate_to_metacogni.description: The paper claims that understanding how semantic memory network structure relates to metacognitive processes accompanying creative thinking can further elucidate the complexity of creativity, motivating the use of network metrics to predict both performance and self-evaluation.

touch_imagery_vividness_positively_corre.name: touch imagery vividness positively correlates with creative writing performance
touch_imagery_vividness_positively_corre.description: A behavioral study found a positive correlation between the vividness of touch (haptic) mental imagery and performance on a creative writing task, suggesting sensory imagery quality contributes to creative output.
touch_imagery_vividness_positively_corre.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

touch_imagery_vividness_facilitates_crea.name: touch imagery vividness facilitates creative writing via semantic integration and reorganization
touch_imagery_vividness_facilitates_crea.description: Using semantic feature indicators derived from writing texts and mediation models, the study found that touch imagery vividness enhances creative writing by promoting semantic integration and reorganization of semantic memory.
touch_imagery_vividness_facilitates_crea.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

semantic_reorganization_plays_a_greater_.name: semantic reorganization plays a greater role than semantic integration in imagery-enhanced creativity
semantic_reorganization_plays_a_greater_.description: A behavioral study comparing mental imagery and semantic understanding strategies in creative writing confirmed imagery's positive impact and indicated that semantic reorganization, beyond semantic integration, is the critical mechanism.
semantic_reorganization_plays_a_greater_.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

sensorimotor_network_supports_sensorimot.name: sensorimotor network supports sensorimotor simulations during imagery-involved creative cognition
sensorimotor_network_supports_sensorimot.description: An fMRI study examining edge communities of functional brain networks during creative writing under mental imagery conditions found that the sensorimotor network facilitates sensorimotor simulations in creative cognition.
sensorimotor_network_supports_sensorimot.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

dorsal_attention_and_salience_networks_c.name: dorsal attention and salience networks collaboratively support goal-directed and reorienting attention in creative writing
dorsal_attention_and_salience_networks_c.description: The fMRI study identified that dorsal attention and salience networks work together during creative writing by maintaining goal-directed attention and reorienting attention as needed.
dorsal_attention_and_salience_networks_c.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

limbic_network_supports_multimodal_seman.name: limbic network supports multimodal semantic processing and novel associations in creative cognition
limbic_network_supports_multimodal_seman.description: The fMRI edge community analysis revealed that the limbic network plays a role in multimodal semantic processing and forming novel associations during imagery-involved creative writing.
limbic_network_supports_multimodal_seman.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

frontoparietal_control_network_and_dmn_c.name: frontoparietal control network and DMN contribute to information integration during creative writing
frontoparietal_control_network_and_dmn_c.description: The fMRI study found that the frontoparietal control network and default mode network jointly contribute to integrating information during creative writing under mental imagery conditions.
frontoparietal_control_network_and_dmn_c.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

dmn_subnetwork_plays_a_special_role_in_i.name: DMN subnetwork plays a special role in integrating semantic information related to objects and actions
dmn_subnetwork_plays_a_special_role_in_i.description: Within the default mode network, a specific subnetwork was identified in the fMRI data as uniquely important for integrating semantic information about objects and actions during creative writing.
dmn_subnetwork_plays_a_special_role_in_i.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

mental_imagery_strategy_use_outperforms_.name: mental imagery strategy use outperforms semantic understanding strategy use in creative writing
mental_imagery_strategy_use_outperforms_.description: A behavioral comparison study directly contrasting mental imagery and semantic understanding strategies found that mental imagery has a positive impact on creative cognition outcomes in creative writing tasks.
mental_imagery_strategy_use_outperforms_.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

mental_imagery_role_in_creativity_as_und.name: mental imagery role in creativity as understudied from a semantic memory perspective
mental_imagery_role_in_creativity_as_und.description: The paper identifies that while the role of mental imagery in creative cognition is acknowledged, the specific cognitive and neural mechanisms, particularly from a semantic memory perspective, remain underexplored.
mental_imagery_role_in_creativity_as_und.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

semantic_feature_indicators_derived_from.name: semantic feature indicators derived from writing texts as method for quantifying integration and reorganization
semantic_feature_indicators_derived_from.description: The study develops semantic feature indicators extracted from creative writing texts to operationalize and measure semantic integration and reorganization as mediators of the imagery–creativity relationship.
semantic_feature_indicators_derived_from.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

fmri_edge_community_analysis_of_function.name: fMRI edge community analysis of functional brain networks during creative writing
fmri_edge_community_analysis_of_function.description: An fMRI paradigm is used to map the distribution of functional brain network edge communities during creative writing under mental imagery versus semantic understanding conditions to identify network-level substrates.
fmri_edge_community_analysis_of_function.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

neural_dynamics_differ_between_creative_.name: neural_dynamics_differ_between_creative_idea_generation_and_evaluation
neural_dynamics_differ_between_creative_.description: Generating creative ideas led to significantly higher network reconfiguration than generating non-creative ideas, whereas evaluating creative and non-creative ideas led to similar levels of network integration, demonstrating distinct neural dynamics for generation versus evaluation phases.
neural_dynamics_differ_between_creative_.paper: Neural Dynamics During the Generation and Evaluation of Creative and Non-Creative Ideas

within_subjects_fmri_design_for_creative.name: within_subjects_fmri_design_for_creative_generation_and_evaluation
within_subjects_fmri_design_for_creative.description: Participants completed two fMRI sessions one week apart: in session one they generated creative (alternative uses) or non-creative (common characteristics) responses; in session two they evaluated their own prior creative and non-creative responses to the same objects, enabling direct within-subject comparison of all four conditions.
within_subjects_fmri_design_for_creative.paper: Neural Dynamics During the Generation and Evaluation of Creative and Non-Creative Ideas

network_reconfiguration_as_neural_marker.name: network_reconfiguration_as_neural_marker_of_creative_idea_generation
network_reconfiguration_as_neural_marker.description: Brain network reconfiguration—not integration—is the key neural dynamic distinguishing creative from non-creative idea generation, driven by differential patterns across the executive control, default mode, and salience networks.
network_reconfiguration_as_neural_marker.paper: Neural Dynamics During the Generation and Evaluation of Creative and Non-Creative Ideas

evaluation_phase_relies_on_similar_neura.name: evaluation_phase_relies_on_similar_neural_integration_regardless_of_idea_creativity
evaluation_phase_relies_on_similar_neura.description: When participants evaluated their own previously generated ideas, the level of brain network integration was similar whether the ideas were creative or non-creative, suggesting evaluation recruits a common neural mechanism independent of idea type.
evaluation_phase_relies_on_similar_neura.paper: Neural Dynamics During the Generation and Evaluation of Creative and Non-Creative Ideas

first_within_subject_demonstration_of_ne.name: first_within_subject_demonstration_of_neural_dynamics_in_creative_generation_vs_evaluation
first_within_subject_demonstration_of_ne.description: This study is claimed to be the first to directly compare within-subject neural dynamics across all four conditions of generating and evaluating creative and non-creative ideas using state-of-the-art network neuroscience methodologies.
first_within_subject_demonstration_of_ne.paper: Neural Dynamics During the Generation and Evaluation of Creative and Non-Creative Ideas

executive_control_default_mode_and_salie.name: executive_control_default_mode_and_salience_network_interaction_as_marker_of_creative_ability
executive_control_default_mode_and_salie.description: The interaction between executive control, default mode, and salience brain networks is an established marker of individual differences in creative ability, providing the theoretical backdrop for examining dynamic recruitment during generation and evaluation.
executive_control_default_mode_and_salie.paper: Neural Dynamics During the Generation and Evaluation of Creative and Non-Creative Ideas

understudied_neural_dynamics_of_generati.name: understudied_neural_dynamics_of_generation_versus_evaluation_in_creativity
understudied_neural_dynamics_of_generati.description: Despite progress in understanding neural correlates of creative thinking, how brain systems are dynamically recruited during the two key components—generation and evaluation of ideas—remains far from understood, motivating the current study.
understudied_neural_dynamics_of_generati.paper: Neural Dynamics During the Generation and Evaluation of Creative and Non-Creative Ideas

standardized_creativity_tasks_as_evaluat.name: Standardized creativity tasks as evaluation method for LLM creative diversity
standardized_creativity_tasks_as_evaluat.description: The study elicits creative responses from many humans and LLMs using standardized creativity tasks and compares population-level response diversity as the primary empirical approach.
standardized_creativity_tasks_as_evaluat.paper: Large language models are homogeneously creative

age_related_stability_of_creative_perfor.name: Age-related stability of creative performance in older adulthood
age_related_stability_of_creative_perfor.description: Contrary to expected decline, creative performance shows age-related stability in older adults, suggesting preserved creative ability across the lifespan.
age_related_stability_of_creative_perfor.paper: Associative thinking and creative ability in older adulthood

intelligence_as_mediating_mechanism_for_.name: Intelligence as mediating mechanism for creative stability in older age
intelligence_as_mediating_mechanism_for_.description: Mediation analyses revealed that intelligence measures serve as the underlying cognitive mechanism explaining why creative thinking remains stable in older adulthood despite cognitive aging.
intelligence_as_mediating_mechanism_for_.paper: Associative thinking and creative ability in older adulthood

creative_ability_as_protective_factor_fo.name: Creative ability as protective factor for cognitive well-being and independence in older adults
creative_ability_as_protective_factor_fo.description: The paper claims that higher creative ability in older adults may help them cope with cognitive decline and maintain everyday independence and physical health.
creative_ability_as_protective_factor_fo.paper: Associative thinking and creative ability in older adulthood

open_question_about_specific_cognitive_b.name: Open question about specific cognitive basis for creative stability across the lifespan
open_question_about_specific_cognitive_b.description: Despite evidence for age-related stability in creative performance, the exact cognitive mechanisms underlying this invariability remain insufficiently understood and constitute an open research question.
open_question_about_specific_cognitive_b.paper: Associative thinking and creative ability in older adulthood

abstract_words_alter_semantic_network_st.name: abstract_words_alter_semantic_network_structure_in_aging
abstract_words_alter_semantic_network_st.description: Including abstract words in semantic memory network estimations significantly altered network structures and, in some cases, minimized age-related differences compared to concrete-word-only networks.
abstract_words_alter_semantic_network_st.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

abstract_words_are_more_resilient_under_.name: abstract_words_are_more_resilient_under_simulated_network_attacks
abstract_words_are_more_resilient_under_.description: Abstract words remained connected in semantic memory networks longer than concrete words under simulated targeted attacks on network nodes, indicating greater structural resilience.
abstract_words_are_more_resilient_under_.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

semantic_diversity_independently_predict.name: semantic_diversity_independently_predicts_node_connectivity_in_semantic_networks
semantic_diversity_independently_predict.description: Words that are more semantically diverse are more connected within semantic memory networks, with this effect being independent of concreteness.
semantic_diversity_independently_predict.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.
