concreteness_independently_predicts_node.name: concreteness_independently_predicts_node_connectivity_in_semantic_networks
concreteness_independently_predicts_node.description: Word abstractness (lower concreteness) independently predicts greater connectivity in semantic memory networks, separate from semantic diversity effects.
concreteness_independently_predicts_node.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

age_differences_minimized_in_individual_.name: age_differences_minimized_in_individual_based_mixed_concreteness_networks
age_differences_minimized_in_individual_.description: When individual-based semantic memory networks included both abstract and concrete words, age-related differences in network structure were minimal, contrasting with prominent differences in group-based analyses.
age_differences_minimized_in_individual_.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

group_based_analyses_show_prominent_age_.name: group_based_analyses_show_prominent_age_differences_consistent_with_prior_literature
group_based_analyses_show_prominent_age_.description: Group-based semantic network analyses revealed age-related decreases in efficiency and interconnectedness and increased segregation, consistent with prior findings even when abstract words were included.
group_based_analyses_show_prominent_age_.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

individual_vs_group_based_network_analys.name: individual_vs_group_based_network_analysis_discrepancy_in_aging_research
individual_vs_group_based_network_analys.description: Individual-based and group-based semantic network analyses yield divergent conclusions about the magnitude of age-related differences in semantic memory, highlighting a methodological challenge for aging research.
individual_vs_group_based_network_analys.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

abstract_and_semantically_diverse_words_.name: abstract_and_semantically_diverse_words_as_understudied_in_semantic_memory_aging_research
abstract_and_semantically_diverse_words_.description: Prior research on semantic memory networks in aging focused predominantly on concrete concepts, leaving abstract words—which comprise a significant portion of knowledge—understudied despite their potentially different organization.
abstract_and_semantically_diverse_words_.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

semantic_relatedness_judgment_paradigm_f.name: semantic_relatedness_judgment_paradigm_for_mixed_concreteness_network_construction
semantic_relatedness_judgment_paradigm_f.description: The study estimated individual and group semantic memory networks from semantic relatedness judgments of words varying in both concreteness and semantic diversity across younger and older adult participants.
semantic_relatedness_judgment_paradigm_f.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

importance_of_analyzing_semantic_network.name: importance_of_analyzing_semantic_networks_with_multiple_approaches
importance_of_analyzing_semantic_network.description: The study demonstrates that using multiple network analysis approaches (individual-based, group-based, attack simulations) is necessary to capture the full picture of age-related semantic memory differences.
importance_of_analyzing_semantic_network.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

mvt_as_framework_for_semantic_memory_sea.name: MVT_as_framework_for_semantic_memory_search_during_associative_fluency
mvt_as_framework_for_semantic_memory_sea.description: The study applies the Marginal Value Theorem (MVT) from optimal foraging theory to characterize how individuals switch between semantic clusters during a polysemous-word associative fluency task, predicting when switching should occur based on diminishing returns within a cluster.
mvt_as_framework_for_semantic_memory_sea.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

majority_of_associative_fluency_response.name: majority_of_associative_fluency_responses_align_with_MVT_predictions
majority_of_associative_fluency_response.description: During the polysemous-word associative fluency task, most participants' responses followed the pattern predicted by the Marginal Value Theorem, supporting the ecological validity of the foraging framework for semantic search.
majority_of_associative_fluency_response.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

mvt_deviant_search_patterns_as_novel_sem.name: MVT_deviant_search_patterns_as_novel_semantic_search_measure
mvt_deviant_search_patterns_as_novel_sem.description: The study introduces MVT-deviant switching patterns—responses that depart from optimal foraging predictions—as a novel behavioral measure of semantic memory search that captures creativity-relevant dynamics beyond simple fluency.
mvt_deviant_search_patterns_as_novel_sem.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

functional_brain_connectivity_predicts_m.name: functional_brain_connectivity_predicts_MVT_deviant_switching_patterns
functional_brain_connectivity_predicts_m.description: Connectome-based modeling revealed that resting-state or task-based functional brain connectivity significantly predicted the extent to which individuals showed MVT-deviant switching during the associative fluency task.
functional_brain_connectivity_predicts_m.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

mvt_deviant_patterns_mediate_brain_conne.name: MVT_deviant_patterns_mediate_brain_connectivity_to_creative_performance
mvt_deviant_patterns_mediate_brain_conne.description: Connectome-based modeling showed that MVT-deviant switching behavior mediated the relationship between functional brain connectivity and creative performance, suggesting a neurocognitive pathway from brain organization to creativity via search dynamics.
mvt_deviant_patterns_mediate_brain_conne.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

cognitive_policy_for_creativity_differs_.name: cognitive_policy_for_creativity_differs_from_policy_optimizing_fluency
cognitive_policy_for_creativity_differs_.description: The study argues that the cognitive search strategy maximizing creative output diverges from the MVT-optimal strategy that maximizes fluency, implying that creativity-relevant search involves non-optimal foraging deviations.
cognitive_policy_for_creativity_differs_.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

polysemous_word_associative_fluency_task.name: polysemous_word_associative_fluency_task_as_paradigm_for_neurocognitive_search_study
polysemous_word_associative_fluency_task.description: A polysemous-word associative fluency task was used to elicit semantic memory search behavior, enabling detection of both clustering and switching dynamics as well as their deviation from MVT predictions within a single paradigm.
polysemous_word_associative_fluency_task.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

clustering_switching_framework_extended_.name: clustering_switching_framework_extended_with_MVT_to_explain_creativity
clustering_switching_framework_extended_.description: The paper extends the existing clustering–switching framework for semantic fluency by integrating it with the MVT, yielding a richer account of how foraging dynamics in semantic memory relate to creative performance.
clustering_switching_framework_extended_.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

connectome_based_modeling_applied_to_sem.name: connectome_based_modeling_applied_to_semantic_search_dynamics
connectome_based_modeling_applied_to_sem.description: Connectome-based predictive modeling was applied to link individual differences in functional brain connectivity to MVT-deviant switching patterns and to mediate the brain-creativity relationship.
connectome_based_modeling_applied_to_sem.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

creativeable_ai_powered_creativity_train.name: Creativeable AI-powered creativity training program
creativeable_ai_powered_creativity_train.description: Creativeable is an AI-powered program that provides automated feedback and adaptively adjusts creative story writing task difficulty without human intervention, offering a scalable personalized creativity training solution.
creativeable_ai_powered_creativity_train.paper: Creativeable: Leveraging AI for Personalized Creativity Enhancement

feedback_with_constant_difficulty_produc.name: Feedback with constant difficulty produces largest creativity gains
feedback_with_constant_difficulty_produc.description: Among the four Creativeable conditions, the F/CL condition (feedback with constant difficulty) led to the most notable improvements in creativity as measured by the alternative uses task.
feedback_with_constant_difficulty_produc.paper: Creativeable: Leveraging AI for Personalized Creativity Enhancement

adaptive_difficulty_combined_with_feedba.name: Adaptive difficulty combined with feedback yields comparatively smaller creativity improvements
adaptive_difficulty_combined_with_feedba.description: The F/VL condition, which combined automated feedback with adaptive (variable) difficulty, exhibited comparatively smaller creativity improvements relative to other conditions, suggesting a tension between structured feedback and increasing task complexity.
adaptive_difficulty_combined_with_feedba.paper: Creativeable: Leveraging AI for Personalized Creativity Enhancement

creativity_improvements_evident_across_a.name: Creativity improvements evident across all four Creativeable conditions
creativity_improvements_evident_across_a.description: All four conditions in the Creativeable study showed some degree of creativity improvement from pre- to post-assessment on the alternative uses task, indicating a general training effect regardless of feedback or difficulty condition.
creativity_improvements_evident_across_a.paper: Creativeable: Leveraging AI for Personalized Creativity Enhancement

balancing_structured_feedback_with_incre.name: Balancing structured feedback with increasing task complexity as design principle for creativity training
balancing_structured_feedback_with_incre.description: The study argues that effective AI-based creativity training requires careful balance between providing structured feedback and progressively increasing task complexity, as combining both may undermine sustained creative growth.
balancing_structured_feedback_with_incre.paper: Creativeable: Leveraging AI for Personalized Creativity Enhancement

semantically_distant_word_prompts_as_cre.name: Semantically distant word prompts as creative story writing task stimulus
semantically_distant_word_prompts_as_cre.description: Participants completed creative story writing tasks using semantically distant word prompts across five rounds, operationalizing the use of semantic distance as a driver of creative challenge in an AI training context.
semantically_distant_word_prompts_as_cre.paper: Creativeable: Leveraging AI for Personalized Creativity Enhancement

ai_based_creativity_training_as_a_scalab.name: AI-based creativity training as a scalable democratizing intervention
ai_based_creativity_training_as_a_scalab.description: The study claims that AI-powered creativity training programs like Creativeable can democratize access to personalized creativity interventions by removing the need for expert human trainers.
ai_based_creativity_training_as_a_scalab.paper: Creativeable: Leveraging AI for Personalized Creativity Enhancement

alternative_uses_task_as_pre_post_creati.name: Alternative uses task as pre-post creativity assessment in AI training study
alternative_uses_task_as_pre_post_creati.description: Participants were assessed on the alternative uses task before and after using Creativeable to measure creativity gains, with a semantic fluency task serving as a control measure.
alternative_uses_task_as_pre_post_creati.paper: Creativeable: Leveraging AI for Personalized Creativity Enhancement

four_condition_experimental_design_varyi.name: Four-condition experimental design varying feedback and difficulty in creativity training
four_condition_experimental_design_varyi.description: The study employed a four-condition between-subjects design crossing feedback presence versus absence with adaptive versus constant difficulty to isolate the independent and interactive effects of these training components.
four_condition_experimental_design_varyi.paper: Creativeable: Leveraging AI for Personalized Creativity Enhancement

emotion_concepts_have_distinct_featural_.name: emotion_concepts_have_distinct_featural_structure_from_concrete_and_other_abstract_concepts
emotion_concepts_have_distinct_featural_.description: Analyses of normative feature data revealed statistically significant differences in the distribution of features among emotion, nonemotion abstract, and concrete concept types, indicating that emotion concepts occupy a distinct representational niche beyond the simple emotion-versus-concrete contrast.

emotion_concepts_show_lower_featural_var.name: emotion_concepts_show_lower_featural_variability_than_expected_under_constructionist_views
emotion_concepts_show_lower_featural_var.description: Contrary to predictions derived from constructionist theories of emotion, emotion concepts were associated with less variability in participant-generated features compared to concrete and other abstract concepts, suggesting more consistent representational structure.

interoceptive_states_as_key_differentiat.name: interoceptive_states_as_key_differentiating_dimension_for_emotion_concept_representation
interoceptive_states_as_key_differentiat.description: The study hypothesizes and tests whether the relative importance of interoceptive states distinguishes emotion concepts (e.g., happiness, sadness) from other abstract concepts (e.g., equality, tyranny) in their featural structure.

normative_feature_data_for_emotion_conce.name: normative_feature_data_for_emotion_concepts_as_empirical_basis
normative_feature_data_for_emotion_conce.description: The study derives a large set of normative feature data for emotion concepts, providing an empirical resource for studying similarities and differences between emotion, nonemotion abstract, and concrete concepts.

restriction_to_native_english_speakers_a.name: restriction_to_native_english_speakers_as_boundary_condition
restriction_to_native_english_speakers_a.description: The authors explicitly note that their sample was restricted to native English speakers and that cross-linguistic validation is needed, limiting the generalizability of the findings about emotion concept structure.

gap_in_conceptual_representation_researc.name: gap_in_conceptual_representation_research_on_emotion_versus_other_abstract_concepts
gap_in_conceptual_representation_researc.description: Theories of semantic organization have historically compared emotion concepts to concrete concepts, neglecting comparison with other abstract concepts such as equality or tyranny, leaving the specific representational status of emotions among abstract concepts undercharacterized.

constructionist_view_of_emotion_as_theor.name: constructionist_view_of_emotion_as_theoretical_framework_predicting_variability
constructionist_view_of_emotion_as_theor.description: Based on constructionist views, the authors predicted that emotion concepts would show more variability in their representation than concrete and other abstract concepts, a prediction that was not confirmed by the data.

dsi_applied_to_scientific_abstracts_and_.name: DSI applied to scientific abstracts and titles predicts citation count
dsi_applied_to_scientific_abstracts_and_.description: The paper claims that Divergent Semantic Integration (DSI), a computational measure correlating with originality, shows a statistically significant positive correlation with the base-10 logarithm of citation count after 5 years across all fields of research examined, with an adjusted R² of 0.13.
dsi_applied_to_scientific_abstracts_and_.paper: Originality in scientific titles and abstracts can predict citation count

dsi_varies_significantly_by_scientific_s.name: DSI varies significantly by scientific subject and field
dsi_varies_significantly_by_scientific_s.description: Statistically significant differences in DSI scores are observed across different subjects and fields of research in the 99,557-abstract corpus from the Web of Science.
dsi_varies_significantly_by_scientific_s.paper: Originality in scientific titles and abstracts can predict citation count

slight_temporal_rise_in_dsi_over_time_in.name: Slight temporal rise in DSI over time in scientific literature
slight_temporal_rise_in_dsi_over_time_in.description: The analysis of the Web of Science corpus reveals a slight increase in DSI scores over time, suggesting scientific writing has become marginally more semantically original.
slight_temporal_rise_in_dsi_over_time_in.paper: Originality in scientific titles and abstracts can predict citation count

dsi_as_a_computational_originality_measu.name: DSI as a computational originality measure applicable to large scientific corpora
dsi_as_a_computational_originality_measu.description: The paper demonstrates the applicability of Divergent Semantic Integration (DSI) — a measure from creativity science — to nearly 100,000 scientific abstracts and titles, enabling large-scale quantitative originality assessment.
dsi_as_a_computational_originality_measu.paper: Originality in scientific titles and abstracts can predict citation count

citation_count_as_a_proxy_for_scientific.name: Citation count as a proxy for scientific impact of original work
citation_count_as_a_proxy_for_scientific.description: The study uses base-10 logarithm of citation count after 5 years as the dependent variable for modeling the impact of originality in scientific publications.
citation_count_as_a_proxy_for_scientific.paper: Originality in scientific titles and abstracts can predict citation count

positive_association_between_scientific_.name: Positive association between scientific originality and academic recognition
positive_association_between_scientific_.description: Higher DSI in a scientific abstract or title is associated with higher citation counts, suggesting that more semantically original scientific contributions receive greater academic recognition.
positive_association_between_scientific_.paper: Originality in scientific titles and abstracts can predict citation count

age_differences_in_semantic_network_stru.name: age_differences_in_semantic_network_structure_via_free_recall
age_differences_in_semantic_network_stru.description: Using a single-list free recall paradigm with 318 participants, older adults (61–88 years) showed higher average shortest path length and modularity, while younger adults (25–55 years) showed higher clustering coefficients, indicating less integrated, more segregated semantic networks in aging.
age_differences_in_semantic_network_stru.paper: How Retrieval Processes Change with Age: Exploring Age Differences in Semantic Network and Retrieval Dynamics

diminished_temporal_contiguity_effect_in.name: diminished_temporal_contiguity_effect_in_older_adults_during_free_recall
diminished_temporal_contiguity_effect_in.description: Both age groups displayed a temporal contiguity effect with forward asymmetry during free recall, but this effect was less pronounced in older adults, suggesting a reduced capacity to reinstate temporal context for memory retrieval.
diminished_temporal_contiguity_effect_in.paper: How Retrieval Processes Change with Age: Exploring Age Differences in Semantic Network and Retrieval Dynamics

subgroup_identification_of_non_temporal_.name: subgroup_identification_of_non_temporal_and_backward_retrieval_strategies
subgroup_identification_of_non_temporal_.description: Within the participant sample, two subgroups were identified that diverged from average retrieval dynamic curves: one relying on non-temporal mechanisms and another employing backward directionality in memory search.
subgroup_identification_of_non_temporal_.paper: How Retrieval Processes Change with Age: Exploring Age Differences in Semantic Network and Retrieval Dynamics

forward_temporal_associations_linked_to_.name: forward_temporal_associations_linked_to_highest_recall_performance
forward_temporal_associations_linked_to_.description: Participants who used forward temporal associations during free recall demonstrated the highest recall performance compared to those using non-temporal or backward retrieval strategies.
forward_temporal_associations_linked_to_.paper: How Retrieval Processes Change with Age: Exploring Age Differences in Semantic Network and Retrieval Dynamics

older_adult_free_recall_decline_linked_t.name: older_adult_free_recall_decline_linked_to_temporal_context_reinstatement_and_network_rigidity
older_adult_free_recall_decline_linked_t.description: The study proposes that lower free recall performance in older adults is related both to a diminished capacity to reinstate temporal context and to less flexible, small-world-like semantic network structure.
older_adult_free_recall_decline_linked_t.paper: How Retrieval Processes Change with Age: Exploring Age Differences in Semantic Network and Retrieval Dynamics

free_recall_paradigm_as_method_for_const.name: free_recall_paradigm_as_method_for_constructing_semantic_memory_networks
free_recall_paradigm_as_method_for_const.description: Semantic memory networks were constructed from free recall data by analyzing the co-occurrence and sequential order of recalled words in a single-list paradigm involving 318 participants across two age groups.
free_recall_paradigm_as_method_for_const.paper: How Retrieval Processes Change with Age: Exploring Age Differences in Semantic Network and Retrieval Dynamics

small_world_network_structure_decline_as.name: small_world_network_structure_decline_as_marker_of_semantic_memory_aging
small_world_network_structure_decline_as.description: The study characterizes older adults' semantic networks as lacking a flexible small-world-like structure, suggesting this represents a specific neural-cognitive marker of age-related semantic memory change.
small_world_network_structure_decline_as.paper: How Retrieval Processes Change with Age: Exploring Age Differences in Semantic Network and Retrieval Dynamics

domain_specific_question_asking_improves.name: domain_specific_question_asking_improves_with_knowledge_acquisition
domain_specific_question_asking_improves.description: Undergraduate students showed improvement in domain-specific question-asking over a semester-long psychology course, while general question-asking remained stable or declined, suggesting that gained knowledge selectively reshapes inquiry in the learned domain.
domain_specific_question_asking_improves.paper: Knowledge reshapes inquiry by changing question asking ability and impacting academic assessment

general_question_asking_stable_or_declin.name: general_question_asking_stable_or_declines_over_knowledge_acquisition
general_question_asking_stable_or_declin.description: In contrast to domain-specific questioning, general question-asking ability did not improve and may have declined over the course of a knowledge-rich semester, indicating that knowledge acquisition does not uniformly enhance all forms of inquiry.
general_question_asking_stable_or_declin.paper: Knowledge reshapes inquiry by changing question asking ability and impacting academic assessment

question_asking_ability_negatively_predi.name: question_asking_ability_negatively_predicts_closed_ended_exam_performance
question_asking_ability_negatively_predi.description: Both general and domain-specific question-asking abilities were negatively related to performance on a closed-ended multiple-choice exam, suggesting that stronger questioning is not advantageous—and may be detrimental—for conventional knowledge-recall assessments.
question_asking_ability_negatively_predi.paper: Knowledge reshapes inquiry by changing question asking ability and impacting academic assessment

question_asking_ability_positively_predi.name: question_asking_ability_positively_predicts_open_ended_project_performance
question_asking_ability_positively_predi.description: Higher general and domain-specific question-asking abilities were positively related to performance on an open-ended final project, highlighting the educational value of questioning skills for generative, non-constrained academic tasks.
question_asking_ability_positively_predi.paper: Knowledge reshapes inquiry by changing question asking ability and impacting academic assessment

longitudinal_question_asking_task_design.name: longitudinal_question_asking_task_design_for_knowledge_acquisition_study
longitudinal_question_asking_task_design.description: Undergraduate students (N=68) completed general and domain-specific question-asking tasks at the start and end of a semester-long Intro to Psychology course, enabling pre-to-post comparison of questioning ability in the context of natural knowledge acquisition.
longitudinal_question_asking_task_design.paper: Knowledge reshapes inquiry by changing question asking ability and impacting academic assessment

differential_academic_outcome_validity_o.name: differential_academic_outcome_validity_of_question_asking_versus_factual_recall
differential_academic_outcome_validity_o.description: Question-asking ability captures a dimension of academic competence orthogonal to factual recall: it negatively predicts closed-ended test performance but positively predicts open-ended project performance, implying that standard closed-ended assessments may undervalue students with strong inquiry skills.
differential_academic_outcome_validity_o.paper: Knowledge reshapes inquiry by changing question asking ability and impacting academic assessment

knowledge_acquisition_as_moderator_of_qu.name: knowledge_acquisition_as_moderator_of_question_asking_specificity_effects
knowledge_acquisition_as_moderator_of_qu.description: The study argues that gained knowledge reshapes inquiry by selectively improving domain-specific questioning while leaving general questioning unchanged or diminished, implicating domain knowledge as a key moderator of questioning ability development.
knowledge_acquisition_as_moderator_of_qu.paper: Knowledge reshapes inquiry by changing question asking ability and impacting academic assessment

educational_value_of_fostering_question_.name: educational_value_of_fostering_question_asking_skills
educational_value_of_fostering_question_.description: The study highlights the educational importance of developing stronger question-asking skills, particularly given their positive relationship with open-ended academic performance, as a call for pedagogical attention to inquiry-based competencies.
educational_value_of_fostering_question_.paper: Knowledge reshapes inquiry by changing question asking ability and impacting academic assessment

spot_the_spy_online_game_as_empirical_pl.name: Spot the Spy online game as empirical platform for studying question-asking
spot_the_spy_online_game_as_empirical_pl.description: The authors developed 'Spot the Spy,' an online serious game where players must find a hidden spy by asking a chatbot agent questions, providing a naturalistic empirical platform for studying question-asking strategies and their cognitive correlates.
spot_the_spy_online_game_as_empirical_pl.paper: Spot the spy: An online game for exploring question-asking in the wild

intelligence_predicts_effective_question.name: Intelligence predicts effective questioning strategies and game performance in Spot the Spy
intelligence_predicts_effective_question.description: Across two studies, higher intelligence scores were associated with more effective questioning strategies and better overall performance in the Spot the Spy game, suggesting a link between general cognitive ability and strategic question-asking.
intelligence_predicts_effective_question.paper: Spot the spy: An online game for exploring question-asking in the wild

optimal_question_asking_strategies_ident.name: Optimal question-asking strategies identified via gameplay analysis
optimal_question_asking_strategies_ident.description: The study identifies specific questioning strategies associated with superior performance in the Spot the Spy game, offering empirical insight into what constitutes effective question-asking in a naturalistic investigative task.
optimal_question_asking_strategies_ident.paper: Spot the spy: An online game for exploring question-asking in the wild

two_study_design_for_replication_and_ext.name: Two-study design for replication and extension of question-asking findings
two_study_design_for_replication_and_ext.description: Study 1 (online, N=103) focused on game development and exploratory validation, while Study 2 (in-lab, N=100) focused on replication and extension, with both studies including cognitive assessments of creativity, curiosity, and intelligence.
two_study_design_for_replication_and_ext.paper: Spot the spy: An online game for exploring question-asking in the wild

gamified_environments_as_tools_for_advan.name: Gamified environments as tools for advancing understanding of cognitive processes and training
gamified_environments_as_tools_for_advan.description: The authors argue that digital serious games like Spot the Spy offer significant advantages for studying cognitive processes in natural settings and hold potential for developing educational and training tools that foster strategic thinking and question-asking.
gamified_environments_as_tools_for_advan.paper: Spot the spy: An online game for exploring question-asking in the wild

creativity_and_curiosity_as_cognitive_co.name: Creativity and curiosity as cognitive correlates of question-asking assessed in Spot the Spy
creativity_and_curiosity_as_cognitive_co.description: Participants completed cognitive tasks assessing creativity and curiosity alongside intelligence before gameplay, raising the open question of whether and how these capacities independently predict question-asking strategies and game performance.
creativity_and_curiosity_as_cognitive_co.paper: Spot the spy: An online game for exploring question-asking in the wild

chatbot_agent_as_interactive_partner_for.name: Chatbot agent as interactive partner for naturalistic question-asking paradigm
chatbot_agent_as_interactive_partner_for.description: Players direct questions to a chatbot agent within the game environment, enabling systematic collection of naturalistic question-asking behavior while maintaining experimental control over the information environment.
chatbot_agent_as_interactive_partner_for.paper: Spot the spy: An online game for exploring question-asking in the wild

forma_mentis_networks_as_tool_for_quanti.name: Forma mentis networks as tool for quantifying STEM mindsets
forma_mentis_networks_as_tool_for_quanti.description: Behavioural forma mentis networks (BFMNs) are used to map the cognitive and emotional ways in which individuals conceptualise STEM subjects by capturing patterns of conceptual association and perception.
forma_mentis_networks_as_tool_for_quanti.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

human_stem_mindsets_show_higher_clusteri.name: Human STEM mindsets show higher clustering than GPT-3.5-simulated mindsets
human_stem_mindsets_show_higher_clusteri.description: Across 177 human participants and 177 GPT-3.5-simulated artificial humans, human forma mentis networks exhibited significantly higher clustering coefficients, indicating that humans form and close triads of conceptual associations when recollecting STEM ideas more than GPT-3.5 does.
human_stem_mindsets_show_higher_clusteri.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

human_stem_experts_show_most_robust_clus.name: Human STEM experts show most robust clustering in forma mentis networks
human_stem_experts_show_most_robust_clus.description: Among the three expertise groups, human experts demonstrated particularly robust clustering coefficients in their BFMNs, reflecting better integration of STEM concepts into their cognitive networks compared to trainees and academics.
human_stem_experts_show_most_robust_clus.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

gpt_3_5_produces_sparser_forma_mentis_ne.name: GPT-3.5 produces sparser forma mentis networks with weaker clustering
gpt_3_5_produces_sparser_forma_mentis_ne.description: GPT-3.5-simulated artificial humans generated sparser networks with weaker clustering, highlighting limitations of the model in replicating human-like mindset structure for STEM concepts.
gpt_3_5_produces_sparser_forma_mentis_ne.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

both_human_and_gpt_3_5_mindsets_frame_ma.name: Both human and GPT-3.5 mindsets frame mathematics in neutral or positive terms
both_human_and_gpt_3_5_mindsets_frame_ma.description: Contrary to findings from other studies using STEM high-schoolers, researchers, and other LLMs, both human participants and GPT-3.5 in this study associated 'mathematics' with neutral or positive conceptual valence in their forma mentis networks.
both_human_and_gpt_3_5_mindsets_frame_ma.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

expertise_level_modulates_stem_mindset_n.name: Expertise level modulates STEM mindset network structure
expertise_level_modulates_stem_mindset_n.description: The study posits that expertise level (trainee, expert, academic) systematically influences the structure of STEM forma mentis networks, with higher expertise associated with greater conceptual integration as measured by clustering.
expertise_level_modulates_stem_mindset_n.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

gpt_3_5_simulation_of_human_participants.name: GPT-3.5 simulation of human participants as methodological approach for mindset research
gpt_3_5_simulation_of_human_participants.description: 177 artificial humans were simulated using GPT-3.5 to parallel the 177 human participants, enabling direct comparison of human and LLM-generated forma mentis networks across expertise groups.
gpt_3_5_simulation_of_human_participants.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

bfmn_clustering_coefficient_as_index_of_.name: BFMN clustering coefficient as index of cognitive memory integration
bfmn_clustering_coefficient_as_index_of_.description: The paper claims that clustering coefficients in forma mentis networks reflect the degree to which individuals integrate concepts into cohesive memory structures, providing cognitive insight into memory organisation.
bfmn_clustering_coefficient_as_index_of_.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

llm_limitations_in_replicating_human_lik.name: LLM limitations in replicating human-like conceptual mindset structure
llm_limitations_in_replicating_human_lik.description: The study identifies a structural limitation of GPT-3.5 in replicating human-like mindset patterns, specifically the inability to produce tightly clustered conceptual association networks characteristic of human cognition.
llm_limitations_in_replicating_human_lik.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

comparison_of_stem_mindset_valence_acros.name: Comparison of STEM mindset valence across studies and LLM types
comparison_of_stem_mindset_valence_acros.description: By comparing mathematics framing results to prior BFMN studies on high-schoolers, researchers, and other LLMs, the paper contributes a cross-sample and cross-model perspective on how STEM subjects are emotionally and cognitively framed.
comparison_of_stem_mindset_valence_acros.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

cognitive_networks_as_framework_for_know.name: cognitive_networks_as_framework_for_knowledge_modelling_and_learning
cognitive_networks_as_framework_for_know.description: The paper positions cognitive network science as a unifying framework bridging computer science, psychology, and network science for modelling knowledge and learning. It reviews how network representations of cognition can quantify knowledge structures and their change over time.
cognitive_networks_as_framework_for_know.paper: Knowledge Modelling and Learning through Cognitive Networks

network_science_tools_for_quantifying_kn.name: network_science_tools_for_quantifying_knowledge_organization_change_over_learning
network_science_tools_for_quantifying_kn.description: The paper advocates using cognitive network science tools to measure how knowledge organization (semantic network structure) changes as a function of learning, going beyond content-based assessments. This addresses the limitation that standard assessments measure knowledge content but not organization.
network_science_tools_for_quantifying_kn.paper: Knowledge Modelling and Learning through Cognitive Networks

semantic_memory_network_structure_as_ind.name: semantic_memory_network_structure_as_indicator_of_learning_quality
semantic_memory_network_structure_as_ind.description: The paper claims that the structural properties of an individual's semantic memory network (e.g., connectivity, clustering) reflect the quality and organization of their knowledge, not merely its quantity. High-knowledge learners are expected to show more interconnected and clustered networks.
semantic_memory_network_structure_as_ind.paper: Knowledge Modelling and Learning through Cognitive Networks

interdisciplinary_synthesis_of_cognitive.name: interdisciplinary_synthesis_of_cognitive_network_science_for_knowledge_and_learning
interdisciplinary_synthesis_of_cognitive.description: The paper provides a synthesis of research strands from computer science, psychology, and network science as they apply to knowledge modelling and learning, identifying convergences and open problems across these disciplines.
interdisciplinary_synthesis_of_cognitive.paper: Knowledge Modelling and Learning through Cognitive Networks

application_of_cognitive_network_science.name: application_of_cognitive_network_science_to_educational_contexts
application_of_cognitive_network_science.description: A stated goal of the paper is to apply cognitive network science methods to educational settings, enabling quantification of how students' knowledge structures reorganize as a result of instruction and experience.
application_of_cognitive_network_science.paper: Knowledge Modelling and Learning through Cognitive Networks

knowledge_reorganization_measurable_via_.name: knowledge_reorganization_measurable_via_network_restructuring
knowledge_reorganization_measurable_via_.description: The paper reports or reviews evidence that successful learning is accompanied by measurable restructuring of semantic memory networks, such as increased integration of previously disconnected concepts.
knowledge_reorganization_measurable_via_.paper: Knowledge Modelling and Learning through Cognitive Networks

gap_in_tools_and_methods_for_cognitive_n.name: gap_in_tools_and_methods_for_cognitive_network_knowledge_modelling
gap_in_tools_and_methods_for_cognitive_n.description: The paper identifies a gap in accessible, validated tools for researchers and educators to construct, analyze, and interpret cognitive networks as models of knowledge, limiting adoption of the framework.
gap_in_tools_and_methods_for_cognitive_n.paper: Knowledge Modelling and Learning through Cognitive Networks

multilayer_network_framework_for_mental_.name: multilayer_network_framework_for_mental_lexicon_review
multilayer_network_framework_for_mental_.description: This paper reviews cognitive multilayer networks as a quantitative and interpretative framework for investigating the mental lexicon, covering formalism, psychological phenomena, and future directions.
multilayer_network_framework_for_mental_.paper: Cognitive modelling with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements and future challenges

multilayer_network_formalism_introductio.name: multilayer_network_formalism_introduction_for_cognitive_science_review
multilayer_network_formalism_introductio.description: The paper provides a gentle introduction to the structure and formalism of multilayer networks as a foundation for cognitive modelling of the mental lexicon.
multilayer_network_formalism_introductio.paper: Cognitive modelling with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements and future challenges

multiple_interactive_cognitive_levels_in.name: multiple_interactive_cognitive_levels_influence_word_acquisition_storage_and_processing
multiple_interactive_cognitive_levels_in.description: Decades of psychological experiments demonstrate that conceptual associations across multiple, interactive cognitive levels (semantic, phonological, syntactic, etc.) greatly influence word acquisition, storage, and processing.
multiple_interactive_cognitive_levels_in.paper: Cognitive modelling with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements and future challenges

semantic_memory_network_integration_pred.name: Semantic memory network integration predicts originality across creative tasks
semantic_memory_network_integration_pred.description: Individual differences in semantic memory network integration consistently predicted originality across both a creative problem-solving task and an alternative uses task, suggesting a stable mechanism linking memory structure to creative performance.
semantic_memory_network_integration_pred.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

ideation_fluency_predicts_originality_ac.name: Ideation fluency predicts originality across creative tasks
ideation_fluency_predicts_originality_ac.description: Ideation fluency consistently predicted originality across both the creative problem-solving and alternative uses tasks in Studies 1 and 2, indicating fluency as a stable predictor of creative output quality.
ideation_fluency_predicts_originality_ac.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

usefulness_is_less_reliably_predicted_th.name: Usefulness is less reliably predicted than originality and shows task-specific associations
usefulness_is_less_reliably_predicted_th.description: Unlike originality, usefulness of creative output was not consistently predicted by semantic memory network properties across tasks, showing associations primarily in the CPS context, indicating context-sensitive mechanisms for usefulness evaluation.
usefulness_is_less_reliably_predicted_th.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

self_evaluations_of_creativity_diverge_f.name: Self-evaluations of creativity diverge from objective outcomes due to heuristic metacognitive biases
self_evaluations_of_creativity_diverge_f.description: Participants' self-evaluations of their creative performance often diverged from objective measures of originality and usefulness, suggesting that self-assessment reflects heuristic cues and metacognitive biases rather than accurate performance monitoring.
self_evaluations_of_creativity_diverge_f.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

within_subjects_comparison_of_aut_and_cp.name: Within-subjects comparison of AUT and CPS self-evaluation and performance
within_subjects_comparison_of_aut_and_cp.description: Study 2 used a within-subjects design where the same participants completed both an alternative uses task and a creative problem-solving task, enabling direct comparison of creative output, originality, usefulness, and self-evaluation across task types.
within_subjects_comparison_of_aut_and_cp.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

individual_based_semantic_memory_network.name: Individual-based semantic memory network construction via relatedness judgment task for creativity and metacognition research
individual_based_semantic_memory_network.description: Semantic memory networks were estimated for individual participants using a relatedness judgment task, enabling examination of how idiosyncratic network properties relate to creative performance and self-evaluation.
individual_based_semantic_memory_network.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

cps_task_as_complementary_creativity_par.name: CPS task as complementary creativity paradigm to AUT for studying semantic memory and metacognition
cps_task_as_complementary_creativity_par.description: The study extends the semantic memory-creativity literature by including a creative problem-solving task alongside the alternative uses task, allowing examination of whether memory-creativity links and self-evaluation biases generalize beyond divergent thinking tasks.
cps_task_as_complementary_creativity_par.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

heuristic_cues_shape_originality_self_as.name: Heuristic cues shape originality self-assessment independently of objective performance
heuristic_cues_shape_originality_self_as.description: Metacognitive self-assessment of originality is driven by heuristic cues such as ideation fluency or semantic distance rather than accurate introspection, leading to systematic biases that are identifiable through regression analyses.
heuristic_cues_shape_originality_self_as.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

fragmentation_of_creative_cognition_rese.name: Fragmentation of creative cognition research as a critical barrier to consensus
fragmentation_of_creative_cognition_rese.description: The paper identifies that despite flourishing research, the field of creative cognition faces fragmentation that hinders consensus and integration across findings, theories, and methods.
fragmentation_of_creative_cognition_rese.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

challenge_1_definitional_inconsistency_f.name: Challenge 1: Definitional inconsistency for core creative cognition constructs
challenge_1_definitional_inconsistency_f.description: The agenda identifies a theoretical challenge around defining core research terms such as divergent thinking, emotions, and metacognition, which impedes cumulative science in creative cognition.
challenge_1_definitional_inconsistency_f.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

challenge_2_types_and_domains_of_knowled.name: Challenge 2: Types and domains of knowledge influencing creative performance
challenge_2_types_and_domains_of_knowled.description: The agenda highlights the need to identify different types and domains of knowledge that influence creative performance, recognizing knowledge as a multidimensional moderator.
challenge_2_types_and_domains_of_knowled.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

challenge_3_measuring_dynamic_interactio.name: Challenge 3: Measuring dynamic interactions of cognitive, affective, and metacognitive processes over time
challenge_3_measuring_dynamic_interactio.description: The agenda calls for methods capable of capturing dynamic interactions between cognitive, affective, and metacognitive processes as they unfold during creative work, rather than treating them as static variables.
challenge_3_measuring_dynamic_interactio.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

challenge_4_incorporating_environmental_.name: Challenge 4: Incorporating environmental and interpersonal contexts into creative cognition models
challenge_4_incorporating_environmental_.description: The agenda identifies a gap in incorporating environmental and interpersonal factors—including collaboration, technology, and embodied cognition—into theories and methods of creative cognition.
challenge_4_incorporating_environmental_.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

collaborative_research_agenda_for_creati.name: Collaborative research agenda for creative cognition as a field-organizing contribution
collaborative_research_agenda_for_creati.description: The paper presents a structured research agenda developed by 50 European researchers at the Lorentz Centre workshop, synthesizing four critical challenges and calling for standardized measures, synthesis of findings, and methodological innovation.
collaborative_research_agenda_for_creati.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

need_for_standardized_measures_in_creati.name: Need for standardized measures in creative cognition research
need_for_standardized_measures_in_creati.description: The agenda explicitly calls for the development of standardized measures as a prerequisite for synthesizing existing findings and building comprehensive theories of creative cognition.
need_for_standardized_measures_in_creati.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

embracing_complexity_rather_than_reducin.name: Embracing complexity rather than reducing it as a methodological principle for creativity science
embracing_complexity_rather_than_reducin.description: The agenda advocates for building theories and methods that embrace rather than reduce the inherent complexity of creativity, including its cognitive, affective, metacognitive, and contextual dimensions.
embracing_complexity_rather_than_reducin.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

role_of_emotions_in_creative_cognition_a.name: Role of emotions in creative cognition as an under-defined construct
role_of_emotions_in_creative_cognition_a.description: The agenda groups emotions alongside divergent thinking and metacognition as core terms requiring clearer definition, suggesting emotional processes in creativity remain theoretically underdeveloped.
role_of_emotions_in_creative_cognition_a.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

metacognition_in_creative_cognition_as_a.name: Metacognition in creative cognition as an under-defined construct
metacognition_in_creative_cognition_as_a.description: The agenda identifies metacognition as a core term whose definition requires theoretical clarification to enable cumulative creative cognition research.
metacognition_in_creative_cognition_as_a.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

the_martian_game_as_an_ecologically_vali.name: The Martian Game as an ecologically valid platform for studying open-ended question asking
the_martian_game_as_an_ecologically_vali.description: The Martian Game is an online gaming environment designed to simulate creative problem solving in realistic contexts, allowing players to ask open-ended questions to an AI chatbot and then produce solutions. It provides a naturalistic, interdisciplinary tool for studying question-asking cognition beyond laboratory constraints.
the_martian_game_as_an_ecologically_vali.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

two_stage_problem_solving_design_problem.name: Two-stage problem-solving design: problem-finding then solution-planning
two_stage_problem_solving_design_problem.description: The Martian Game structures play into a problem-finding phase (questioning an AI chatbot named Mark) and a solution-planning phase (producing written and visual designs for a solar energy system). This separation allows independent measurement of question quality and solution quality.
two_stage_problem_solving_design_problem.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

multi_dimensional_question_coding_scheme.name: Multi-dimensional question coding scheme for complexity, originality, and relevance
multi_dimensional_question_coding_scheme.description: Questions asked during the game are coded along three dimensions—complexity, originality, and relevance—providing quantitative measures of cognitive potential in open-ended question asking.
multi_dimensional_question_coding_scheme.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

complex_questions_promote_effective_prob.name: Complex questions promote effective problem solving in open-ended creative tasks
complex_questions_promote_effective_prob.description: The game supports the hypothesis that asking complex questions during the problem-finding phase leads to more effective problem solving, as reflected in higher originality and appropriateness of resulting solutions.
complex_questions_promote_effective_prob.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

ai_chatbot_as_interactive_partner_for_na.name: AI chatbot as interactive partner for naturalistic open-ended question asking
ai_chatbot_as_interactive_partner_for_na.description: The game uses an AI chatbot ('Mark') as the information source that players query, enabling a controlled yet naturalistic question-asking environment that simulates realistic information-seeking in creative problem solving.
ai_chatbot_as_interactive_partner_for_na.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

pilot_study_validation_of_the_martian_ga.name: Pilot study validation of The Martian Game
pilot_study_validation_of_the_martian_ga.description: A pilot study is reported that validates the game's potential for examining open-ended cognition, demonstrating feasibility and supporting the link between question asking and problem-solving outcomes.
pilot_study_validation_of_the_martian_ga.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

solar_energy_system_design_for_a_martian.name: Solar energy system design for a Martian city as creative problem-solving scenario
solar_energy_system_design_for_a_martian.description: The game uses the domain-specific scenario of designing a solar energy system for a Martian city as the creative problem-solving context, chosen to simulate realistic and interdisciplinary problem solving while being novel enough to reduce prior knowledge bias.
solar_energy_system_design_for_a_martian.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

open_ended_question_asking_as_understudi.name: Open-ended question asking as understudied due to naturalistic study challenges
open_ended_question_asking_as_understudi.description: The paper identifies that the cognitive mechanisms of question asking remain poorly understood because it is difficult to study naturally, motivating the development of the game as an ecological alternative.
open_ended_question_asking_as_understudi.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

question_originality_and_relevance_as_me.name: Question originality and relevance as measurable dimensions alongside complexity
question_originality_and_relevance_as_me.description: Beyond complexity, the game coding scheme captures originality and relevance of questions, enabling a multi-faceted characterization of question cognitive potential in creative problem solving.
question_originality_and_relevance_as_me.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

solution_originality_and_appropriateness.name: Solution originality and appropriateness as outcome measures of question-driven problem solving
solution_originality_and_appropriateness.description: Player-produced solutions (written and visual designs) are rated for originality and appropriateness, providing dependent measures that capture both creative novelty and practical quality of problem-solving outputs.
solution_originality_and_appropriateness.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

network_science_applications_across_expe.name: Network science applications across experimental psychology topics
network_science_applications_across_expe.description: This special issue introduction demonstrates how complex networks—with varied node types (people, words, brain regions) and connection types (friendships, semantic similarity, coactivation)—are applied across a wide range of psychological topics, yielding insights unavailable from conventional approaches.

complex_network_structure_influences_pro.name: Complex network structure influences processes operating within a system
complex_network_structure_influences_pro.description: The introduction asserts that the weblike structure emerging from nodes and connections shapes the processes that operate within that system, positioning network topology as causally relevant to psychological function.

varied_node_and_edge_definitions_as_a_me.name: Varied node and edge definitions as a methodological strength of network science in psychology
varied_node_and_edge_definitions_as_a_me.description: The introduction highlights that nodes can represent people, words, or brain regions, and edges can represent friendships, semantic similarity, or coactivation, illustrating the flexibility of the network science framework for experimental psychology.

network_science_metrics_reveal_informati.name: Network science metrics reveal information unavailable from conventional approaches
network_science_metrics_reveal_informati.description: The introduction explicitly claims that network metrics reveal information that could not be found using contemporary and conventional approaches in psychology.

special_issue_as_a_call_to_broaden_netwo.name: Special issue as a call to broaden network science adoption in experimental psychology
special_issue_as_a_call_to_broaden_netwo.description: The guest editors and authors aim to encourage other researchers to apply computational techniques from network science to their own questions of interest, framing the special issue as a motivating exemplar.

mental_navigation_as_a_common_mechanism_.name: Mental navigation as a common mechanism for intelligence, creativity, and openness
mental_navigation_as_a_common_mechanism_.description: The paper argues that mental navigation—operationalized via verbal fluency task performance modeled over a cognitive multiplex network—is a shared underlying mechanism linking individual differences in creativity, intelligence, and openness to experience.
mental_navigation_as_a_common_mechanism_.paper: A cognitive multiplex network approach to investigate mental navigation and predict high-level cognition

cognitive_multiplex_network_as_model_of_.name: Cognitive multiplex network as model of the mental lexicon for fluency task analysis
cognitive_multiplex_network_as_model_of_.description: Verbal fluency task responses (animal fluency and hot-synonym fluency) from 479 participants are modeled using a cognitive multiplex network representation of the mental lexicon, enabling quantification of mental navigation trajectories.
cognitive_multiplex_network_as_model_of_.paper: A cognitive multiplex network approach to investigate mental navigation and predict high-level cognition

verbal_fluency_tasks_as_operational_prox.name: Verbal fluency tasks as operational proxies for mental navigation
verbal_fluency_tasks_as_operational_prox.description: The study operationalizes mental navigation through two verbal fluency tasks—a 2-minute animal fluency task and a 2-minute generating synonyms of 'hot' task—rather than other cognitive paradigms, linking navigation patterns to network topology.
verbal_fluency_tasks_as_operational_prox.paper: A cognitive multiplex network approach to investigate mental navigation and predict high-level cognition

prediction_of_high_level_cognition_from_.name: Prediction of high-level cognition from mental navigation measures via regression
prediction_of_high_level_cognition_from_.description: Quantitative measures of mental navigation derived from multiplex network modeling of fluency task responses significantly predicted participants' creativity, intelligence, and openness to experience, replicating across both fluency tasks.
prediction_of_high_level_cognition_from_.paper: A cognitive multiplex network approach to investigate mental navigation and predict high-level cognition

high_level_cognitive_prediction_online_t.name: High-level Cognitive Prediction online tool
high_level_cognitive_prediction_online_t.description: The authors developed and made publicly available an online tool—the High-level Cognitive Prediction tool—that operationalizes their multiplex network approach to enable prediction of high-level cognition from verbal fluency data.
high_level_cognitive_prediction_online_t.paper: A cognitive multiplex network approach to investigate mental navigation and predict high-level cognition

replication_of_navigation_cognition_link.name: Replication of navigation-cognition links across two distinct fluency task types
replication_of_navigation_cognition_link.description: The predictive relationship between multiplex-network-derived mental navigation metrics and high-level cognition was replicated across both the animal fluency task and the hot-synonym fluency task, supporting robustness of the approach.
replication_of_navigation_cognition_link.paper: A cognitive multiplex network approach to investigate mental navigation and predict high-level cognition

converging_computational_methods_as_a_st.name: Converging computational methods as a strategy for studying high-level cognition complexity
converging_computational_methods_as_a_st.description: The paper demonstrates the use of multiple converging computational tools—multiplex network modeling, verbal fluency analysis, and regression modeling—as a unified strategy for elucidating complex high-level cognitive constructs.
converging_computational_methods_as_a_st.paper: A cognitive multiplex network approach to investigate mental navigation and predict high-level cognition

stable_emotion_co_occurrence_network_str.name: Stable emotion co-occurrence network structure across crises and normal periods
stable_emotion_co_occurrence_network_str.description: Analysis of large-scale Japanese social media data across earthquake events, COVID-19 vaccination periods, and non-crisis periods reveals that the rank order of emotion co-occurrence links remains highly stable at the population level despite contextual variation.
stable_emotion_co_occurrence_network_str.paper: Stable Emotional Co-occurrence Patterns Revealed by Network Analysis of Social Media

crisis_specific_strengthening_of_tension.name: Crisis-specific strengthening of Tension-associated emotion links
crisis_specific_strengthening_of_tension.description: During earthquake and pre-COVID-19-vaccination periods, emotion links associated with Tension are significantly strengthened in the co-occurrence network compared to non-crisis baselines, even though the overall rank structure remains intact.
crisis_specific_strengthening_of_tension.paper: Stable Emotional Co-occurrence Patterns Revealed by Network Analysis of Social Media

network_based_computational_framework_fo.name: Network-based computational framework for emotion co-occurrence dynamics in social media
network_based_computational_framework_fo.description: The study proposes a novel computational approach grounded in network theory that leverages large-scale social media text to identify and evaluate links between emotions through the co-occurrence of emotion-related words, enabling systematic and scalable analysis.
network_based_computational_framework_fo.paper: Stable Emotional Co-occurrence Patterns Revealed by Network Analysis of Social Media

challenge_to_context_based_assumption_of.name: Challenge to context-based assumption of emotion co-occurrence
challenge_to_context_based_assumption_of.description: The findings challenge the prevailing assumption that emotion co-occurrence patterns are primarily context-dependent, suggesting instead that emotions possess an intrinsic stable network structure at the population level.
challenge_to_context_based_assumption_of.paper: Stable Emotional Co-occurrence Patterns Revealed by Network Analysis of Social Media

japanese_social_media_corpus_spanning_cr.name: Japanese social media corpus spanning crises and non-crisis decade as empirical basis
japanese_social_media_corpus_spanning_cr.description: The study employs a large-scale Japanese social media dataset covering multiple crisis events (earthquakes and COVID-19 vaccination) and non-crisis periods across approximately ten years, providing the empirical foundation for population-level emotion network analysis.
japanese_social_media_corpus_spanning_cr.paper: Stable Emotional Co-occurrence Patterns Revealed by Network Analysis of Social Media

emotion_co_occurrence_operationalized_vi.name: Emotion co-occurrence operationalized via word co-occurrence in social media text
emotion_co_occurrence_operationalized_vi.description: Emotion links in the network are constructed by measuring the co-occurrence of emotion-related concept words in social media posts, providing a proxy for how emotions co-occur in public discourse.
emotion_co_occurrence_operationalized_vi.paper: Stable Emotional Co-occurrence Patterns Revealed by Network Analysis of Social Media

emotion_network_link_rank_as_stable_popu.name: Emotion network link rank as stable population-level psychological index
emotion_network_link_rank_as_stable_popu.description: The paper argues that the rank ordering of emotion co-occurrence links constitutes a stable psychological index at the population level that is robust to both crisis type and temporal change.
emotion_network_link_rank_as_stable_popu.paper: Stable Emotional Co-occurrence Patterns Revealed by Network Analysis of Social Media

scibert_enhanced_dsi_for_scientific_text.name: SciBERT-enhanced DSI for scientific text originality
scibert_enhanced_dsi_for_scientific_text.description: The paper advances the original BERT-based DSI method by substituting SciBERT (a model trained on scientific corpora) to better capture conceptual originality in scientific abstracts and titles.
scibert_enhanced_dsi_for_scientific_text.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

dsi_predicts_5_year_citation_counts_in_s.name: DSI predicts 5-year citation counts in scientific abstracts
dsi_predicts_5_year_citation_counts_in_s.description: Modelling SciBERT-DSI and BERT-DSI as predictors of log 5-year citation counts alongside field, publication year, and log author count yields statistically significant relationships with adjusted R-squared of approximately 0.103 and 0.101 respectively.
dsi_predicts_5_year_citation_counts_in_s.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

dsi_role_in_early_citation_accrual_is_st.name: DSI role in early citation accrual is stronger for fewer-author papers
dsi_role_in_early_citation_accrual_is_st.description: The study observes that DSI plays a more pronounced role in the accrual of early citations specifically for papers with fewer authors, suggesting an interaction between author count and originality impact.
dsi_role_in_early_citation_accrual_is_st.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

dsi_varies_substantially_across_scientif.name: DSI varies substantially across scientific subjects and fields
dsi_varies_substantially_across_scientif.description: DSI scores differ significantly across subjects and research fields in the 51,200-abstract corpus, indicating that conceptual originality as measured by DSI is domain-dependent.
dsi_varies_substantially_across_scientif.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

declining_correlation_between_dsi_and_ci.name: Declining correlation between DSI and citation counts over time
declining_correlation_between_dsi_and_ci.description: The correlation between DSI and citation counts decreases over publication time, suggesting that the relationship between conceptual originality and academic recognition changes across historical periods.
declining_correlation_between_dsi_and_ci.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

dsi_as_direct_textual_measure_of_scienti.name: DSI as direct textual measure of scientific originality in scientometrics
dsi_as_direct_textual_measure_of_scienti.description: Because existing scientometric measures rarely assess originality in textual content, the paper positions DSI as a valuable tool for directly quantifying conceptual originality embedded in scientific writing at scale.
dsi_as_direct_textual_measure_of_scienti.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

large_multi_field_scientific_abstract_co.name: Large multi-field scientific abstract corpus as empirical basis
large_multi_field_scientific_abstract_co.description: The study uses 51,200 scientific abstracts and titles sourced from the Web of Science, spanning multiple subjects and fields, as the empirical basis for DSI computation and citation modelling.
large_multi_field_scientific_abstract_co.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

scibert_vs_bert_comparison_for_dsi_compu.name: SciBERT vs BERT comparison for DSI computation on scientific texts
scibert_vs_bert_comparison_for_dsi_compu.description: The paper implicitly raises the question of whether domain-trained language models (SciBERT) outperform general models (BERT) for measuring originality in scientific texts via DSI.
scibert_vs_bert_comparison_for_dsi_compu.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

associative_thought_enhances_scene_gist_.name: Associative thought enhances scene-gist memory but impairs scene-detail memory
associative_thought_enhances_scene_gist_.description: In an online experiment (N=75), participants who generated chained free associations after viewing scenes showed better gist memory but worse detail memory compared to participants who performed phonological processing, suggesting associative thought promotes contextual encoding at the expense of fine-grained detail.
associative_thought_enhances_scene_gist_.paper: Spontaneous associative thought may facilitate scene-gist memory via implicit scene-labeling

scene_labeling_mediates_the_effect_of_as.name: Scene labeling mediates the effect of associative thought on scene-gist memory
scene_labeling_mediates_the_effect_of_as.description: In a follow-up experiment (N=74), explicit scene labeling before the processing task eliminated memory differences between associative and phonological conditions, indicating that implicit labeling during free association is the mechanism through which associative thought prioritizes gist encoding.
scene_labeling_mediates_the_effect_of_as.paper: Spontaneous associative thought may facilitate scene-gist memory via implicit scene-labeling

creativity_correlates_with_semantic_dist.name: Creativity correlates with semantic distance of scene-based associations but not with scene memory
creativity_correlates_with_semantic_dist.description: Across both experiments, individual creativity scores were not correlated with scene-gist or scene-detail memory, but were positively correlated with the semantic distances between free associations generated from scenes, extending the link between creativity and breadth of associative processes to a scene-memory paradigm.
creativity_correlates_with_semantic_dist.paper: Spontaneous associative thought may facilitate scene-gist memory via implicit scene-labeling

post_perceptual_associative_processes_in.name: Post-perceptual associative processes influence higher-order cognitive functions including memory consolidation
post_perceptual_associative_processes_in.description: The study argues that spontaneous associative thought occurring after perception affects how scenes are encoded and consolidated in memory, implicating post-perceptual associative processes as a mechanism linking scene perception, memory consolidation, and creative thought.
post_perceptual_associative_processes_in.paper: Spontaneous associative thought may facilitate scene-gist memory via implicit scene-labeling

chained_free_association_as_experimental.name: Chained free association as experimental paradigm for post-perceptual associative processing
chained_free_association_as_experimental.description: Participants generated chained free associations after each scene viewing trial as an operationalization of spontaneous associative thought; this is compared to a phonological processing control (listing words beginning with a specific letter) to isolate associative processing effects on memory.
chained_free_association_as_experimental.paper: Spontaneous associative thought may facilitate scene-gist memory via implicit scene-labeling

semantic_distance_between_associations_d.name: Semantic distance between associations does not moderate associative thought memory effect
semantic_distance_between_associations_d.description: The enhancement of scene-gist memory by associative thought was observed regardless of the conceptual (semantic) distances between association responses, suggesting the memory benefit is driven by associative processing per se rather than by how semantically remote the associations are.
semantic_distance_between_associations_d.paper: Spontaneous associative thought may facilitate scene-gist memory via implicit scene-labeling

implicit_scene_labeling_as_mechanism_lin.name: Implicit scene labeling as mechanism linking associative thought to scene-gist memory
implicit_scene_labeling_as_mechanism_lin.description: The authors propose that when participants perform free association after scene viewing, they implicitly label the scene's gist as part of generating contextually relevant associations, and this labeling process—not the associative chain itself—is responsible for enhanced gist memory.
implicit_scene_labeling_as_mechanism_lin.paper: Spontaneous associative thought may facilitate scene-gist memory via implicit scene-labeling

jabberwocky_context_as_non_supportive_ba.name: jabberwocky_context_as_non_supportive_baseline_for_metaphor_comprehension
jabberwocky_context_as_non_supportive_ba.description: The study uses jabberwocky sentences (matched nonsense contexts) as a non-supportive baseline to isolate the effect of meaningful narrative context on metaphor processing, controlling for syntactic structure while removing semantic content.
jabberwocky_context_as_non_supportive_ba.paper: Context matters: Novel metaphors in supportive and non-supportive contexts (Preprint)
